# Test Issues Archive

This archive stores historical QA status logs plus FIXED and WONTFIX issue records moved out of `TEST_ISSUES.md`. The active issue list now keeps only unresolved OPEN issues.

## Archived Status Log

- 2026-06-06 05:55 UTC: **Runtime login unblock, backend/schema deploy, hourly release fix, Android APK 1.0.35 publish, no commit.** Restored APP/web login after confirming the production/QA egress IP `129.146.180.88` had been repeatedly blocked by login-failure protection; all matching live `ip_blacklist_entries` rows are now `RELEASED`, active LOGIN blocks are `0`, and Redis `login:ip:129.146.180.88` no longer exists. Added `SECURITY_IP_BLACKLIST_TRUSTED_IPS` to live env and deploy examples, and updated `TokenBlacklistService` so trusted CI/QA IPs do not increment Redis login-failure counters. Deployed the rebuilt backend after adding the missing production `reviews.image_urls` schema column and source migration `V5__review_image_urls.sql`; backend and Nginx are active and `/api/app/config` is healthy. Fixed the hourly release path by selecting Node >=20 for Capacitor and removing Node-12-incompatible optional chaining in the mobile APK builder. Ran the full release activation successfully and published release-signed `shoptest-1.0.35.apk` / versionCode `10035`: size `3122023`, SHA256 `a7753de4e5d535230025ee502ed041223307e5cc950dcc1b616b9cb21769373a`, certificate SHA-256 `9962289890D74A1FE9DA3E4D6471D2C00B21C76FC9C0622FB93348CF825D880A`. Public manifest `https://pet.686888666.xyz/downloads/mobile-version.json` reports `1.0.35`; bad-password login now returns backend `400` at `/auth/login` instead of blacklist `403`; versioned APK `https://pet.686888666.xyz/downloads/shoptest-1.0.35.apk` returns 200 with the expected size. Local Nginx APK responses have `no-store`; Cloudflare may still cache the stable `shoptest.apk`, so QA should use the manifest/versioned URL or `shoptest.apk?v=1.0.35` for install/update checks. Device/emulator login validation remains pending.

- 2026-06-06 04:52 UTC: **Source-only frontend build-warning cleanup, no tests, no build, no APK, no commit.** Cleaned up the warnings emitted by the 1.0.34 build without rerunning the build: `Checkout.tsx` available-coupons `useMemo` now depends on the full `couponQuote` object instead of only `couponQuote?.availableCoupons`, `Home.tsx` includes `t` in the home data load effect dependencies, and `Profile.tsx` removes unsupported `aria-pressed` from mobile controls with `role="tab"` while preserving `aria-selected` and roving `tabIndex`. `git diff --check` passed for the touched files. Next permitted frontend/APK build should verify that the three warning classes no longer appear.

- 2026-06-06 04:25 UTC: **Source-only auth/session navbar cleanup, no tests, no build, no APK, no commit.** Archived **F1196/F1197/F1210** as current-source-covered because current `Navbar.tsx` reads only normalized session keys through `safeStorage` (`token`, `username`, `role`, counters) and no longer parses a raw `localStorage.user` object in render. Source-fixed **F1198** and aligned storefront logout with AdminLayout by logging logout revoke errors, showing the localized partial-logout warning, clearing the local auth session, and navigating with `buildLoginUrlFromWindow()` plus `replace`. Archived **F1064** as current-source-covered because `AdminLayout` already uses the same safe login redirect on auth failure and logout. Marked **F1212** stale/current-source-covered because current `OrderTracking.tsx` has no `fetchOrders` path; public order tracking uses anonymous `/orders/track` requests, while signed-in order operations still go through the shared auth interceptor. Regression requested for Navbar logout partial-failure warning, post-login return URL after logout, legacy `localStorage.user` corruption, and order-tracking signed-in action expiry. Static checks only; no Jest/Maven/Playwright/build/runtime reload was run.

- 2026-06-06 04:15 UTC: **Runtime APP/WebView login outage fixed and archived.** Root cause was the live static Nginx config using `proxy_pass $shop_backend_origin/` inside `location /api/`; with a variable plus URI, `/api/app/config` and `/api/auth/login` were forwarded to backend `/`, causing public `/api/app/config` to return 401 and APP/WebView password login to fail before the real `/auth/login` controller path. Updated live `/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/shoptest` and repository `deploy/nginx/shoptest-static.conf` to rewrite `/api/(.*)` to `/$1` before proxying, and to pass `/ws/` plus `/uploads/` through with their original URI. Added `https://127.0.0.1/api/app/config` with `Host: pet.686888666.xyz` to `scripts/restart-shoptest-runtime.sh` health checks. Verification: `nginx -t` passed, `systemctl reload nginx` completed, public `/api/app/config` returned 200 through both local Nginx and Cloudflare, and a bad-password `POST /api/auth/login` returned backend 400 with response path `/auth/login`. No real user password was tested, no APK build was required, and no Maven/Jest/Playwright suite was run.

- 2026-06-06 04:05 UTC: **Source-only F1427 product import category/brand lookup archive sync, no tests, no build, no APK, no commit.** Archived **F1427** as current-source-covered because current CSV import is in `ProductServiceImpl.processCsvImport(...)` and already preloads category data once via `loadImportCategoryLookup()` before the row loop. Category id/name/path validation uses that in-memory lookup per row, brand import is a plain product string field assignment, and current import code has no `BrandRepository` dependency or per-row brand query. `ProductUrlImportService` has no category/brand DB lookup loop. This closes only the reported category/brand import lookup N+1. Product id update lookups, existing variant SKU owner scans, duplicate-name validation, import row limits, and broad unbounded `findAll()` cleanup remain separate concerns. Regression required for CSV categoryId/categoryName/path/ambiguous/missing-category and brand-value imports. No Maven/Jest/build/APK/Playwright/curl/service restart was performed.

- 2026-06-06 03:51 UTC: **Source-only F1426 admin user summary aggregation archive sync, no tests, no build, no APK, no commit.** Archived **F1426** as current-source-covered because the reported `AdminUserService.getUserStats` path is absent in current source. Current `/admin/users/summary` calls `UserService.adminSummary(...)`, which delegates to one `UserMapper.adminSummary` SQL statement using conditional aggregates for total, active, banned, admin, customer, missing email, missing phone, and ready users under the same keyword/role/status filters. This closes only admin user summary count fan-out. User list pagination/export limits, role mutation, and broader user-table indexes remain separate concerns. Regression required for summary counts, health score/admin ratio, and filter coherence with mixed user fixtures. No Maven/Jest/build/APK/Playwright/curl/service restart was performed.

- 2026-06-06 03:38 UTC: **Source-only F1425 public review list N+1 archive sync, no tests, no build, no APK, no commit.** Source-fixed **F1425** in the current `ReviewService.getPublicReviewsByProductId(...)` path by changing `ReviewRepository.findApprovedPublicByProductId(...)` and `findPublicByProductIdIncludingUserPending(...)` to join-fetch both `Review.product` and `Review.user` before DTO mapping. Public review reads remain bounded by `review.public-max-rows`; `PublicReviewResponse.from(...)` can now read product id, masked username, and editability without per-review lazy product/user loads. Current review images live in the `reviews.image_urls` column, so no separate review-image load path exists for this source issue. Regression required for anonymous/logged-in product review lists, current-user pending reviews, review images/admin replies, inactive products, ordering, limits, average rating, and query logs if available. No Maven/Jest/build/APK/Playwright/curl/service restart was performed.

- 2026-06-03 20:53 UTC: **Checkout coupon opportunity regression F759, no commit.** Rechecked the current `TEST_ISSUES.md` Regression #208 failures. The backend `SupportWebSocketHandlerAdminPayloadTest.class` permission failure was not reproducible: targeted Maven passed 2 tests. The frontend `Checkout.test.tsx:223` timeout was real: the checkout data-loading effect re-ran after payment-channel state changed, causing a second cart/address load and a later coupon quote to overwrite the coupon-opportunity state. Fixed and archived **F759** by keeping checkout cart/address loading independent from payment-channel state; the existing payment-channel effect remains responsible for restoring the preferred payment method. - 2026-06-03 17:32 UTC: **Development/backend API contract F741 customer payment refund-reference leak, no commit.** Delegated API/backend tester Linnaeus found and the parent fixed **F741**: `PaymentResponse` contained `refundReference`, and customer/guest payment endpoints in `PaymentController` reused it for `/payments/order/{orderId}`, `/payments/guest/order/{orderId}`, latest-payment reads, payment creation, and payment sync. A refunded payment could therefore expose gateway/manual refund references to customers or guests even though the frontend `PaymentCustomer` type intentionally does not model that admin-only field. Added `PaymentCustomerResponse` without `refundReference`, refactored `PaymentResponse` to extend it for admin responses, and changed customer/guest PaymentController paths to return the customer DTO while keeping admin payment, simulation, legacy admin payment confirmation, and refund responses on `PaymentResponse`. - 2026-06-03 16:30 UTC: **Development/frontend regression F740 rich-detail mapped-private media URL hardening, no commit.** Found and fixed **F740** while continuing the F738 media-contract audit: product rich-detail preview kept a separate host-safety implementation from the shared persistent media resolver and did not reject IPv4-mapped IPv6 private/loopback hosts such as `https://[::ffff:192.168.1.10]/image.jpg` or `https://[::ffff:7f00:1]/image.jpg`. The frontend API normalizer already rejects those through `normalizePersistentImageUrl`, so an operator could preview a mapped-private rich media URL that would then be dropped before save. `ProductRichDetail.isHttpMediaUrl` now reuses `normalizePersistentImageUrl` directly, aligning preview, ProductManagement filtering, API submit normalization, and persistent display policy for `/uploads/...`, legacy `uploads/...`, safe HTTP(S), unsafe hosts, credentials, ports, encoded backslashes/NULs, protocol-relative URLs, and non-upload root-relative paths. - 2026-06-03 16:13 UTC: **Development/frontend regression F738 rich-detail root-relative media preview hardening, no commit.** Found and fixed **F738** after the F736/F735 media-policy hardening: product rich-detail preview still accepted arbitrary root-relative media URLs such as `/assets/products/preview-only.jpg`, while the frontend API normalizer and backend product save/import validator only preserve uploaded paths under `/uploads/...` or safe HTTP(S) URLs. A merchant could therefore preview an image/video in the product rich-detail editor that would be silently dropped before save or rejected by backend direct-save/import paths. The rich-detail media helper now accepts root-relative paths only when they are under `/uploads/...`, continues to normalize legacy `uploads/...` paths to `/uploads/...`, and still rejects unsafe schemes, obfuscated control/backslash payloads, localhost/private hosts, credentials, and non-standard ports. Added focused frontend regression coverage for `/assets/...` preview rejection and API detail-content normalization. - 2026-06-03 13:45 UTC: **Development/frontend regression F734 product rich-media URL contract hardening, no commit.** Continued the commercial-readiness UI/API contract audit after F733. Found and fixed **F734** in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx` and `frontend/src/api/index.ts`: the product rich-detail editor/preview helper treated bare relative media URLs such as `uploads/products/detail.jpg` as valid by resolving them against the current browser page, while the admin API normalizer and backend product save/import contract only accept root-relative `/...` or absolute `http(s)://...` media URLs. This could make an operator see a rich image/video preview that is silently dropped before save, or submit localhost/private/non-standard-port media URLs that the backend then rejects. Rich media URL validation now matches the backend-facing contract more closely: root-relative URLs are allowed, bare relative URLs are rejected, absolute HTTP(S) URLs must not include credentials, localhost/private/link-local/multicast/internal hosts are rejected, and non-standard web ports are rejected. Product management's main image/gallery/detail media filters inherit the stricter helper, and API payload normalization now drops out-of-contract detail media before sending. Added focused regression coverage in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` and `frontend/src/api/index.test.ts`. Verification: `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` passed with 7 tests; `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/api/index.test.ts` passed with 51 tests; full `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 226 tests and existing React act/Router future warnings only; `npm run build` passed with the existing Browserslist stale-data warning and generated `main.52f0e7d6.js`; `git diff --check -- frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx frontend/src/api/index.ts frontend/src/api/index.test.ts TEST_ISSUES.md frontend/public/downloads/mobile-version.json frontend/build/downloads/mobile-version.json` passed; public/build/live downloads still contain no APK/AAB files, and public/build mobile manifests remain unsigned with empty APK URLs. - 2026-06-03 13:22 UTC: **Development/backend regression F733 notification broadcast contract hardening, no commit.** Continued the notification HTML/server contract audit after F732. Found and fixed **F733** in `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationService.java`: backend HTML notification sanitization rejected safe path-relative links such as `coupons/spring` and `./profile?tab=orders` even though the admin preview/client sanitizer kept them, so an operator could preview a clickable internal campaign/order link that was silently stripped before customer delivery. Tester-agent review also found the backend start-tag regex missed browser-accepted slash-before-attribute markup such as `<a/href=...>` and `<img/src=... onerror=...>`, leaving those attributes outside the sanitizer rebuild path. The same service path also relied on the frontend and database to bound notification title/message sizes, so direct admin API calls could reach customer lookup/batch insert with a title longer than the `notifications.title VARCHAR(100)` contract or a message longer than the admin composer's 5000-character contract. The backend now trims and validates title/message length before batching, preserves safe relative URLs, sanitizes slash-separated attributes, still rejects unsafe schemes, protocol-relative URLs, credentials, control characters, backslashes, encoded NUL/backslash payloads, and keeps `_blank` rel protection. Added focused regression coverage in `src/test/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationServiceTest.java`. Verification: `./mvnw -Dtest=NotificationServiceTest test` passed with 10 tests; full `./mvnw test` passed with 414 tests; frontend `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/utils/sanitizeHtml.test.ts` passed with 6 tests; `git diff --check -- src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationService.java src/test/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationServiceTest.java` passed; public/build/live downloads still contain no APK/AAB files. - 2026-06-03 13:03 UTC: **Development/static+build F732 rich-media preview iframe hardening, no commit.** Continued the commercial-readiness source audit after F731. Found and fixed **F732** in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetailEditor.tsx`: the admin product rich-detail editor's video preview iframe did not apply the same iframe containment used by the storefront rich-detail renderer, so an embedded YouTube/Vimeo preview in the admin editor lacked explicit sandboxing, referrer policy, and feature allowlist. The preview iframe now uses `sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-presentation"`, `referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin"`, and `allow="fullscreen; picture-in-picture"`, matching the storefront renderer's safer embedding contract. Verification: a structured iframe scan over `frontend/src` found zero iframe tags missing `sandbox` or `referrerPolicy`; `git diff --check -- frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetailEditor.tsx frontend/public/downloads/mobile-version.json TEST_ISSUES.md` passed; `npm run build` passed with the existing Browserslist stale-data warning only and generated `main.fa4e7972.js`. The build regenerated unsigned mobile manifest/build assets but generated no public APK/AAB, and public/build/live downloads still contain no APK/AAB files. No live static deployment, service restart, APK generation/publish, Maven, Jest, Playwright, screenshot/video/trace, commit, or destructive git operation was performed. - 2026-06-03 12:43 UTC: **Development/regression F730 late-provider-payment reconciliation hardening, no commit.** Fixed **F730** by making provider-reported successful payment after local cancellation/expiry/failure non-fulfilling and explicitly auditable: generic callbacks, Stripe webhooks, and Stripe sync now move those payment rows to `RECONCILE_REQUIRED` instead of marking the order ready to ship, and an additional pending-payment/order-already-cancelled race fallback also records `RECONCILE_REQUIRED` rather than leaving the external receipt as ordinary pending. `PaymentResponse` keeps transaction evidence visible for `RECONCILE_REQUIRED`, Profile's Stripe success return now opens the orders tab, syncs order payments, refreshes orders, displays paid/pending/reconcile messages, and removes the return URL parameters, and admin order management now labels the review state and allows refund submission for pending-payment/cancelled orders only when reconcile-required payment evidence exists. Refund handling can claim and refund the latest `PAID` or `RECONCILE_REQUIRED` payment, restores `RECONCILE_REQUIRED` if an external refund attempt fails after claim, and keeps cancelled/pending-payment reconciliation refunds behind stored gateway/manual reference evidence. Verification: `./mvnw -Dtest=PaymentFlowServiceTest test` passed with 41 tests, full `./mvnw test` passed with 410 tests, prior F730 frontend `npm run build` passed, prior F730 full `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 224 tests, touched-file `git diff --check` passed, and locale JSON parse passed. Parallel tester-agent review was requested for the backend and frontend F730 diffs, but no tester-agent patch was integrated in this local batch. No live static deployment, APK generation/publish, service restart, commit, or destructive git operation was performed. **F334 remains OPEN** until a release keystore/certificate and release-signed APK are provided. Current totals: 724 issues, 698 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 22 OPEN.

- 2026-06-03 10:52 UTC: **Development/regression F729 payment/order lock-order hardening, no commit.** Fixed **F729** by unifying successful payment and payment-expiry cancellation write ordering around the order row first. `PaymentService.simulatePaid`, generic `handleCallback`, Stripe webhook completion, and Stripe sync now validate the order and CAS the order to `PENDING_SHIPMENT` before writing `payments` as `PAID`; if the order claim fails, the payment row is not marked paid. `OrderService.confirmPayment` now also CAS-claims `PENDING_PAYMENT -> PENDING_SHIPMENT` before updating or inserting the manual paid payment record, and rolls back if the payment write fails. The expired-payment path no longer writes `payments` before cancelling the order when the expired payment is the last active pending payment: it now claims/cancels the order first, then marks the current payment `EXPIRED`, then closes any remaining pending payment rows, removing the remaining `payments -> orders` reverse lock order found by tester-agent review. Added focused regression coverage for callback/simulate/simulate-callback/manual-confirm success ordering, CAS-failure no-write behavior, real `OrderService.updateStatusIfCurrent` ordering before `markPaidDetailed`, manual-confirm insert ordering, and last-pending-payment expiry ordering. Opened **F730** for the separate payment reconciliation/product-flow risk where a provider may report success after a local pending-payment cancellation, and where Stripe success return to Profile may still require explicit user-visible sync/reconciliation behavior when webhooks are delayed; this is not the F729 lock-order bug and needs a dedicated product-safe handling decision. Verification: `./mvnw -Dtest=PaymentFlowServiceTest test` passed with 35 tests, targeted backend `./mvnw -Dtest=PaymentFlowServiceTest,PaymentControllerSimulationAccessTest,AdminControllerPaymentResponseTest,OrderControllerLegacyAdminAuditTest,OrderServiceDashboardStatusesTest test` passed with 47 tests, full `./mvnw test` passed with 403 tests, and touched-file `git diff --check` passed. No frontend source, frontend build/Jest, Playwright, live static deployment, APK artifact, service restart, commit, or destructive git operation was performed in this backend-only batch. **F334 remains OPEN** until a release keystore/certificate and release-signed APK are provided. Current totals: 724 issues, 697 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 23 OPEN.

- 2026-06-02 23:39 UTC: **Development/static F653 native button type hardening, no tests, no commit.** Continued the UI/design and form-safety static pass. Found many native `<button>` elements across storefront, support, checkout, profile, admin dashboard, audit, and product/admin operation surfaces that did not explicitly declare `type`. In HTML, those buttons default to `submit` when rendered inside or later moved into form contexts, which can cause accidental form submission during UI refactors or modal/form composition. Fixed **F653** by adding `type="button"` to all native non-submit buttons under `frontend/src` while preserving existing click handlers, labels, disabled states, and layout. Static verification only: a multiline PCRE scan found zero remaining native `<button>` tags without a `type` attribute under `frontend/src`, a duplicate-type scan found zero duplicates, and `git diff --check` passed for the touched button files. - 2026-06-01 22:49 UTC: **Development/static Android App F561 release APK certificate verification hardening, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F561** by making `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-android-apk.js` inspect the finished release APK signing certificate with Android SDK `apksigner --print-certs`, falling back to `keytool -printcert -jarfile`, before staging or publishing. Updated mobile README release-signing guidance. Static checks: `node --check scripts/build-android-apk.js`, targeted signing/publish/wildcard/brand/cleartext scans, and trailing-whitespace scans passed; no Gradle/Jest/Maven/build/UI smoke or APK artifact inspection was run per instruction. **F334 remains OPEN** until a real release-signed APK is produced. - 2026-06-01 02:15 UTC: **Development/static F266 user-address concurrency fix, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F266** by serializing user-address writes through the existing database user-row lock. Address add/update/delete/default-switch operations now lock the owner with `UserMapper.findByIdForUpdate(...)` before address count reads, default clears, inserts, deletes, or default assignments; default switching and default deletion re-read address state after the lock before applying changes. Added focused service coverage for locking before add limit reads and before default clear/set operations. Static checks only; no Maven/Jest/build run was performed per instruction. Current totals after F265/F266/F267/F277/F276 source fixes: 558 issues, 509 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 45 OPEN (pending QA retest).

- 2026-06-01 02:11 UTC: **Development/static F265 wishlist mutation validation fix, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F265** by hardening `WishlistService` for both `/wishlist` and `/favorites` mutation paths: positive user/product id validation is enforced, new wishlist inserts first verify the product exists and is public/active, concurrent duplicate inserts are treated as idempotent success, and remaining insert integrity failures are converted into controlled bad-request exceptions instead of surfacing database constraint errors. Added focused service coverage for missing product, inactive product, duplicate race, and already-wishlisted no-op behavior. Static checks only; no Maven/Jest/build run was performed per instruction. Current totals after F265/F267/F277/F276 source fixes: 558 issues, 508 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 46 OPEN (pending QA retest).

- 2026-06-01 02:06 UTC: **Development/static F267 coupon grant recipient validation fix, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F267** by adding a batch user-existence lookup for admin coupon grants and rejecting unknown recipient ids before any coupon claimed-quantity increment or `user_coupons` insert occurs. `AdminController.grantCoupon(...)` already catches the resulting `IllegalArgumentException`, so these failures now use the controlled `COUPON_GRANT` audit failure path and 400 response instead of leaking database foreign key errors. Added focused service coverage for the no-increment/no-insert invariant. Static checks only; no Maven/Jest/build run was performed per instruction. Current totals after F267/F277/F276 source fixes: 558 issues, 507 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 47 OPEN (pending QA retest).

Also recorded the completed **F277/F276** WebSocket hardening: support sockets now prune closed sessions, enforce configurable global and per-user connection limits before registration, close excess sockets with WebSocket code 1013, apply per-session text/binary message limits, and configure the servlet WebSocket container idle timeout and buffer sizes from runtime config. Static checks only; no Maven/Jest/build run was performed per instruction. Current totals after F277/F276 source fix: 558 issues, 506 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 48 OPEN (pending QA retest).

- 2026-06-01 01:24 UTC: **Development/static F279 pet-gallery upload sanitization fix, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F279** by changing pet-gallery uploads from raw `Files.copy(...)` persistence to decode-and-reencode storage before metadata is saved. Uploaded JPG files are rewritten as clean JPG, PNG/GIF inputs are rewritten as clean PNG, stored `contentType` and `fileSize` now reflect the sanitized bytes, and oversized sanitized output is rejected. The upload allowlist was tightened to JPG/PNG/GIF in the backend, Home upload entry, Pet Gallery upload page, and English/Spanish/Chinese invalid-type copy so users are not offered formats that cannot be safely re-encoded by the current server image pipeline. Static checks only; no Maven/Jest/build run was performed per instruction. Current totals after F279 source fix: 558 issues, 504 FIXED, 4 WONTFIX, 50 OPEN (pending QA retest).

Product list now invalidates its category request/cache, clears wishlist and personalized recommendation state, and re-runs relevant loaders after auth transitions. Product detail now invalidates its recommendation cache, clears wishlist/reviewable-order/recommendation state, resets deferred content loading, and re-runs its product/session-aware loader after auth transitions. - 2026-05-31 21:54 UTC: **Development/static admin product pagination fix, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F175** by changing `/admin/products` to always return `ProductAdminPageResponse` with `items`, `total`, `page`, `size`, `totalPages`, `hasNext`, and `hasPrevious`; the default admin page is now `page=0`, `size=50`, sorted by `updatedAt,desc`. `ProductService.findAdminProductPage(...)` now queries through a JPA `Specification` and `PageRequest` for keyword/category-descendant/status/featured/discount/price filters instead of loading the whole products table before slicing, and legacy `findAdminProducts(...)` delegates to the paged path. `adminApi.getProducts(...)` now normalizes both the new page response and legacy arrays, and `ProductManagement.tsx` sends server `keyword`, `categoryId`, `status`, `page`, `size`, and sort params with controlled Ant Table pagination using the backend `total`. Static `rg` found no remaining touched-path references to `ResponseEntity<List<Product>> getProducts`, `productService.findAll()` from the admin products endpoint, `api.get<Product[]>('/admin/products')`, or `setProducts(response.data)` in Product Management. - 2026-05-31 21:20 UTC: **Development/static product URL import DNS rebinding guard, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F167** by adding DNS pin snapshots around every product URL import outbound fetch. `normalizeAndValidateUri(...)` still blocks non-http(s), credentials, non-standard ports, and private/local/multicast addresses; now `fetchHtml(...)` and `sendTopmRequest(...)` also resolve and validate the public address set immediately before sending, then re-resolve after the response and reject the import if the host's address set changed or re-resolves to a blocked address. This covers the main page fetch, redirects, nested `goods_url` fallback fetches, and TOPM dynamic POST/GET fallback endpoints. - 2026-05-31 21:17 UTC: **Development/static infrastructure default cleanup, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F166** by removing the hardcoded `158.101.11.223` infrastructure IP from runtime defaults, deploy templates, and deployment docs. `application.properties`, `application.yml`, and the gateway config now default Nacos/Redis to local-only values; deploy env examples and compose files now require explicit backend/gateway origins or use neutral internal placeholders; the static Nginx default points to localhost; and docs use `db.internal.example`, `redis.internal.example`, `nacos.internal.example`, and `gateway.internal.example` placeholders. - 2026-05-31 18:37 UTC: **Development/static F339 Nginx security-header inheritance fix, no builds, no automated tests, no screenshots, no commit.** Fixed **F339** in `deploy/nginx/shoptest-static.conf` and `deploy/nginx/shoptest-edge.conf.template` by repeating the full security header set in every HTTPS child location that declares `add_header Cache-Control`: `/healthz`, SPA not-found fallback, `/static/`, denied `*.map`, `/runtime-config.js`, generic static assets, and `/uploads/`. HTTP `/healthz` also now returns the core non-HSTS security headers alongside no-store. This avoids Nginx `add_header` inheritance loss without adding a separate include file that the current single-file static deploy and template-mounted edge container would not automatically install. Static checks only: `git diff --check -- deploy/nginx/shoptest-static.conf deploy/nginx/shoptest-edge.conf.template` passed, and a text scan found no `Cache-Control` child block missing the expected security headers. No screenshot, video, trace, or image capture was produced.

- 2026-05-31 13:38 UTC: **Development/static sensitive-auth rate-limit fix, no builds, no automated tests, no commit.** Fixed **F284**, **F282**, and **F281** by updating `RateLimitService` so sensitive auth requests consume layered buckets: a shared `auth:sensitive` per-client bucket across login/register/email-code/email-login/forgot-password/password-reset-code/refresh/admin-bootstrap routes, endpoint-specific limits for register/password-reset/email-login/refresh/admin-bootstrap, and the existing global scope bucket. - 2026-05-30 20:05 UTC: **Generic refund response validation review found F297.** Continued static code review across refund provider flows. Found **F297 (HIGH)**: `RefundService.refundGenericApiPayment()` sends amount/currency to the gateway but `parseRefundReference()` accepts a successful response with only status plus refund reference, ignoring or not requiring returned refunded amount/currency. The backend can therefore mark a payment/order fully refunded after a partial or wrong-currency refund response. Current totals: 447 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 142 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 20:00 UTC: **Homepage and search review found F294-F296.** Continued static code review across homepage, search functionality, and product comparison. Found 3 new issues:
  - **F294 (HIGH)**: SearchBar fires on every keystroke with no debounce—excessive API calls and poor UX when typing quickly.
  - **F295 (MEDIUM)**: SearchController has no server-side pagination size limit—`size=999999` can retrieve entire catalog causing OOM.
  - **F296 (MEDIUM)**: ProductList client-side-only filtering for materials/colors—never sent to server, causes performance issues with large catalogs.
  Current totals: 446 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 141 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 19:45 UTC: **Stripe webhook payment validation review found F293.** Continued static code review across payment callback and Stripe checkout flows. Found **F293 (HIGH)**: `PaymentService.handleStripeWebhook()` processes signed `checkout.session.completed` events by marking the local payment PAID and moving the order to `PENDING_SHIPMENT` without validating Stripe `payment_status`, `amount_total`, or `currency` against the local payment/channel. This is separate from F224, which covers the manual provider-sync path. Current totals: 443 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 138 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 19:20 UTC: **Order tracking and SLA review found F289-F292.** Continued static code review across order lifecycle, SLA management, and order tracking. Found 4 new issues:
  - **F289 (HIGH)**: SLA threshold mismatch between frontend (72h) and SQL (24h) for RETURN_SHIPPED—dashboard and per-order SLA tags show contradictory information.
  - **F290 (HIGH)**: Missing RETURN_REQUESTED in SQL SLA overdue filters—return requests pending >24h won't appear in SLA overdue summary.
  - **F291 (HIGH)**: Race condition in `updateOrderStatus` without optimistic locking—blind UPDATE without current-status check allows invalid transitions under concurrent load.
  - **F292 (MEDIUM)**: Frontend re-sorts server-paginated results with different priority—pagination boundaries inconsistent with displayed sort order.
  Current totals: 442 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 137 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 19:05 UTC: **Coupon and return flow deep dive found F287-F288.** Continued static code review across coupon management and order return/refund flows. Found 2 new issues:
  - **F287 (CRITICAL)**: Coupon claiming from CouponCenter page completely broken—`CouponCenter.tsx` hardcodes `userId: 0` in both batch and individual claim calls, causing authorization failure for all authenticated users.

- 2026-05-30 18:55 UTC: **Email-code storage review found F286.** Continued static code review across the email verification-code service after F285. Found 1 new issue:
  - **F286 (HIGH)**: `EmailLoginService` silently falls back from Redis to JVM-local code/rate maps independently on send and verify. In multi-instance deployments, codes sent during Redis outages can fail after Redis recovers or when verification routes to another instance, because the code exists only in the sender JVM.
  Current totals: 436 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 131 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 18:20 UTC: **Rate limiting deep dive found F281-F284.** Continued static code review across rate limiting and throttling implementation. Positive: login has multi-layer protection (per-IP lockout, per-account lockout, IP blacklisting). Found 4 new issues:
  - **F281 (HIGH)**: Per-endpoint rate limit key allows multiplicative bypass—each endpoint path gets its own bucket, so hitting 5 auth endpoints gives 5× the limit.
  - **F282 (HIGH)**: No endpoint-specific rate limits on sensitive auth endpoints—register allows 120/min, forgot-password allows 120/min, refresh allows 120/min.
  - **F283 (MEDIUM)**: Global rate limiting is in-memory only—`ConcurrentHashMap` not distributed across instances.
  - **F284 (MEDIUM)**: Registration endpoint lacks dedicated rate limiting—mass account creation possible at 120/min per IP.
  Current totals: 434 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 129 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 18:10 UTC: **Evidence correction marked F280 WONTFIX.** Rechecked F280 against current `ProductUrlImportService` and found the broad “no SSRF protection” claim is false: `normalizeAndValidateUri()` rejects non-http(s), credentials, non-standard ports, missing hosts, and resolved local/private/link-local/multicast addresses; redirects are normalized through the same guard. The remaining DNS rebinding/TOCTOU risk is already tracked by F167, so F280 was marked WONTFIX as duplicate/invalid. Current totals: 430 issues, 302 FIXED, 3 WONTFIX, 125 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 18:00 UTC: **File upload security review found F278-F280.** Continued static code review across file upload security. Positive: PetGallery upload has strong controls (magic byte validation, MIME allowlist, UUID filenames, path traversal prevention, quota enforcement). - 2026-05-30 17:40 UTC: **Admin role and WebSocket review found F272-F277.** Continued static code review across admin role/permission management and WebSocket security. Found 6 new issues:
  - **F277 (HIGH)**: WebSocket handler has no per-user or global connection limit—resource exhaustion via connection flood.
  - **F276 (MEDIUM)**: WebSocket handler has no server-side idle timeout—stale connections leak memory indefinitely.
  - **F275 (MEDIUM)**: `AdminRoleService.canAccess` default-allow for unmapped admin paths—new endpoints are automatically accessible to all admins.
  - **F274 (MEDIUM)**: `GET /admin/roles` has no super admin check—any admin can list all roles and permissions.
  - **F273 (CRITICAL)**: Hardcoded username "guhao" unconditionally promoted to SUPER_ADMIN on every startup—guaranteed backdoor.
  - **F272 (LOW)**: Client WebSocket reconnect uses fixed 2.5s interval—thundering herd on server recovery.
  Current totals: 427 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 124 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 17:20 UTC: **Database schema review found F268-F271.** Continued static code review across database schema, migrations, and data access patterns. Found 4 new issues:
  - **F271 (HIGH)**: `ProductMapper.xml` missing `free_shipping` and `free_shipping_threshold` columns—MyBatis never reads or writes these fields, so all products have null free shipping data.
  - **F270 (HIGH)**: V1 migration creates `product_reviews` but `schema.sql` uses `reviews` with no rename migration—databases initialized from V1_init.sql will have wrong table name.
  - **F269 (HIGH)**: Missing composite indexes on `orders(status, created_at)`, `payments(order_no, channel)`, `payments(transaction_id)`, `notifications(user_id, is_read)`—degrades all admin queries.
  - **F268 (MEDIUM)**: Missing FK constraints on `orders.coupon_id`, `orders.user_coupon_id`, and `reviews.order_id`—orphaned references possible.
  Current totals: 420 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 117 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 16:50 UTC: **Error handling review found F261-F264.** Continued static code review across error handling and resilience patterns. Found 4 new issues:
  - **F261 (HIGH)**: `AuthController.forgotPassword` leaks raw `e.getMessage()` to clients—can expose database constraint names, SQL errors, or class paths.
  - **F262 (MEDIUM)**: `ErrorController` returns Thymeleaf view names in REST API backend—template resolution failures produce unhelpful 500 errors.
  - **F263 (MEDIUM)**: Inconsistent error response format across controllers—`GlobalApiExceptionHandler` returns structured JSON while controller-local catches return ad-hoc `Map.of("error", ...)` shapes.
  - **F264 (MEDIUM)**: `AuthController.register` returns HTTP 400 for all exceptions including infrastructure failures—masks server errors as client errors.
  Current totals: 415 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 112 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 16:45 UTC: **Static coupon-grant QA found F267.** Continued coupon/admin mutation review after the alert-center recheck. Found that `CouponService.grant()` accepts normalized target user IDs without verifying the users exist, increments coupon claimed quantity, then inserts into `user_coupons`. Because `user_coupons.user_id` has a foreign key to `users(id)` and `AdminController.grantCoupon()` only catches `IllegalArgumentException | IllegalStateException`, granting to a nonexistent or concurrently deleted user can fall through to a database integrity exception and generic server error instead of the controlled `COUPON_GRANT` failure/audit response. Recorded F267 as OPEN. Current totals: 411 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 108 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 16:15 UTC: **Logistics and config center review found F256-F260.** Continued static code review across logistics/shipping and configuration management. Found 5 new issues:
  - **F256 (HIGH)**: Tracking number causes URL injection in `trackWithProvider` via unescaped `String.replace()`—URL metacharacters in tracking numbers inject extra query parameters.
  - **F257 (HIGH)**: `trackWithProvider` discards all external provider response data—always returns hardcoded EXTERNAL status with no actual tracking events.
  - **F258 (MEDIUM)**: Config center sensitive key pattern misses common secret key names like `*.key` and `*.api-key`—Kuaidi100 API signing key leaks unmasked.
  - **F259 (MEDIUM)**: `ConfigCenterService` creates a new Nacos ConfigService on every request—resource exhaustion from repeated connection pool/gRPC channel creation.
  - **F260 (MEDIUM)**: Tracking number has no format validation—URL-unsafe characters pass through, amplifying the URL injection surface.
  Current totals: 410 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 107 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 15:50 UTC: **Address concurrency QA found F266.** Continued customer data mutation review after wishlist validation. Found that `UserAddressService.addAddress()` enforces the per-user address limit with a read-then-insert check, and default-address changes clear all defaults before setting one row, but `user_addresses` has no database constraint or locking to enforce one default address per user. Concurrent address creates can exceed the configured max, and concurrent default updates can leave multiple `is_default=true` rows, making checkout/default-address selection unstable. Recorded F266 as OPEN. Current totals: 405 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 102 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 15:50 UTC: **Wishlist mutation validation QA found F265.** Continued the wishlist/favorites review after F253. Found that `WishlistService.addToWishlist()` checks only whether a row already exists, then inserts the requested `productId` without verifying product existence, active status, or handling duplicate concurrent inserts. Because `wishlist.product_id` has a foreign key to `products(id)` and `(user_id, product_id)` has a unique key, invalid product IDs or racing duplicate favorite actions can fall through to database constraint exceptions and generic server errors from `/wishlist/me/toggle`, legacy `/wishlist`, or `/favorites`. Recorded F265 as OPEN. Current totals: 404 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 101 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 15:50 UTC: **Dashboard analytics and pet/wishlist review found F247-F255.** Continued static code review across two additional dimensions: admin dashboard analytics accuracy and wishlist/pet gallery/pet profile features. Found 9 new issues:
  - **F247 (HIGH)**: Dashboard loads entire orders table into memory for aggregation with no caching—performance degrades linearly with order count.
  - **F248 (HIGH)**: Missing database indexes on `orders` table (status, created_at, user_id) degrade all admin filter and dashboard queries to full table scans.
  - **F249 (MEDIUM)**: Dashboard `netRevenue` is identical to `totalRevenue`—both set to `paidRevenue`, making one KPI card redundant.
  - **F250 (MEDIUM)**: Dashboard SLA uses Java `LocalDateTime.now()` while order list uses MySQL `NOW()`—timezone mismatch in containerized deployments.
  - **F251 (MEDIUM)**: Admin order summary runs 8 separate COUNT queries (N+1 pattern) per page view.
  - **F252 (MEDIUM)**: `PetProfileController` performs unsafe cast of authentication principal without `instanceof` check—500 error on non-standard principal types.
  - **F253 (MEDIUM)**: `WishlistService.resolveProductImageUrl` crashes with NPE when product has null `imagesList`.
  - **F254 (MEDIUM)**: PetGallery like endpoint has no rate limiting—like counts can be inflated via IP rotation.
  - **F255 (MEDIUM)**: PetGallery upload uses JVM-local `synchronized` keyword—ineffective in multi-instance deployments.
  Current totals: 403 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 100 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 15:10 UTC: **Extended multi-dimensional review found F232-F246.** Continued static code review across three additional dimensions: search/notification/support systems, i18n/API contracts, and data validation patterns. Backend Maven still unavailable. Frontend Jest still fails all 42 suites (F155/F156 unchanged). Found 15 new issues:
  - **F232 (HIGH)**: Notification list endpoint has no pagination or LIMIT clause—unbounded result sets for users with many notifications.
  - **F233 (HIGH)**: `broadcastToCustomers` loads all customer IDs into memory in a single transaction—OOM risk for large customer bases.
  - **F234 (HIGH)**: HTML sanitizer does not block `data:` URI scheme, allowing potential XSS bypass in notification content.
  - **F235 (MEDIUM)**: Search endpoint returns entire product catalog when no pagination is specified.
  - **F236 (MEDIUM)**: WebSocket handler extracts JWT from URL query parameter, exposing tokens in server logs.
  - **F237 (HIGH)**: Spanish translation missing verb "cargar" in `loadFailedRetry`—grammatically broken error message.
  - **F238 (MEDIUM)**: i18n auto-detection ignores Chinese and non-Mexican Spanish locales.
  - **F239 (HIGH)**: `mergeProduct()` unconditionally nulls 12 fields when omitted from partial update request.
  - **F240 (HIGH)**: No XSS sanitization on stored user-generated content (reviews, product descriptions).
  - **F241 (HIGH)**: `UserService.update()` performs no field validation before persisting.
  - **F242 (HIGH)**: Admin user update skips email format validation and uniqueness checks.
  - **F243 (MEDIUM)**: No Bean Validation annotations on any JPA entity class.
  - **F244 (MEDIUM)**: Order tracking endpoint exposes email address in URL query parameters.
  - **F245 (MEDIUM)**: `addOrderItem` accepts untyped OrderItem with no validation.
  - **F246 (MEDIUM)**: `RegisterRequest` has `@Valid` but no constraint annotations.
  Current totals: 394 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 91 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 14:30 UTC: **Multi-dimensional code review found F220-F231.** Performed comprehensive static code review across four dimensions: payment/order flow, cart/coupon system, authentication/security, and frontend code quality. Backend Maven (`mvn`) is unavailable in the current environment. Frontend Jest still fails all 42 suites due to `@adobe/css-tools` optional chaining syntax error in `setupTests.ts` (F155/F156 unchanged). Found 12 new issues:
  - **F229 (CRITICAL)**: Admin bootstrap endpoint `POST /users/create-admin` is publicly accessible (`permitAll`) with a non-atomic race condition on initial admin creation.
  - **F230 (HIGH)**: JWT secret validation bypassed in non-production mode—`JwtService.ensureJwtSecretConfigured()` returns early when `isProductionMode()` is false, accepting empty or weak secrets.
  - **F231 (HIGH)**: Non-empty `roleCode` values other than `USER` independently grant `ROLE_ADMIN` privileges via `UserDetailsImpl` authority mapping, regardless of the `role` field.
  - **F228 (CRITICAL)**: Direct order creation API (`POST /orders`) accepts client-supplied `originalAmount`, `discountAmount`, and `totalAmount` without server-side price verification—any authenticated user can create orders for arbitrary amounts.
  - **F227 (HIGH)**: Race condition in `PaymentService.createPayment()` read-then-insert pattern can produce duplicate active payment records for the same order-channel pair.
  - **F226 (HIGH)**: `Stripe.apiKey` is set as a global static field before each Stripe operation, causing race conditions in concurrent requests across different channels.
  - **F225 (HIGH)**: `assertNextStatus` REFUNDED escape hatch excludes `RETURNED` status, leaving returned orders stuck without a refund path if the refund gateway call fails.
  - **F224 (HIGH)**: `syncProviderPaymentState` marks Stripe payments as PAID without verifying the paid amount matches the expected payment amount, unlike `handleCallback` which performs this check.
  - **F223 (MEDIUM)**: Checkout coupon application is not atomic with order amount update—a crash after `couponService.useCoupon()` but before `orderRepository.update()` can consume the coupon without applying the discount.
  - **F222 (MEDIUM)**: Access tokens remain valid after password reset—`UserService.resetPassword()` and `updatePassword()` do not invalidate existing JWT tokens.
  - **F221 (CRITICAL)**: Module-level caches in `ProductList.tsx` and `ProductDetail.tsx` persist across auth transitions, risking cross-user data leakage when users share an SPA session.
  - **F220 (MEDIUM)**: `getCouponEstimatedValue` returns the payable amount instead of the discount amount for DISCOUNT coupons (e.g., shows 12 instead of 108 for a 90%-off coupon with threshold 120).
  Current totals: 378 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 75 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 14:00 UTC: **Static category-delete QA found F217.** Checked category management after brand status validation. Found that category deletion only pre-checks child categories, but does not check products assigned to the category before calling repository delete. Since `products.category_id` has a database foreign key to `categories(id)`, deleting a product-bearing leaf category can fall through to a database constraint exception that bypasses the controllers' `IllegalArgumentException` failure handling and actionable audit/error response. Recorded F217 as OPEN. Full local regression was not rerun because the previous manual full regression completed at 13:45 UTC, less than 20 minutes ago. Current totals: 366 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 63 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 13:54 UTC: **Static brand-status QA found F216.** Checked brand management after the carrier status validation pass. Found that `BrandService.save()` defaults only blank statuses to `ACTIVE` and otherwise persists any non-empty value, while BrandManagement only filters exact `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE` and public `/brands` returns only exact `ACTIVE` rows. Unsupported brand statuses can appear raw in the unfiltered admin table, disappear from supported status filters, and be omitted from storefront brand selectors. Recorded F216 as OPEN. Full local regression was not rerun because the previous manual full regression completed at 13:45 UTC, less than 20 minutes ago. Current totals: 365 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 62 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 13:10 UTC: **Static logistics-carrier QA found F214.** Checked logistics carrier management after the count/list scope pass. - 2026-05-30 12:08 UTC: **Static privileged-account status QA found F210.** Checked whether user-status updates have the same backend privilege boundary as role changes and deletion. Found that standard admins with the `users` page permission cannot change roles or delete existing admin/super-admin accounts, but the `PUT /admin/users/{id}` status branch does not require super admin before setting `BANNED` or `ACTIVE`. The frontend ban/unban button is disabled only for self, not for privileged target accounts. Recorded F210 as OPEN. Current totals: 359 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 56 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:56 UTC: **Static current-operator status QA found F209.** Checked user-management self-protection after the contact-edit review. Found that the UI disables ban/unban for the current operator, but backend `AdminController.updateUser()` only blocks current-operator role changes and deletion, not status changes. A direct `PUT /admin/users/{currentId}` with `{"status":"BANNED"}` can ban the active admin/super-admin account; later JWT requests stop authenticating because banned users are not enabled. Recorded F209 as OPEN. Current totals: 358 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 55 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:43 UTC: **Static config-center QA found F208.** Checked the masked sensitive-value workflow in config center. Found that backend responses mask sensitive keys in `content`, the frontend writes that masked content directly into the editable textarea, and apply/publish then rejects any sensitive key value containing `****`. Operators who only need to change an unrelated non-sensitive property must still know, replace, or remove every existing masked secret line before saving. Recorded F208 as OPEN. Current totals: 357 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 54 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:40 UTC: **User-reported alert-center mismatch rechecked.** User again reported “告警中心显示结果是空，但是上面有告警数目”. - 2026-05-30 11:39 UTC: **Static user contact validation QA found F207.** Checked user-management edits after the role-editor review. Found that the admin profile modal sends raw email/phone/address values to `PUT /admin/users/{id}`, and backend `AdminController.updateUser()` copies email/phone directly into the existing user before calling generic `userService.update(...)`. That path bypasses the validated `updateProfileContact(...)` contract used for customer self-profile edits, including email format/lowercase normalization, phone normalization/length limit, and duplicate email/phone checks. Recorded F207 as OPEN. Current totals: 356 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 53 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:26 UTC: **Static payment blacklist QA found F206.** Checked payment failure handling after the IP blacklist audit pass. Found that `PaymentController` records every `IllegalArgumentException`/`IllegalStateException` from create, sync, simulation, callback, and Stripe webhook paths as a payment blacklist failure, including backend/provider faults such as missing Stripe secrets, missing generic create URLs, gateway 5xx/non-2xx responses, invalid gateway responses, disabled simulation, missing production callback secret, and missing Stripe webhook secret. Repeated legitimate attempts during a payment outage can therefore escalate the customer or webhook IP to `BLOCKED`. Recorded F206 as OPEN. Current totals: 355 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 52 OPEN.

Recorded F205 as OPEN. Current totals: 354 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 51 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:06 UTC: **Static user-role QA found F203.** Checked user management role editing after permission-management QA. Found that the page exposes both `roleCode` and legacy `role` editors. For custom-role users, changing the legacy role to `ADMIN` preserves the old role code, and backend effective permissions still prioritize that role code. Operators can therefore believe they assigned standard ADMIN while permissions remain on the old custom role. Recorded F203 as OPEN. Current totals: 352 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 49 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:04 UTC: **Static order-management summary QA found F202.** Checked order quick summary cards after dashboard drilldown review. Found that backend quick-card counts are calculated with the active status/search scope, but clicking a quick card clears the status filter and opens the global quick queue. A card can therefore display 0 or a small filtered count and then navigate to a non-empty or much larger table. Recorded F202 as OPEN. Current totals: 351 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 48 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 11:01 UTC: **Static dashboard payment-method QA found F201.** Checked dashboard analytics after the drilldown-scope pass. Found that `paymentMethodBreakdown` counts every order with a nonblank `paymentMethod`, including unpaid/cancelled attempts created in `PENDING_PAYMENT`, while adjacent revenue, sales-trend revenue, and top-product metrics use paid-status scopes. Payment-method share can therefore be dominated by failed checkout attempts. Recorded F201 as OPEN. Current totals: 350 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 47 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:57 UTC: **Static coupon-management QA found F200.** Checked coupon management for the same count/list scope mismatch class as the alert-center report. Found that `/admin/coupons` returns only the newest `admin.coupons.search-max-rows` rows and `CouponManagement.tsx` applies keyword/status/scope filters locally, while `/admin/coupons/summary` counts all coupons. Older matching coupons can therefore make the insight cards non-zero while the filtered/search table is empty. Recorded F200 as OPEN. Current totals: 349 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 46 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:47 UTC: **Static Pet Gallery admin QA found F199.** Checked the Pet Gallery management status filters and backend soft-delete flow. Found that the UI exposes `ALL`, `ACTIVE`, and `DELETED` filters, and deletion sets photo status to `DELETED`, but the admin list endpoint always calls `findAllForAdmin()`, which returns only `ACTIVE` photos. The `DELETED` filter is therefore permanently empty after reload and admins cannot audit removed uploads from the page. Recorded F199 as OPEN. Current totals: 348 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 45 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:44 UTC: **Static dashboard logistics QA found F198.** Checked the dashboard logistics health panel after F197. Found that backend `shippedOrders` counts only `SHIPPED` orders, but `ordersWithTracking` counts every order with a non-blank tracking number regardless of status. Frontend divides those mixed scopes for tracking coverage and renders them next to a `SHIPPED`-scoped missing-tracking warning, so historical completed/returned tracked orders can inflate coverage to 100% while current shipped orders still lack tracking. Recorded F198 as OPEN. Current totals: 347 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 44 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:41 UTC: **Alert-center mismatch rechecked and dashboard fulfillment drilldown QA found F197.** User again reported “告警中心显示结果是空，但是上面有告警数目”; current code still defaults the alert list to `OPEN`, sends `status=OPEN`, and renders all-status summary cards, so F160 remains OPEN and no duplicate issue was added. Continued dashboard drilldown QA found that the commercial-readiness fulfillment card displays `pendingShipmentOrders + missingTrackingOrders` but routes either to `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT` or `/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING`, never both. If both queues have rows, the destination omits work included in the displayed count. Recorded F197 as OPEN. Current totals: 346 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 43 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:31 UTC: **Static permission-management role QA found F196.** Checked role/permission management after comparing frontend permission keys with backend enforcement. Found that `PermissionManagement.tsx` shows the same edit action for built-in `ADMIN` and `SUPER_ADMIN` roles and opens an editable modal, but backend `AdminRoleService.save()` rejects those reserved role codes with `Role code is reserved or empty`. Super admins can therefore spend time editing built-in role fields only to hit an unavoidable save failure. Recorded F196 as OPEN. Current totals: 345 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 42 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:28 UTC: **Static review-management insight QA found F195.** Checked admin review management after the count/list mismatch pass. Found that the insight cards mix scopes: backend `summary` counts PENDING/APPROVED/HIDDEN across all reviews matching only the search term, while `lowRating`, `needsReply`, and `averageRating` are computed from the current paginated `reviews` page in `ReviewManagement.tsx`. Moving pages can change those quality/reply metrics even though the filtered result set is unchanged, and status-filtered tables can still show all-status summary counts. Recorded F195 as OPEN. Current totals: 344 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 41 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:24 UTC: **User-reported alert-center mismatch rechecked.** User again reported “告警中心显示结果是空，但是上面有告警数目”. Rechecked current code and kept F160 OPEN: `AlertManagement.tsx` still initializes `status` to `OPEN`, still sends `status=OPEN` to `adminApi.getAlerts()`, still renders all-status counts from `/admin/alerts/summary`, and still shows only the generic empty state when that filtered list is empty. No duplicate issue was added. Full regression was not rerun because the previous local full regression completed at 10:13 UTC, less than 20 minutes ago. Current totals remain 343 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 40 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:22 UTC: **Static product batch action QA found F194.** Checked admin batch actions after comparing order and product management behavior. Found that ProductManagement keeps `selectedProductIds` after filters change and sends all selected ids to batch approve/reject, even if some rows are now hidden by status/category/search/listing-quality filters. Hidden products can therefore be approved or rejected unintentionally. Recorded F194 as OPEN. Current totals: 343 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 40 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:20 UTC: **Static dashboard refunding drilldown QA found F193.** Checked payment/refund dashboard cards after F185/F184. Found that the `refunding` card displays `refundingPayments`, counted from active `payments.status='REFUNDING'`, but routes to `/admin/audit-logs?view=refunds`, which filters the audit log to completed `REFUND_COMPLETE` events. If only in-progress/stuck refunds exist, the dashboard can show a non-zero refunding count and drill into an empty or unrelated completed-refund audit list. Recorded F193 as OPEN. Current totals: 342 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 39 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:17 UTC: **Static alert search QA found F192.** Checked the alert-center filter path after F160/F191. Found that the frontend sends omitted severity/category for `ALL` and blank filters, but backend `SystemAlertService.search()` normalizes null severity to `WARNING` and null category to `APPLICATION` before applying SQL filters. Default alert searches can therefore hide CRITICAL/ERROR/INFO or non-APPLICATION alerts even though the UI says all severities/categories. Recorded F192 as OPEN. Current totals: 341 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 38 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:08 UTC: **Static alert lifecycle QA found F191.** Checked alert-center state transitions after the count/list mismatch. Found that the frontend only shows Ack for `OPEN` alerts and batch acknowledge avoids `RESOLVED` rows, but the single backend acknowledge path updates by id without a current-status guard. A direct or stale single acknowledge request can move a resolved alert back to `ACKNOWLEDGED`. Recorded F191 as OPEN. Current totals: 340 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 37 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 10:04 UTC: **Alert-center mismatch rechecked and support WebSocket audit gap found F190.** User again reported “告警中心显示结果是空，但是上面有告警数目”; current code still defaults the alert list to `OPEN` while rendering all-status summary cards, so F160 remains OPEN and no duplicate issue was added. Continued static support QA found that connected admin replies prefer the support WebSocket path, but `SupportWebSocketHandler` calls `supportService.sendAdminMessage(...)` / `closeSession(...)` without the security audit logging present in the REST admin support controller. Recorded F190 as OPEN. Current totals: 339 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 36 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 09:59 UTC: **Static support realtime queue QA found F189.** Checked support management realtime updates after F187. Found that initial support sessions are loaded with the selected `status`, but WebSocket `MESSAGE`, `SESSION_UPDATED`, and `SESSION_CLOSED` updates mutate the local `sessions` array without reapplying the active `OPEN`/`CLOSED` filter. Realtime pushes can show open sessions in the closed queue or leave newly closed sessions visible in the open queue until a full reload. Recorded F189 as OPEN. Current totals: 338 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 35 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 09:58 UTC: **Static operations SLA drilldown QA found F188.** Checked dashboard operations SLA navigation. Found that the SLA cards count overdue subsets, but navigate to broad status filters such as `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT` instead of the existing `/admin/orders?quick=SLA_OVERDUE` filter. The drilldown can therefore show non-overdue rows that were not part of the dashboard SLA count. Recorded F188 as OPEN. Current totals: 337 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 34 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 09:54 UTC: **Static support queue QA found F187.** Continued checking management pages for count/list scope mismatches. Found that `SupportManagement.tsx` defaults the queue to `OPEN` and requests `/admin/support/sessions?status=OPEN`, but the insight bar renders global counts from `/admin/support/summary`, including closed sessions. If only closed historical sessions exist, support management can show a non-zero closed-session count above an empty default queue with a generic `No conversations` / `暂无会话` empty state. Recorded F187 as OPEN. Current totals: 336 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 33 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 09:38 UTC: **Static refund recovery QA found F185.** Checked the admin refund modal after reviewing the dashboard refunding count. Found that OrderManagement disables the refund modal OK button whenever loaded payments contain no `PAID` payment, even if the latest payment is already `REFUNDING`. Backend `RefundService.refundPaidPayment()` has explicit recovery logic for `REFUNDING` payments, so a stuck/in-progress refund cannot be continued from the UI. Recorded F185 as OPEN. Current totals: 334 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 31 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC: **Evidence review corrected F163-F169/F173/F175.** Rechecked the 08:45 deep-analysis entries against code. F163 is now WONTFIX because `User.password` is already `JsonProperty.WRITE_ONLY` and covered by `UserJsonSerializationTest`; no admin password-hash response exposure is evidenced. F164 was narrowed to notification HTML persistence, not product `detailContent`, because product rich detail renders structured text/media while notification broadcast stores HTML unchanged if direct callers bypass the frontend sanitizer. F167 was narrowed to DNS rebinding/TOCTOU SSRF risk because private/local IP validation already exists. F168 was corrected to guest-checkout-specific anti-spam controls, since global `RateLimitFilter` does cover `/orders/checkout/guest`. F169 was corrected to missing `/ws/support` in IP blacklist protected path prefixes. F173 now focuses on manual `Map.of("error", ...)` responses bypassing the shared error envelope, and F175 now focuses on missing pagination metadata plus ProductManagement full-list loading. Current totals: 330 issues, 301 FIXED, 2 WONTFIX, 27 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 08:23 UTC: **Static i18n runtime-key scan found missing literal keys.** Since full regression is not due until 08:26 UTC, ran low-cost static QA over literal `t('...')` calls and the effective locale objects loaded by `i18n.tsx`. The scan found 38 unresolved literal keys in English/Chinese and 18 in Spanish. Because `translate()` humanizes keys when English also lacks the path, storefront/admin UI can show placeholders such as `Next Action Ready Title`, `Direct Ready`, `Choose Options Action`, `Result Context Label`, `Login`, `Products`, and `Support`. Recorded F162 as OPEN in `QA_ISSUES.md`. Current totals: 310 issues, 301 FIXED, 1 WONTFIX, 8 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 08:17 UTC: **Locale duplicate-key follow-up.** Because F157 showed Spanish pet-gallery admin keys missing even though a block of translations exists in `es.json`, ran a static duplicate-key scan and direct `JSON.parse` check. Found `pages.petGalleryAdmin` duplicated in all three locale files: English lines 2076/2801, Spanish lines 1113/2102, and Chinese lines 1907/2910. JSON keeps only the later object, which explains the Spanish 12-key coverage failure and also drops current pet-gallery management copy in English/Chinese. Recorded F161 as OPEN in `QA_ISSUES.md`. Current totals: 309 issues, 301 FIXED, 1 WONTFIX, 7 OPEN.

- 2026-05-30 07:14 UTC: **Admin pet-gallery moderation permission mapping.** Added a dedicated `pet-gallery` backend page permission, mapped `/admin/pet-gallery` routes to it, exposed the permission in the role editor, and added admin-layout labels so limited admin roles cannot directly call pet-gallery moderation APIs unless explicitly granted. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 07:11 UTC: **Custom admin role fallback hardening.** Restricted the standard-admin permission fallback to users whose broad role and roleCode are both the built-in `ADMIN`; inactive, empty, or missing custom role permission rows now resolve to no fallback permissions instead of broad admin access. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 07:07 UTC: **Admin order detail/payment contract separation.** Added `/admin/orders/{id}`, `/admin/orders/{id}/items`, `/admin/orders/{id}/payments`, and `/admin/orders/payments/{paymentId}/sync`; added matching `adminApi` helpers; switched OrderManagement and SupportManagement order-detail/payment reads to admin APIs; and removed unused frontend admin payment helpers from `paymentApi` so backend pages no longer depend on customer/general order/payment paths. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 07:05 UTC: **Admin role permission mapping fix.** Mapped `/admin/roles` and `/admin/users/{id}/role-code` to the `permissions` privilege in the backend admin permission resolver, left `/admin/me/permissions` available for menu bootstrap, and added `questions` to the frontend role editor permission list. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 06:56 UTC: **Admin catalog write contract separation.** Added `/admin/products`, `/admin/brands`, and `/admin/categories` create/update/delete endpoints with existing validation and audit logging, added matching `adminApi` helpers, and switched ProductManagement, BrandManagement, and CategoryManagement writes to admin APIs. Existing public write routes remain for compatibility. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 06:49 UTC: **Public product legacy-field contract cleanup.** Removed fallback-only `categoryName`, raw `rating`, `sizes`, and `colors` from `ProductPublic`; added `ProductCatalogSnapshotProduct` for local snapshot/fallback data; storefront pages now use `averageRating`, and `sizes/colors` remain only as compatibility input for option normalization. - 2026-05-30 06:42 UTC: **Admin category read contract separation.** Added authenticated `GET /admin/categories` for full category rows, added `adminApi.getCategories()`, changed CategoryManagement to use the admin endpoint, and kept ProductManagement category selectors on public `CategoryPublic` via `/categories`. Existing admin permission mapping covers `/admin/categories` as `categories`. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 06:33 UTC: **Customer/guest payment frontend contract split.** Renamed the safe storefront payment response type to `PaymentCustomer`, changed customer/guest payment create, sync, history, latest, Checkout, Profile, OrderTracking, and payment recovery helpers to use it, and kept provider/refund/callback fields only on `AdminPayment` for admin order management. - 2026-05-30 06:07 UTC: **Customer support frontend contract split.** Customer and guest support API calls now use `SupportSessionCustomer` / `SupportMessageCustomer`, matching the narrowed backend DTOs and keeping admin ids, context keys, sender ids, admin unread counters, usernames, and payload metadata out of the customer widget contract. Admin support management still uses full support types. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 05:42 UTC: **Logistics response raw payload minimization.** Logistics tracking responses now expose only normalized trackingNumber, carrier, status, summary, and events. - 2026-05-30 05:39 UTC: **Product-question submit response minimization.** Signed-in customer question submission now returns `ProductQuestionPublicResponse` instead of raw `ProductQuestion`, keeping only id, productId, question, answer, answeredAt, and createdAt in the storefront submit response. - 2026-05-30 05:34 UTC: **Customer and guest order response minimization.** Customer order list/detail/items, guest order detail/items, checkout responses, direct customer create responses, and public order tracking now return `OrderCustomerResponse` / `OrderItemCustomerResponse` through `OrderTrackResponse`. Storefront payloads no longer expose owner `userId`, coupon linkage ids, raw order-item `orderId`, or operational update timestamps, and guest shipping addresses are returned without the embedded guest email. Admin order management keeps full `Order` / `OrderItem` data. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; static patch checks only.

- 2026-05-30 05:19 UTC: **Customer support response minimization.** Customer and guest support REST endpoints now return `SupportSessionCustomerResponse` / `SupportMessageCustomerResponse`, removing `userId`, `assignedAdminId`, `contextKey`, `senderId`, and admin read counters from storefront support payloads. - 2026-05-30 05:12 UTC: **Customer notification response minimization.** Customer notification list endpoints now return `NotificationResponse`, exposing only id, type, title, message, contentFormat, isRead, and createdAt. - 2026-05-30 05:08 UTC: **Cart and wishlist response minimization.** Customer cart list endpoints now return `CartItemResponse`, and wishlist plus `/favorites` list endpoints now return `WishlistItemResponse`, removing owner `userId` from storefront cart/wishlist payloads while preserving item/product display fields, selected specs, price, stock/status, and wishlist createdAt. - 2026-05-30 05:02 UTC: **Customer address response minimization.** Customer address list/detail/default/create/update endpoints and the legacy `/user/addresses` alias now return `UserAddressResponse`, exposing only id, recipientName, phone, address, isDefault, createdAt, and updatedAt. `userId` remains server-side for ownership checks and admin/internal logic, but is no longer returned by customer address responses. - 2026-05-30 04:15 UTC: **Public product review privacy hardening.** Public `/reviews/product/{productId}` and `/products/{productId}/reviews` now return `PublicReviewResponse` instead of raw `Review` entities, masking customer names and removing internal `userId`, `orderId`, `status`, and product entity payloads from storefront responses. - 2026-05-30 03:58 UTC: **Sensitive public endpoint IP blacklist coverage.** Expanded the protected IP blacklist path defaults and config-center template to include forgot-password, register/refresh, admin bootstrap, guest order tracking/actions, and guest support. - 2026-05-30 03:53 UTC: **Guest email claim verification.** Registration now requires a valid mailbox code before an existing `GUEST` checkout email can be converted into a registered account, so knowing a guest checkout email is not enough to inherit that order/support history. `UserService` refuses guest-account upgrades unless `AuthController` has verified the email code, and the register page now prompts for/sends a one-time code only when the backend flags `emailCodeRequired`. Email-code failures are recorded as register-email-claim failures for IP blacklist/audit clarity. No commits made and no build/UI/API tests run per instruction; `git diff --check` passed for touched files.
- 2026-05-30 03:38 UTC: **Pet-gallery like idempotency and F102 follow-up.** Public pet-gallery likes now use a required `viewerKey` (`user:{id}` or `ip:{clientIp}`) and a unique `(photo_id, viewer_key)` constraint, so repeated or concurrent anonymous likes from the same IP cannot increment a photo more than once. Startup schema hardening backfills viewer keys, removes duplicate historical likes, recalibrates historical `like_count` from de-duplicated like rows, and adds the new index/constraint for old databases. Ordinary signed-in support now only lists, counts unread messages for, and allows REST/WebSocket access to null/blank-context support sessions, while `guest-order:{orderNo}` sessions remain accessible only through `/support/guest/...`. - 2026-05-30 03:31 UTC: **Guest support order context isolation.** Added optional `support_sessions.context_key` with startup schema/index hardening.
