### F748: English notification campaign readiness copy exposes an internal QA label

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Admin notification broadcast / Commercial copy
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 18:33 UTC commercial copy scan found `frontend/src/locales/en.json` had `pages.notificationAdmin.readinessEyebrow` set to `Campaign QA`. `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.tsx` renders this string in the notification broadcast readiness panel, so admin operators could see internal QA wording in a commercial workflow.
- Expected: Operator-facing campaign readiness copy should use production/commercial language and avoid internal QA labels.
- Actual: The English notification broadcast readiness eyebrow displayed `Campaign QA`.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 18:34 UTC by changing the English copy to `Campaign readiness` and extending `frontend/src/i18n.test.ts` to reject standalone `QA` labels across all English locale strings.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:34 UTC EN/ES/ZH locale JSON parse passed; `rg -n "\\bQA\\b" frontend/src/locales/en.json frontend/src/locales/es.json frontend/src/locales/zh.json frontend/src -g '*.json' -g '*.tsx' -g '*.ts'` found only the i18n test assertion name; targeted `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/i18n.test.ts` passed with 1 suite / 4 tests.

### F747: Mobile admin batch-selection checkboxes have tiny hit targets and weak row context

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Mobile Web admin tables / Batch selection accessibility
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 18:20 UTC mobile Web UI tester review found `/admin/orders` at `360x740` rendered row-selection checkbox wrappers around `16x22` while the selection cells were much larger, so tapping the selection-column blank area did not select a row. The same small checkbox pattern was observed on `/admin/alerts`, and the row checkboxes lacked order/alert context names. Static scan found the same `rowSelection` pattern in IP blacklist and product management.
- Expected: Mobile batch-selection controls should provide at least a 44px hit target and expose row-specific names such as the order number, alert title, IP address, or product name.
- Actual: Users had to hit the small checkbox glyph directly, and assistive technology could encounter generic selection controls without row context.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 18:22 UTC by adding shared `shop-admin-selection-table` mobile CSS for 56px selection columns and 44px checkbox wrappers, adding `labelTableSelectionCheckbox(...)` for table-header select-all labels, and wiring row-specific `aria-label`/`title` values in `OrderManagement`, `AlertManagement`, `IpBlacklistManagement`, and `ProductManagement` with EN/ZH/ES locale keys.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:27 UTC locale JSON parse passed; targeted frontend Jest `src/i18n.test.ts` passed; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed; `npm run build` passed with existing Browserslist stale-data warnings and generated `main.5ed68247.js`.

### F746: Desktop storefront and admin controls expose weak or misleading UI semantics

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Desktop Web storefront and admin UI semantics
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 18:08 UTC desktop Web UI tester review found product-detail related-carousel slick cloned slides remained keyboard-focusable while `aria-hidden=true`; cart quantity steppers exposed repeated generic `Increase Value`/`Decrease Value` names across product rows; Pet Gallery delete confirmation visible copy omitted the target photo; and several admin pagination controls read as `left`/`right` instead of previous/next page actions.
- Expected: Hidden carousel clones should not enter the Tab order, repeated row actions should include row context, destructive confirmations should visibly name the target, and pagination controls should expose previous/next page semantics.
- Actual: Keyboard and assistive-technology users could focus invisible carousel controls, hear ambiguous cart/admin controls, or confirm deletion without visible target context.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 18:14 UTC by adding carousel focus cleanup utilities/tests and applying them to ProductDetail, replacing cart quantity steppers with contextual custom increment/decrement controls, adding target details to Pet Gallery delete confirmations, and centralizing localized admin pagination previous/next labels through `paginationLabels.tsx`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:27 UTC targeted frontend Jest `src/utils/carouselAccessibility.test.ts src/i18n.test.ts` passed; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed; `npm run build` passed with existing Browserslist stale-data warnings and generated `main.5ed68247.js`.

### F745: Native App update and WebView UI recovery gaps

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Mobile App / WebView runtime metadata / Update gate / Mobile UI hotfix
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 18:08 UTC App UI tester review found WebView runtime config reported `1.0.27/10027` while Android Gradle and release manifest remained `1.0.23/10023`, allowing mandatory update checks for `1.0.24-1.0.27` to be skipped. The mandatory update dialog could become a dead end if all download open paths failed, the coupon mobile keyboard hotfix watched `.coupon-center__mobileAction` while the page rendered `.coupon-center-page__mobileActionBar`, and English checkout displayed internal `Checkout QA` copy.
- Expected: Packaged App runtime metadata should match the release manifest, mandatory update failures should provide a recovery action, keyboard hotfix selectors should match rendered mobile action bars, and customer copy should not expose internal QA wording.
- Actual: App users could miss required updates, get stuck in a mandatory update modal after download failure, see coupon action bars fail to move above the keyboard, or see internal QA copy in checkout.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 18:14 UTC by restoring App runtime version metadata to `1.0.23/10023`, adding a mandatory-update copy-download-link recovery path and localized copy, adding the actual coupon action-bar selector to packaged mobile UI hotfix assets, extending `verify-mobile-release-assets.js` to validate runtime version consistency, and changing English checkout copy to `Checkout readiness`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:27 UTC targeted frontend Jest `src/utils/mobileUpdate.test.ts src/i18n.test.ts` passed; `npm run build` passed; `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile` `npm run prepare:web`, `npx cap sync`, and `node scripts/verify-mobile-release-assets.js web android/app/src/main/assets/public ios/App/App/public` passed. Capacitor reported iOS pod install/xcodebuild clean skipped because CocoaPods/xcodebuild are not installed.

### F742: Admin support session responses expose internal context keys

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Backend + frontend / Admin support API and WebSocket contract
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 17:43 UTC support API contract audit found `SupportAdminSessionPageResponse` stored `List<SupportSession>`, `SupportController` returned raw `SupportSession` from admin support send-message, close, assign, and reopen responses, and `SupportWebSocketHandler.adminPayload(...)` broadcast raw `SupportSession` to admin sockets. `SupportSession.contextKey` is an internal routing key used for guest-order support isolation, while `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx` does not display it and only included it in queue search.
- Expected: Admin support APIs and WebSocket payloads should expose only the stable fields needed by the admin console; internal support routing keys should remain server-side implementation details.
- Actual: Admin support session REST and WebSocket responses could serialize `contextKey` values such as `guest-order:<orderNo>`, making an internal routing key part of the public admin API contract.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 17:47 UTC by adding `SupportAdminSessionResponse` without `contextKey`, mapping admin support page items and action/message/WebSocket session payloads through that DTO, stripping `contextKey` from legacy admin support page responses in the frontend API normalizer, removing it from the frontend `SupportSession` type, and removing it from support queue search.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 17:48 UTC targeted backend `./mvnw -Dtest=SupportControllerAdminResponseTest,SupportWebSocketHandlerAdminPayloadTest test` passed with 5 tests covering REST and WebSocket admin session serialization without `contextKey`; targeted frontend `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/api/index.test.ts` passed with 53 tests including legacy admin support page `contextKey` stripping; `npm run build` passed with the existing Browserslist stale-data warning and generated `main.322761ff.js`; touched-file `git diff --check` passed.

### F741: Customer and guest payment APIs expose refund references through the shared payment DTO

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Backend / Payment API contract / Customer and guest payment responses
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 16:48 UTC delegated API/backend tester review found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/dto/PaymentResponse.java` defined `refundReference` and populated it for refunded payments. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/PaymentController.java` reused that DTO for customer and guest payment reads, latest-payment reads, payment creation, and payment sync, while frontend `PaymentCustomer` intentionally lacks `refundReference` and reserves it for `AdminPayment`.
- Expected: Customer and guest payment APIs should expose checkout/payment display state only; refund gateway/manual references should remain admin-only.
- Actual: Refunded customer/guest payment responses could include `refundReference`.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 16:48 UTC by adding `PaymentCustomerResponse` without `refundReference`, making `PaymentResponse` extend it for admin responses, and returning the customer DTO from customer/guest `PaymentController` paths. Admin payment and refund paths continue to use `PaymentResponse`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 17:32 UTC targeted backend `PaymentControllerCustomerResponseTest,AdminControllerPaymentResponseTest,PaymentControllerSimulationAccessTest,OrderControllerLegacyAdminAuditTest` passed with 16 tests, including customer/guest no-`refundReference` serialization and admin `simulate-paid` returning `PaymentResponse`; full `./mvnw test` passed with 434 tests; full frontend `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 227 tests; frontend `npm run build` passed with the existing Browserslist stale-data warning and generated `main.77a0b329.js`; public/build/live download directories contain no APK/AAB files; public/build mobile manifests remain `releaseSigned:false` with empty `apkUrl` and `legacyApkUrl`.

### F740: Rich-detail preview accepts IPv4-mapped private media hosts that API submit drops

- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 16:25 UTC URL-boundary audit found `ProductRichDetail.isHttpMediaUrl` used a separate host validator that rejected plain localhost/private IPv4 hosts but missed IPv4-mapped IPv6 private/loopback hosts such as `https://[::ffff:192.168.1.10]/image.jpg` and `https://[::ffff:7f00:1]/image.jpg`. The shared persistent media resolver used by API submit normalization rejects those values, creating another preview-vs-save mismatch.
- Expected: Rich-detail preview, ProductManagement filtering, API submit normalization, and persistent display should use the same media allowlist.
- Actual: Rich-detail preview could accept mapped-private HTTP(S) media URLs that API submit normalization would drop.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 16:25 UTC in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx` by making `isHttpMediaUrl` delegate to `normalizePersistentImageUrl` from `frontend/src/utils/mediaAssets.ts`. Added regression coverage in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` and `frontend/src/api/index.test.ts`.

### F738: Product rich-detail preview still accepts non-upload root-relative media paths

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product rich-detail editor and storefront renderer / Media URL contract
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 16:08 UTC source audit found `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx` still returned true for any `trimmed.startsWith('/')` media URL. After F735/F736, `frontend/src/api/index.ts` normalizes persistent product media through `normalizePersistentImageUrl`, and backend `ImageUrlValidator` rejects non-upload root-relative paths. As a result, `/assets/products/preview-only.jpg` could render in admin rich-detail preview but be dropped before API submit or rejected by backend direct save/import.
- Expected: Product rich-detail preview, ProductManagement filtering, frontend submit normalization, and backend save/import validation should share the same persistent media contract: uploaded local media must be `/uploads/...` or legacy `uploads/...`, and all other local/root-relative paths should be rejected; safe HTTP(S) URLs remain allowed.
- Actual: The rich-detail preview helper accepted non-upload root-relative paths such as `/assets/products/preview-only.jpg`.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 16:08 UTC in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx` by allowing root-relative media only under `/uploads/...` while preserving legacy rootless `uploads/...` normalization. Added regression coverage in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` and `frontend/src/api/index.test.ts`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 16:13 UTC targeted frontend Jest `src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` and `src/api/index.test.ts` passed with 2 suites / 59 tests; full `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 227 tests; `npm run build` passed and generated `main.e5d274b2.js`; touched-file `git diff --check` passed; no APK/AAB files exist under `frontend/public/downloads`, `frontend/build/downloads`, or `/var/www/shoptest/downloads`; `frontend/public/downloads/mobile-version.json` and `frontend/build/downloads/mobile-version.json` remain `releaseSigned:false` with empty `apkUrl` and `legacyApkUrl`. ### F736: Legacy uploaded product image paths can be dropped on save while product backend accepts non-upload root paths

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend/backend / Catalog image persistence / Product, category, brand, rich-detail, and variant media
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 15:40 UTC source audit found the shared display resolver preserved legacy persisted `uploads/...` image paths by rendering them as `/uploads/...`, but frontend API submit normalization cleared the same values before save and rich-detail preview rejected them. A merchant editing an existing product/category/brand record with legacy upload paths could therefore see an image before edit and lose it after saving unrelated changes. The backend brand/category validator also rejected rootless uploaded paths instead of normalizing them, while the product direct-save/import validator still accepted arbitrary root-relative paths such as `/assets/products/main.jpg`, creating a policy gap against the stricter persistent-image contract from F735.
- Expected: Persistent catalog images should round-trip legacy uploaded paths by normalizing `uploads/...` to `/uploads/...`, and should reject every other bare or non-upload root-relative path. Product main images, gallery images, rich-detail media, and variant images should follow the same persistent-image policy in frontend submit paths and backend direct-save/import paths.
- Actual: Legacy upload paths could be displayed but then cleared on submit, while direct product save/import could still persist non-upload root-relative paths.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 15:40 UTC in `frontend/src/api/index.ts`, `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/util/ImageUrlValidator.java`, and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/impl/ProductServiceImpl.java`. Frontend API normalization now preserves `uploads/...` as `/uploads/...`, rich-detail preview accepts the same legacy uploaded shape, the shared backend image validator normalizes rootless upload paths, and product save/import now rewrites uploaded main/gallery/detail/variant image URLs while rejecting non-upload root-relative paths.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 15:40 UTC targeted frontend Jest `src/api/index.test.ts` and `src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` passed with 2 suites / 59 tests; targeted Maven `BrandServiceTest,CategoryServiceImplTest,ProductSaveServiceTest,ProductImportServiceTest` passed with 79 tests; full `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 227 tests; full `./mvnw test` passed with 430 tests; `npm run build` passed and generated `main.ad69b26b.js`; touched-file `git diff --check` passed; no APK/AAB files exist under `frontend/public/downloads`, `frontend/build/downloads`, or `/var/www/shoptest/downloads`; public/build mobile manifests remain unsigned with empty APK URLs. No live deployment, service restart, APK generation/publish, screenshot/video/trace, commit, or destructive git operation was performed.

### F735: Persisted catalog image resolver accepts data/blob and non-upload relative URLs

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend/backend / Persisted catalog media / Product, category, and brand images
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 14:52 UTC source audit found the shared frontend media resolver accepted persisted `data:image/*`, `blob:`, protocol-relative or arbitrary relative/API-relative image URLs on product, category, brand, cart, order, support, storefront, and pet-gallery image surfaces. Product variant images and local catalog snapshots could preserve the same unsafe values, and direct backend brand/category API saves accepted unsafe `logoUrl`/`imageUrl` values. A delegated tester review confirmed the risk and then found a residual IPv4-mapped IPv6/private-host bypass, which was fixed before final verification.
- Expected: Persisted catalog images should be either safe credential-free HTTP(S) URLs on standard web ports or `/uploads/...` paths; generated local fallbacks should remain explicit fallbacks, not persisted data/blob image input.
- Actual: Stored or submitted image data could render injected/stale/invalid images, preserve unsafe local values, or resolve unexpected relative/API-origin paths across multiple storefront/admin surfaces.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 14:52 UTC in `frontend/src/utils/mediaAssets.ts`, `frontend/src/api/index.ts`, `frontend/src/utils/productOptions.ts`, `frontend/src/utils/productCatalogSnapshot.ts`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/util/ImageUrlValidator.java`, `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/BrandService.java`, and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/impl/CategoryServiceImpl.java`. The frontend now normalizes persistent image URLs through a strict allowlist, keeps generated fallback data-SVG URLs only through `generatedFallbackImageUrls`, rejects unsafe persistent values during API submit and local option/snapshot reads, and the backend rejects unsafe brand/category image saves.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 14:52 UTC targeted Jest image/API suites passed with 4 suites / 66 tests; targeted Maven `BrandServiceTest,CategoryServiceImplTest` passed with 10 tests; full `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 227 tests; `npm run build` passed and generated `main.9f3a1077.js`; full `./mvnw test` passed with 424 tests; touched-file `git diff --check` passed; public/build/live downloads contain no APK/AAB files; public/build mobile manifests remain unsigned with empty APK URLs. No live deployment, service restart, APK generation/publish, screenshot/video/trace, commit, or destructive git operation was performed.

### F734: Product rich-detail media preview accepts URLs the save contract drops or rejects

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product management rich-detail media / Preview-save contract
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 13:45 UTC source audit compared `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx`, `frontend/src/api/index.ts`, and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/impl/ProductServiceImpl.java`. The shared rich-detail media helper accepted bare relative URLs such as `uploads/products/detail.jpg` because it resolved them against `window.location.origin`, so ProductManagement could mark them valid and show image/video previews. The API normalizer/backend contract only accepts root-relative `/...` or absolute `http(s)://...` URLs, and the backend rejects localhost/private/internal hosts plus non-standard web ports. As a result, operators could preview media that was removed before save, or send media URLs that backend validation rejected.
- Expected: Product rich media preview, product form filtering, API normalization, and backend validation should agree on the same URL shapes so invalid media is shown as invalid before save and valid previewed media is preserved.
- Actual: The preview helper was more permissive than the save/API/backend contract for bare relative URLs and backend-rejected network targets.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 13:45 UTC in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx` and `frontend/src/api/index.ts`. Rich media URLs now allow root-relative paths and credential-free HTTP(S) URLs only, reject bare relative paths, reject unsafe/control/backslash/encoded-backslash shapes, reject localhost/private/link-local/multicast/internal hosts, and reject non-standard web ports. ProductManagement main-image/gallery/detail filtering inherits the stricter helper, and API payload normalization now drops out-of-contract detail media before sending.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 13:45 UTC `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/components/ProductRichDetail.test.tsx` passed with 7 tests; `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/api/index.test.ts` passed with 51 tests; full `npm test -- --watchAll=false` passed with 45 suites / 226 tests and existing React act/Router future warnings only; `npm run build` passed with the existing Browserslist stale-data warning and generated `main.52f0e7d6.js`; touched-file `git diff --check` passed; public/build/live downloads still contain no APK/AAB files, and public/build mobile manifests remain unsigned with empty APK URLs. ### F733: Backend notification broadcast strips preview-approved relative links and lacks server-side field bounds

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Backend / Admin notification broadcast / HTML sanitization and API contract
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 13:22 UTC source audit compared `frontend/src/utils/sanitizeHtml.ts`, `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.tsx`, and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationService.java`. The admin preview/client sanitizer resolves URL attributes with a browser base URL and preserves safe path-relative links such as `coupons/spring` and `./profile?tab=orders`, but the backend sanitizer rejected every non-fragment URL without an explicit scheme unless it started with `/`. The saved customer notification could therefore lose an internal campaign/order link that the admin preview showed as clickable. Tester-agent backend security review also found the backend start-tag regex missed browser-accepted slash-before-attribute markup such as `<a/href=javascript:...>` and `<img/src=... onerror=...>`, so those attributes could bypass the normal attribute rebuild/sanitization path. The same backend broadcast path accepted direct API payloads with titles longer than the `notifications.title VARCHAR(100)` and admin form contract, and messages longer than the admin composer's 5000-character limit, before starting active-customer lookup and batch insertion.
- Expected: The backend notification broadcast contract should match the preview for safe internal links, keep the existing unsafe URL/HTML stripping, and reject out-of-contract title/message sizes before customer batching or database writes.
- Actual: Safe path-relative links were stripped server-side, while oversize direct API payloads were not rejected until later database/batch behavior.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 13:22 UTC in `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationService.java`. `broadcastToCustomers(...)` now trims and validates title/message length before batching, passes the normalized title through batch creation, allows scheme-less relative URLs after the existing unsafe-shape checks, and expands start-tag matching so slash-separated attributes are rebuilt and sanitized instead of passed through raw. The sanitizer continues to reject event/style/srcdoc attributes, blocked executable/embedded tags, unsafe schemes, protocol-relative URLs, credentialed absolute URLs, control characters, raw/encoded backslashes, and encoded NUL payloads. `_blank` anchors still receive `rel="noopener noreferrer"`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 13:22 UTC `./mvnw -Dtest=NotificationServiceTest test` passed with 10 tests; full `./mvnw test` passed with 414 tests; `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/utils/sanitizeHtml.test.ts` passed with 6 frontend sanitizer tests; `git diff --check -- src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationService.java src/test/java/com/example/shop/service/NotificationServiceTest.java` passed; public/build/live downloads still contain no APK/AAB files. No frontend build, Playwright/E2E, screenshot/video/trace, live deployment, service restart, APK generation/publish, commit, or destructive git operation was performed.

### F732: Admin rich-detail video preview iframe lacks the storefront embed containment

- Area: Frontend / Product rich-detail editor / Embedded media preview

The storefront `ProductRichDetail` renderer already used a safer iframe contract, so the admin editor preview had weaker containment than the customer-facing renderer.

- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 13:03 UTC in `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetailEditor.tsx`. Added `sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-presentation"`, `referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin"`, and `allow="fullscreen; picture-in-picture"` to the editor preview iframe while preserving existing YouTube/Vimeo/direct-video preview behavior.

### F696: Signed-in pending-payment rollback can hide cart-restore failures

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Order tracking / Pending-payment rollback and cart recovery
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 06:01 UTC buyer-flow static regression found `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx` using `Promise.allSettled` for signed-in cart restore after `orderApi.cancel(...)`, writing failed signed-in cart additions into guest cart, then still showing `rollbackPaymentSuccess` and navigating to `/cart`. Signed-in cart views read server cart data, so guest-cart fallback items would not be visible after navigation.
- Expected: After canceling a pending-payment order, signed-in restore failures should not be hidden in guest cart or reported as full success. The UI should only navigate to cart when restored items will actually be visible there.
- Actual: A signed-in buyer could see a success message while one or more restored products were missing from the signed-in cart.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 06:01 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx` and checkout locale files. Signed-in restore now counts fulfilled/failed `cartApi.addItem(...)` results and never falls back to guest cart. Guest restore still uses guest cart with success/failure counting. If any restore fails, the page keeps the canceled order visible and shows the localized `rollbackPaymentCartRestorePartial` warning; full success still shows the success message and navigates to `/cart`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 06:01 UTC targeted scans confirmed `restoreTrackedItemsToCart`, `restoreResult.failed`, and `rollbackPaymentCartRestorePartial` are wired, with the new checkout key present in English, Chinese, and Spanish locales. `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, Gradle, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish/cleanup, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F695: Frontend mobile manifest generator can mark an APK signed without verifying the APK certificate

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Mobile release manifest generation / APK signing gate
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 05:41 UTC release-readiness static regression found `frontend/package.json` running `node scripts/generate-mobile-version.js` as ordinary `prebuild`, while `frontend/scripts/generate-mobile-version.js` treated `MOBILE_RELEASE_SIGNED=true` plus a non-debug-looking `MOBILE_RELEASE_CERTIFICATE_SHA256` string as enough to emit signed `apkUrl` and `legacyApkUrl` values. Unlike `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-android-apk.js`, it did not read the selected APK's actual signing certificate before generating a signed manifest.
- Expected: Any script that can emit a signed/public mobile release manifest must verify the actual APK certificate, reject Android Debug certificates, and require the APK certificate SHA-256 to match the manifest certificate value.
- Actual: A misconfigured frontend build environment could mark a stale or debug APK as signed by setting environment variables, bypassing the stronger mobile release script gate.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 05:41 UTC in `frontend/scripts/generate-mobile-version.js`. Added `apksigner` / `keytool` certificate extraction helpers and made `validateReleaseSigningMetadata(...)` read the selected APK certificate whenever `MOBILE_RELEASE_SIGNED=true`. The script now fails if the certificate cannot be read, is the Android Debug certificate, or differs from `MOBILE_RELEASE_CERTIFICATE_SHA256`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 05:41 UTC `node --check frontend/scripts/generate-mobile-version.js` passed, `git diff --check -- frontend/scripts/generate-mobile-version.js` passed, and targeted scans confirmed `readApkCertificateSha256`, the mismatch error, and `validateReleaseSigningMetadata(releaseSigned, certificateSha256, latestApk.filePath)` are present. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, Gradle, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish/cleanup, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F692: Pending-shipment refund restock UI and audit log can disagree with actual backend behavior

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend + Backend / Admin order refund / Inventory and audit consistency
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 05:20 UTC admin/data static regression found the refund modal allowed unchecking `restock=false` for `PENDING_SHIPMENT` orders, while `OrderService.refundOrder(...)` still restored stock whenever the original order status was `PENDING_SHIPMENT`. `AdminController` audit details recorded only the requested `restock` flag, so the audit log could say `restock=false` even when stock was restored.
- Expected: For pending-shipment refunds, the UI and audit log should show the actual mandatory restock behavior, or the backend should follow the request. Commercial inventory/audit records must not diverge.
- Actual: UI, request payload, actual stock mutation, and audit details could disagree.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 05:20 UTC. `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx` now computes `effectiveRestock`, submits it, disables the restock checkbox for `PENDING_SHIPMENT` orders, and shows an informational mandatory-restock note. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java` now audits `effectiveRestock` and records `requestedRestock` when it differs. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java` names the effective restock decision as `shouldRestock`, matching the source behavior.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 05:20 UTC targeted scans confirmed `effectiveRestock`, `refundRestockRequired`, `restockAudit`, and `shouldRestock` wiring. `git diff --check` passed for the touched source files. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, Gradle, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish/cleanup, or commit was performed per current instruction.

- Expected: Changing storefront announcement visibility should require a target-aware confirmation that includes the announcement label and target action.
- Actual: The switch updated status immediately on change.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 05:20 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.tsx`. Wrapped the status switch in `Popconfirm`; confirmation copy and buttons use the announcement title plus enable/disable action, and the switch is disabled while the update is in progress.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 05:20 UTC targeted scan confirmed `toggleStatus(record, status !== 'ACTIVE')` is now called from `Popconfirm.onConfirm` and cancel/confirm buttons expose target labels. `git diff --check` passed for touched source files. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, Gradle, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish/cleanup, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F678: Product Q&A frontend sanitizer and length cap diverge from backend contract

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product detail and API client / Product Q&A data contract
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 03:45 UTC API/data consistency static regression found that F675 aligned ordinary whitespace normalization, but ProductDetail still did not strip ASCII control characters while `frontend/src/api/index.ts` and `ProductQuestionServiceImpl` do. The API client also sent `normalizeTextParam(question, 1000)` while backend default `product-question.max-question-chars` is `500`, and the ProductDetail `TextArea` had no `maxLength`.
- Expected: ProductDetail pending text, API payload normalization, and backend default limits should use the same control-character handling and question length cap so pending-state comparison and validation feedback remain consistent.
- Actual: Extreme pasted control-character input could still leave pending text mismatched against backend-returned public Q&A text, and users could enter/send more than the backend's default question limit before receiving a server-side error.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 03:45 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx` and `frontend/src/api/index.ts`. ProductDetail now uses `PRODUCT_QUESTION_MAX_LENGTH=500`, strips ASCII control characters before whitespace collapse, and adds `maxLength`/`showCount` to the question `TextArea`. The API client now uses `MAX_PRODUCT_QUESTION_LENGTH=500` for `questionApi.ask`. Preserved login gating, pending save/clear behavior, cache clearing, and existing API route.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 03:45 UTC `git diff --check -- frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx frontend/src/api/index.ts` passed. Targeted source scans confirmed `stripQuestionControlChars`, `PRODUCT_QUESTION_MAX_LENGTH`, `MAX_PRODUCT_QUESTION_LENGTH`, `maxLength`, `showCount`, and the `normalizeTextParam(question, MAX_PRODUCT_QUESTION_LENGTH)` call. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish/cleanup, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F675: ProductDetail pending Q&A can remain stuck when backend normalizes whitespace

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product detail / Public Q&A pending state consistency
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 03:36 UTC storefront/API static regression found that the F669 pending-question cleanup path in `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx` compared answered public questions and local pending questions with `question.question.trim() === pendingQuestion.question.trim()`, while backend public Q&A can return whitespace-collapsed question text. A submitted question such as `Food   allergy?` could be stored or returned as `Food allergy?`, leaving the local pending item visible after the answered public question loads.
- Expected: Pending Q&A save, duplicate prevention, and answered-question reconciliation should use the same whitespace normalization as the backend-visible public question text.
- Actual: The frontend used `trim()` only, so internal whitespace differences could prevent local pending questions from clearing after merchant reply/publication.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 03:36 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx`. Added `normalizeQuestionText(value)` using `trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')` and applied it to empty-question validation, submitted pending text, duplicate pending filtering, and answered-question reconciliation. Preserved login gating, API submission, pending list cap, success/error handling, and public answered-question rendering.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 03:36 UTC targeted source scan confirmed `normalizeQuestionText` is defined and used for answered/pending comparison, empty validation, submission, and pending duplicate filtering. `git diff --check` passed for ProductDetail, Checkout, locales, and this issue log after update. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish/cleanup, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F673: Android release script can publish package versionCode that disagrees with release metadata

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Mobile App / Android release pipeline / Version metadata consistency
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 03:06 UTC delegated release QA found that existing public/debug APK artifacts report Android package `versionCode=23`, while `frontend/public/downloads/mobile-version.json`, `frontend/src/generated/mobileRelease.ts`, and runtime config use `versionCode=10023` for `versionName=1.0.23`. Static source review of `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-android-apk.js` showed the release path derived Gradle package `nextVersionCode` by incrementing the fallback integer, while `prepareReleaseStage()` still generated frontend release metadata with `toMobileVersionCode(versionName)`. A future release from `1.0.23` would therefore package Android `versionCode=24` but publish metadata `versionCode=10024`.
- Expected: Android package `versionCode`, WebView runtime current version, `mobile-version.json`, and `mobileRelease.ts` should use one version-code convention for the same `versionName` so native update comparisons and public release metadata cannot diverge.
- Actual: The mobile Gradle fallback used `versionCode 23` for `versionName 1.0.23`, and the release bump script mixed patch-like package codes with semantic manifest codes.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 03:11 UTC in `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/android/app/build.gradle` and `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-android-apk.js`. Current Gradle fallback now uses `versionCode 10023` for `versionName 1.0.23`. The release script now increments the semantic version name first and derives the Android package `versionCode` from that name via the same `major*10000+minor*100+patch` convention used by the frontend release manifest generator. The next release after `1.0.23` will use `1.0.24` with Android/package/manifest `versionCode=10024`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 03:11 UTC static source reads confirmed `android/app/build.gradle` now has `versionCode mobileVersionCode ? mobileVersionCode.toInteger() : 10023`, and `scripts/build-android-apk.js` now has `getNextVersionName(currentVersionName)`, `toMobileVersionCode(nextVersionName, currentVersionCode + 1)`, and `SHOPTEST_MOBILE_ANDROID_VERSION_CODE: String(nextVersionCode)`. No build, Gradle, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact cleanup/publish, or commit was performed per current instruction. **F334 remains OPEN** because existing public APK files are still debug-signed and the webroot manifest still exposes APK URLs while `releaseSigned:false`.

### F669: ProductDetail question submission does not leave a durable pending-reply state

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product detail / Product Q&A shopper feedback
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 02:42 UTC delegated buyer-journey static QA reported that ProductDetail Q&A submission clears the question input, refetches public Q&A, and shows only a transient toast. Backend source confirms `ProductQuestionServiceImpl#getPublicByProductId` calls `findAnsweredByProductId`, and `ProductQuestionRepository#findAnsweredByProductId` filters to answered questions only, so a newly submitted unanswered question cannot appear in the public list.
- Expected: After a shopper submits a product question, the product detail page should keep a visible confirmation or pending state explaining that the question is awaiting merchant reply. The input and submit action should also expose product-aware target context.
- Actual: The question was posted successfully, but the page immediately cleared the input and refreshed a public answered-only list. Once the toast disappeared, the shopper had no durable on-page proof that the question was queued.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 02:45 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css`, and `frontend/src/locales/{en,zh,es}.json`. ProductDetail now stores a local pending-question item after a successful POST, renders a visible pending-reply alert and pending question card, clears matching pending entries when answered public Q&A returns, resets pending state when navigating to another product, and adds product-aware labels to the question input and submit button. Preserved the backend public answered-only Q&A contract, existing question API call, login guard, empty-question validation, and public Q&A rendering.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 02:45 UTC `git diff --check` passed for ProductDetail and locale files. Targeted scans confirmed `PendingProductQuestion`, `pendingQuestions`, pending Q&A locale keys/classes, `questionInputLabel`, `questionSubmitActionLabel`, and the `questionApi.ask` pending-state update are present. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, Maven, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F664: ProductDetail option Radio.Group received unsupported title prop

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product detail / Ant Design prop compatibility
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 01:08 UTC regression run #186 reported frontend build failure `TS2322: Property 'title' does not exist on type 'IntrinsicAttributes & RadioGroupProps'` at `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:1302`. The failing prop was introduced while adding product/option target context to ProductDetail option groups.
- Expected: ProductDetail option controls should keep target-aware context for assistive users and hover inspection, but only pass props supported by Ant Design component types so the frontend build remains green.
- Actual: The option `Radio.Group` had both `aria-label` and `title`. `aria-label` is accepted for the intended accessibility context, but `RadioGroupProps` does not include `title`, so TypeScript rejected the page.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 01:08 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx`. Moved the `title` and wrapper-level group semantics to the surrounding `div role="group"` and kept the supported `aria-label` on `Radio.Group`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 01:08 UTC targeted multiline scan found zero `Radio.Group` blocks with `title=` under `frontend/src`; `git diff --check` passed for `ProductDetail.tsx` and this issue log before the log update; local Ant Design type inspection confirmed `RadioGroupProps` lacks `title`. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish, or commit was performed per current instruction.

### F658: Default Modal footer buttons expose generic OK/Cancel controls

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Profile, order operations, admin management, and product quick-add / Modal footer accessibility
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: 2026-06-03 00:30 UTC static mobile UI/accessibility review found ordinary `<Modal>` instances using Ant Design default footers without complete target-aware button props. Affected flows included IP blacklist manual block, order tracking return request and return-shipment submission, admin order shipment/refund/batch shipment, product-question answer, review reply, product-list quick add, logistics carrier editor, and Profile edit-profile/change-password/address/pet/after-sales modals. These were distinct from `Modal.confirm` and Popconfirm fixes because they were normal form/editor modals with default OK/Cancel footers.
- Expected: Default modal footers should expose the object and action being saved, submitted, refunded, shipped, answered, replied to, or cancelled. Disabled/loading states should be preserved, but the accessible control name should not be a detached generic OK/Cancel inside repeated order, profile, product, or admin workflows.
- Actual: The visible form content and business logic were correct, but several modal footer buttons were still generic or only carried disabled/loading props. This weakened keyboard, screen-reader, and compact mobile clarity for repeated commercial operations such as refunding orders, submitting return tracking, answering product questions, or editing profile assets.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-03 00:30 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/IpBlacklistManagement.tsx`, `OrderTracking.tsx`, `OrderManagement.tsx`, `ProductQuestionManagement.tsx`, `ReviewManagement.tsx`, `ProductList.tsx`, `LogisticsCarrierManagement.tsx`, and `Profile.tsx`. Added target-aware `okButtonProps` and `cancelButtonProps`, added explicit save/submit/action button text where default OK was ambiguous, and preserved existing API calls, validation, disabled/loading states, permission checks, and modal lifecycle behavior.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 00:30 UTC full `frontend/src` block-level source scan confirmed every non-custom-footer, non-`footer={null}` `<Modal>` now includes both `okButtonProps` and `cancelButtonProps`; deprecated AntD prop scan found zero `popupClassName`, `destroyOnClose`, `visible`, `onVisibleChange`, `bodyStyle`, `maskStyle`, `dropdownRender`, or `destroyPopupOnHide` matches under `frontend/src`; `git diff --check` passed for the touched files and this issue log. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish, or commit was performed per current instruction. **F334** remains OPEN for release-signed APK/public artifact readiness.

### F654: ProductList preview Modal still uses deprecated destroyOnClose

- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 23:44 UTC by replacing `destroyOnClose` with `destroyOnHidden` in `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx`, preserving the same unmount-on-close behavior for preview content.

### F653: Native non-submit buttons omit explicit button type

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Storefront and Admin UI / Form safety and interaction semantics
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: Static UI/form-safety review found native `<button>` elements across storefront navigation/home/product/cart-style flows, checkout/payment selection, customer support, profile/order filters, notification/history/wishlist controls, product/admin operation cards, admin dashboard, and audit-log metric filters that did not explicitly declare a `type` attribute.
- Expected: Native buttons that are not intended to submit a form should declare `type="button"` so their behavior stays stable when rendered inside forms, modals, popovers, or refactored card/form containers.
- Actual: Browser defaults make `<button>` behave as `type="submit"` in form contexts. Most current instances were not actively submitting because of surrounding layout, but the omission created avoidable accidental-submit risk for commercial checkout/profile/admin form surfaces.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 23:39 UTC by adding `type="button"` to all native non-submit buttons under `frontend/src`, preserving existing click handlers, labels, active states, disabled states, and layout. Existing intentional Ant Design submit buttons with `htmlType="submit"` were not changed.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 23:39 UTC multiline PCRE source scan found zero remaining native `<button>` tags without a `type` attribute under `frontend/src`, duplicate-type scan found zero duplicates, and `git diff --check` passed for the touched button files. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish, or commit was performed per current instruction. **F334** remains OPEN for release-signed APK/public artifact readiness.

### F652: Product-detail quantity value is rendered as a no-op button

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Product detail / Shopper purchase controls and accessibility
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: Static storefront shopper/UI review found the product-detail quantity selector rendered the current numeric quantity as `<Button className="product-quantity__value">{quantity}</Button>` between the decrement and increment buttons. The middle control had no `onClick`, no value-edit behavior, and no contextual accessible name.
- Expected: The quantity stepper should expose only actionable controls as buttons, while the current quantity should be announced as a read-only value/status. Increment and decrement controls should have stable labels that include the current quantity context.
- Actual: Keyboard and assistive-technology users could focus a button-looking element that performed no action, weakening purchase-control clarity and creating a small but real UI polish gap on a primary commercial flow.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 23:34 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx` by replacing the no-op quantity `Button` with a read-only `<span role="status" aria-live="polite">`, changing decrement/increment to icon buttons with localized `aria-label`/`title` values, and keeping the stepper as a labeled quantity group. Updated `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css` so the read-only value keeps the same compact/mobile sizing and pill styling. Added `quantityValue`, `decreaseQuantity`, and `increaseQuantity` translations in English, Chinese, and Spanish.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 23:34 UTC static/source verification confirmed the new quantity labels, status markup, and icon controls are present. `git diff --check` passed for the touched ProductDetail and locale files. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish, or commit was performed per current instruction. **F334** remains OPEN for release-signed APK/public artifact readiness.

### F651: Popconfirm mobile popup styling depends on deprecated/local compatibility props

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Storefront confirmation overlays / AntD compatibility and mobile UI
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: Static follow-up after **F650** found remaining Popconfirm components still using `popupClassName="shop-mobile-popup-layer ..."`, and `frontend/src/antd-popconfirm-compat.d.ts` locally augmented `PopconfirmProps` to accept that legacy prop. These Popconfirm usages were intentionally outside the earlier Select/Picker deprecation cleanup, but they still left the mobile confirmation overlay styling dependent on a brittle local compatibility shim.
- Expected: Popconfirm overlays should use Ant Design's official `classNames.root` API while preserving the existing overlay classes needed for mobile touch-target styling and native Android Back popup detection.
- Actual: The visible confirmation UI worked, but styling and type compatibility relied on a legacy prop plus local type augmentation, increasing future Ant Design upgrade risk and weakening commercial-readiness console/type hygiene.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 23:27 UTC by migrating Popconfirm popup class usage in cart drawer, cart page, home, wishlist, product compare, stock alerts, browsing history, profile, notifications, and pet gallery from `popupClassName` to `classNames={{ root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer ...' }}`. Removed the now-unneeded `frontend/src/antd-popconfirm-compat.d.ts` shim.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 23:27 UTC static/source verification found zero `popupClassName` or `antd-popconfirm-compat` matches under `frontend/src`; `git diff --check` passed for the touched Popconfirm files before this issue-log update. No build, Jest, Playwright/E2E, smoke/curl, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, public artifact publish, or commit was performed per current instruction. **F334** remains OPEN for release-signed APK/public artifact readiness, and the backend Maven failure remains the known environment/ownership issue around `V4__user_password_changed_at.sql`.

### F650: Non-Navbar Select and Picker controls still emit Ant Design popup deprecation warnings

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Storefront and Admin UI / Component compatibility and console quality
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 23:16 UTC no-screenshot public Playwright regression after the Navbar fix still captured Ant Design warnings: `[antd: Select] popupClassName is deprecated. Please use classNames.popup.root instead.` Static review found deprecated popup props on Select, Cascader, TreeSelect, DatePicker, and RangePicker controls across storefront product list, Pet Finder, coupon center, checkout, profile, customer support, rich-detail editor, user/admin operation pages, audit/log pages, and moderation pages. Navbar-specific `popupClassName="shop-nav__select-popup"` was already fixed in F649, so this was a separate all-site compatibility gap.
- Expected: Select/Picker-style controls should use the current Ant Design `classNames.popup.root` API so storefront and admin smoke tests do not carry avoidable deprecation warnings.
- Actual: The visible controls worked, but repeated runtime warnings remained on public and admin routes, weakening console-based smoke tests and increasing future Ant Design upgrade risk.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 23:16 UTC by replacing Select/Cascader/TreeSelect/DatePicker/RangePicker `popupClassName="shop-mobile-popup-layer"` with `classNames={{ popup: { root: 'shop-mobile-popup-layer' } }}` in the affected storefront, profile/checkout/support, review/editor, and admin management files. Existing Popconfirm popup classes were intentionally left in place because they are a different component surface and were not the Select/Picker warning source in this regression.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 23:16 UTC targeted scans found zero remaining Select/Cascader/TreeSelect/DatePicker/RangePicker `popupClassName` usage and zero `popupClassName="shop-mobile-popup-layer"` matches outside Popconfirm-specific surfaces. `git diff --check` passed for the touched files. `npm run build` passed with only the existing Browserslist warning. After restarting the frontend dev server, curl smoke passed for `/api/products`, `/`, and backend `/actuator/health`. No-screenshot Playwright public regression passed 11 routes across desktop/mobile with route render, no horizontal overflow, product-detail Compare, order-tracking persistent-error, and no Select popup warning checks. No-screenshot Playwright admin fixture regression passed user-role confirmation, user health grid, permission controls, product-admin controls, and no Select popup warning checks. Frontend Jest passed 42/42 suites and 218/218 tests. No screenshot, video, trace, commit, release APK generation, or public artifact publish was performed.

### F648: Local CRA dev proxy does not match production `/api` prefix stripping

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Local development proxy / API smoke reliability
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC local smoke showed `http://localhost:3000/api/products` did not reliably return backend product JSON with production-equivalent routing. The simple CRA proxy path forwarded `/api/products` to the backend as `/api/products`, while the production Nginx/gateway path strips the `/api` prefix before reaching backend routes such as `/products`.
- Expected: Local frontend smoke and E2E should be able to call same-origin `/api/*` and receive the same backend route semantics as production.
- Actual: Local `/api/*` smoke could receive frontend/incorrect-route behavior, forcing tests to route around the app and weakening dev-service confidence.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC by adding `frontend/src/setupProxy.js` with `http-proxy-middleware`: `/api` now proxies to the backend target and rewrites `^/api` to an empty prefix; `/ws` proxies to the backend with WebSocket support. Removed the ineffective `package.json` proxy field so the explicit setup file is authoritative.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC after restarting the frontend dev server, `curl http://localhost:3000/api/products` returned HTTP 200 with `application/json;charset=UTF-8`, and `curl http://localhost:3000/products/9210` returned the SPA HTML. `npm run build` passed; no screenshot, video, or trace was produced.

### F647: Permission and product admin controls lack contextual labels and confirmations

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Admin Permission and Product Management / Accessibility and destructive-operation clarity
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC admin-operations review found `frontend/src/pages/PermissionManagement.tsx` and `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx` still had repeated toolbar controls, modal fields, row actions, import/export commands, and approval/rejection confirmations that relied on visible layout, placeholder text, or generic button copy instead of stable page/target context.

- Actual: The pages were visually usable, but repeated admin controls were weaker for keyboard/screen-reader navigation and reliable E2E selectors, and several approval/rejection confirmations did not name the target action at the confirmation-button level.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC. `PermissionManagement.tsx` now labels search/new/export actions, row edit actions, modal OK/Cancel buttons, modal fields, and wraps the permission checkbox group in an outer `role="group"` for ARIA context. `ProductManagement.tsx` now uses current `classNames` Select/Picker popup props, labels toolbar search/category/status and import/export/add/refresh commands, adds contextual Popconfirm OK/Cancel labels for row and batch approve/reject/review/delete paths, and labels product and URL-import modal fields/actions.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC `git diff --check` passed for the touched admin files, targeted scans confirmed no remaining targeted deprecated `popupClassName="shop-mobile-popup-layer"`, and `npm run build` passed. No screenshot, video, trace, or commit was produced.

### F646: User role changes apply immediately and repeated user controls lack target context

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Admin User Management / Role-change safety and accessibility
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC admin-operations review found role-code changes in `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` were submitted directly from the role Select `onChange`, without a confirmation step. The same page also had repeated toolbar filters, row role/status/delete controls, profile modal fields/actions, and mobile health metrics that depended on nearby layout or horizontal scrolling for context.
- Expected: Changing a user's role should require explicit confirmation, repeated user-management controls should identify the target user/filter/field/action, and mobile metrics should remain visible without forcing a horizontal-scroll inspection pattern.
- Actual: A misclick in the role Select could immediately change role state, and repeated controls were harder to distinguish in keyboard/screen-reader and E2E regression flows.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC in `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` and `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.css`. Role code changes now route through `confirmRoleCodeChange(...)` and `Modal.confirm`; toolbar filters/actions, row role Selects, ban/unban/delete confirmation buttons, profile modal OK/Cancel actions, and modal fields now carry contextual labels/titles. Mobile health metrics now use a visible two-column grid instead of the previous horizontal-scroll pattern.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 22:53 UTC targeted scans confirmed the direct role-change handler was removed from the Select, contextual labels are present, targeted deprecated popup props are gone, and old `Progress width=` usage is replaced with current sizing. `git diff --check` and `npm run build` passed; no screenshot, video, trace, or commit was produced.

### F639: Payment modal method group used an unsupported title prop

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Payment Modal / Type safety and contextual payment labels
- Severity: HIGH
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 21:24 UTC user-requested APK packaging hit a frontend production build failure in `frontend/src/components/Payment.tsx`: `TS2322` reported that `title` does not exist on Ant Design `RadioGroupProps`. The unsupported prop came from the F637 contextual-label change.
- Expected: Payment-modal accessibility context should be implemented with props supported by Ant Design component types, so production builds and APK packaging are not blocked.
- Actual: The payment method group had a useful contextual `aria-label`, but also carried an unsupported `title` prop on `Radio.Group`, making the production TypeScript build fail.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 21:24 UTC. Removed the unsupported `title` from `Radio.Group` while retaining the group `aria-label`; method options and the confirm action still expose order, amount, and selected payment-channel context through supported labels/titles.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 21:30 UTC `git diff --check -- frontend/src/components/Payment.tsx frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.tsx TEST_ISSUES.md` passed, and targeted source inspection confirmed the `Radio.Group` no longer has a `title` prop. The user-requested debug APK packaging completed after the fix; no additional build/test/E2E/screenshot/restart/commit was run after resuming the no-build goal.

### F634: Storefront mobile action fit and contrast guards are incomplete

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Home, Product List, Pet Finder, and Product Detail / Mobile layout and visual accessibility
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 20:53 UTC static source review found the Pet Finder mobile next-step action row had a later cascade block forcing `.pet-finder-page__nextStepActions` into no-wrap horizontal flex scrolling, with each `.ant-space-item` fixed at `min(190px, 62vw)`. That could push the second action outside a 390px mobile viewport when the container has page/card padding. The same pass found several customer-visible conversion surfaces still relying on low-contrast shallow orange or default Ant Design secondary text: Pet Finder's product price used inline `#ee4d2d`, Pet Finder primary buttons/green tags used low-contrast default tones, Home editorial primary CTAs and header action chip needed stronger foreground/background contrast, Product List mobile active discovery/price/action states reused shallow orange, and Product Detail brand/readiness support text stayed in weak secondary styling.
- Expected: Core customer storefront pages should keep action controls inside the viewport on mobile and maintain readable contrast for secondary text, price text, tags, and primary conversion buttons without depending on runtime contrast repair.
- Actual: The Pet Finder next-action row could horizontally overflow on narrow devices, and several storefront support/conversion text surfaces had insufficient static contrast for commercial-readiness UI.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 20:53 UTC. `frontend/src/pages/PetFinder.css` now overrides the final mobile next-step action row to a responsive grid with contained `.ant-space-item` and button widths, falling to one column on very narrow screens. `frontend/src/pages/PetFinder.tsx` moved the product price from an inline shallow-orange style to `.pet-finder-page__productPrice`. Scoped contrast guards were added to `PetFinder.css`, `Home.css`, `ProductList.css`, and `ProductDetail.css` for secondary copy, price text, active mobile controls, green tags, editorial/product primary CTAs, and product-detail readiness/brand text.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 20:53 UTC `git diff --check -- frontend/src/pages/PetFinder.tsx frontend/src/pages/PetFinder.css frontend/src/pages/Home.css frontend/src/pages/ProductList.css frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.css` passed. Targeted source scans confirmed the Pet Finder inline `#ee4d2d` price style was removed and the new mobile action/contrast guard selectors are present. No automated tests, build, smoke/E2E, screenshot, video, trace, service restart, APK generation, or commit was run in this pass per current instruction.

### F592: Filter and status summary controls rely on visual active styles without full state semantics

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Status cards, quick filters, product listing quality filters, and profile order filters / Accessibility and navigation behavior
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: 2026-06-02 15:54 UTC static source review found several repeated filter/status controls communicating selection only through `is-active` or `--active` classes. Admin alert summary `Card` controls were clickable and keyboard-enabled but lacked explicit button role, pressed state, and target labels. Notification, browsing-history, product listing quality, profile mobile-entry, and profile order filter buttons lacked `aria-pressed`. Product admin featured star toggles had active styling but no switch/toggle state. Profile after-sale `Need shipment` metric displayed the `RETURN_APPROVED` count but clicked into the broader `AFTER_SALE` filter, so the visible metric and resulting order list did not match.
- Expected: Filter/status/toggle controls should expose selected state programmatically, keep stable count/context labels for assistive technology and E2E selectors, and drill down to the same scope shown by the metric.
- Actual: Several controls relied on color/active styling alone, and the profile after-sale need-shipment metric opened a broader result set than its count represented.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 15:54 UTC. Added `role="button"`, `aria-pressed`, and contextual labels to clickable alert status cards; added `aria-pressed` and count/context labels to notification, browsing history, product listing quality, profile mobile-entry, profile after-sale, and profile order filter controls; added pressed state to product featured star toggles; added active styling for profile after-sale metric buttons; and routed the need-shipment metric through a precise `RETURN_APPROVED` order filter.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 15:54 UTC `git diff --check -- frontend/src/pages/AlertManagement.tsx frontend/src/pages/Notifications.tsx frontend/src/pages/BrowsingHistory.tsx frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx frontend/src/pages/Profile.css` passed. Targeted source scans confirmed the reviewed active/status controls are now paired with `aria-pressed` or explicit role/button semantics. No Jest, production build, E2E, smoke test, server restart, APK generation, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was run in this pass per current instruction.

### F587: Storefront product localization can leave blank product names across cards

Several storefront surfaces render localized `product.name` directly, including Home product cards, ProductList grid cards, checkout-path buttons, product detail recommendations, compare, browsing history, pet finder, stock alerts, and cart add-ons. A blank backend product name could therefore produce empty visible product titles, empty image `alt` values, and action labels such as `View pick:` with no product context.

- Actual: The shared localization helper preserved blank product names, leaving each consuming card/action to handle the fallback individually.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 13:48 UTC. Added a shared localized name fallback in `localizeProduct()` for blank product names: `Product #id`, `Producto #id`, or `商品 #id` based on language. Added regression coverage in `frontend/src/utils/localizedProduct.test.ts` for English, Spanish, and Chinese blank-name behavior.
- Retest: 2026-06-02 14:28 UTC `frontend/src/utils/localizedProduct.test.ts` passed as part of the full frontend Jest run; production build and no-screenshot Playwright storefront smoke passed with product cards/details using non-empty names. No screenshots, videos, or traces were produced.

### F586: Recommendation cards can render blank product names and unnamed controls

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Cart add-on assistant and pet-personalized recommendation cards / Accessibility and product-data fallback
- Severity: LOW
- Evidence: Static source review found `AddOnAssistant.tsx` rendered recommendation image `alt`, visible recommendation name, and repeated Add buttons directly from `product.name`; `PetPersonalizedAssistant.tsx` rendered image `alt`, media/name buttons, visible recommendation name, and quick-add/select-options actions from `product.name`. Other cart/order/support surfaces already fall back to `pages.profile.productFallback` when backend product names are blank, but these recommendation widgets did not, so a blank or whitespace backend product name could create an unnamed image, an empty product-name button, and repeated Add/quick-add controls that were hard to distinguish with assistive technology.
- Expected: Recommendation cards should always expose a localized, non-empty product display name and action name, even when backend product data is incomplete.
- Actual: Add-on and pet-personalized recommendation cards trusted `product.name` for visible text and accessibility labels.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 07:19 UTC. Both components now compute `productName = product.name.trim() || t('pages.profile.productFallback', { id })` inside each card and use that value for image `alt`, visible names, media/product button labels, and add/select action labels/titles. This keeps customer-facing recommendation cards named and keyboard/screen-reader friendly even with incomplete product records.
- Retest: `git diff --check -- frontend/src/components/AddOnAssistant.tsx frontend/src/components/PetPersonalizedAssistant.tsx` passed. `npm run build` in `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend` completed successfully; the only emitted warnings were existing Browserslist age notices plus existing ESLint warnings in `Navbar.tsx` and `ProductList.tsx`.

### F585: Forgot-password App header text is white on a light auth background before contrast guard recovery

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Android App / WebView auth flow / Forgot password UI contrast
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: Static source/color review only. `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx:135-140` renders `/forgot-password` as `<main className="shopee-login-root shopee-login-root--reset">` with `<section className="shopee-login-card shopee-login-card--reset">` and direct brand text (`.shopee-login-mark`, `.shopee-login-subtitle`), but it does not include the dark `.shopee-login-card__header` wrapper used by `/login`. In the native App stylesheet, `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/mobile-app.css:469-475` gives `.shopee-login-root` a light background (`#fff7f0` to `#f4f7f5` to `#eaf4ef`), `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/mobile-app.css:488-496` makes `.shopee-login-card` transparent, and `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/mobile-app.css:590-593` forces `.shopee-login-mark`, `.shopee-login-subtitle`, and `.shopee-login-card__intro` to `#ffffff !important`. The static contrast ratios are approximately `#ffffff` on `#fff7f0` = 1.06:1, on `#f4f7f5` = 1.08:1, and on `#eaf4ef` = 1.12:1. Reset-page-specific CSS at `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/Login.css:1716-1723` only adjusts brand layout, and `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/Login.css:2439-2464` only adjusts reset spacing/card width; no dark background or dark text override is present.
- Expected: In the Android native WebView, `/forgot-password` should render the ShopMX brand and reset subtitle with readable foreground/background contrast from the static stylesheet itself, at least 4.5:1 for normal subtitle text, without relying on delayed JavaScript post-processing.
- Actual: The reset page can initially render white brand/subtitle text on the light App auth background/card area. The broad F584 runtime scanner can later mark low-contrast text with `data-shop-contrast-text="dark"`, but that is asynchronous (`refreshMobileContrastGuard()` schedules a scan after route/render) and the static CSS remains a confirmed low-contrast state.
- Repro/Notes: Static repro path: open the Android App WebView route `/forgot-password`. Compare it with `/login`, where `/root/shoptest/frontend/src/mobile-app.css:577-587` places `.shopee-login-card__header` on a dark image/green overlay before the same white text rules apply. The forgot-password route omits that header wrapper, so the white text rule applies to brand/subtitle text sitting on the light native auth background. No build, emulator, UI smoke, screenshot, generated asset sync, code change, or commit was performed.
- Impact: Users entering the password-reset flow may see the page title/brand area nearly invisible during initial render or if the runtime contrast scanner is delayed/disabled. This is especially risky on Android WebView route transitions, low-end devices, or immediately after opening the reset page from Login.
- Fix recommendation: Add a static native App reset-page override, for example scope `.shopee-login-card--reset .shopee-login-mark` and `.shopee-login-card--reset .shopee-login-subtitle` to dark readable text, or wrap the reset brand in the same dark `.shopee-login-card__header` treatment as `/login`. Keep the runtime contrast guard as a fallback, not the primary contrast source for this header.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 03:25 UTC in `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/mobile-app.css`. The native App reset route now gives `.shopee-login-root--reset .shopee-login-brand` a bordered white panel, forces `.shopee-login-mark` to `#173f2b`, and forces `.shopee-login-subtitle` to `#3f5147`, so `/forgot-password` has readable static header contrast before any runtime contrast scan runs.

### F583: Android Back does not dismiss Ant Design Modal.confirm dialogs

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Android App / Native Back navigation / Checkout and order confirmation dialogs
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: Static source review only. `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/utils/nativeBack.ts:44-52` implements the generic overlay fallback by clicking visible `.ant-modal-close` or `.ant-drawer-close` buttons, and `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/utils/nativeBack.ts:103-129` only checks popup layers before that overlay fallback. Ant Design `Modal.confirm` dialogs do not render an `.ant-modal-close` button and are not covered by the popup selector. Customer paths still use `Modal.confirm(...)`: checkout pending-payment rollback at `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:997-1005`, order-tracking cancel-payment rollback and confirm-receipt dialogs at `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx:328-375`, and profile confirm-receipt at `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:550-557`. Mobile admin flows also use the same pattern, for example coupon grants at `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx:325-330`, order status transitions at `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx:819-859`, and product import confirmation at `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx:1488-1502`.
- Expected: In the Android native WebView shell, pressing hardware Back while a `Modal.confirm` dialog is visible should cancel or close the top confirmation dialog and consume the Back event. It should not navigate away, return home, or minimize the app while the destructive/transactional confirmation is still visible.
- Actual: `consumeNativeBack()` does not identify visible `.ant-modal-confirm` dialogs. With no explicit native Back handler registered for these confirm instances, Back falls through to route-stack navigation, home replacement, or app minimize/exit after `consumeAntDesignOverlayBack()` returns false.
- Repro/Notes: Static repro path: in the Android App, open checkout after creating a pending payment and tap the rollback/cancel-payment action, or open Order Tracking/Profile for a shipped order and tap Confirm receipt. When the Ant Design confirmation dialog is visible, press Android Back. Expected the dialog closes/cancels first; actual source flow has no `Modal.confirm` close/cancel branch and can navigate/minimize instead. No npm build, Gradle task, Maven/Jest test, generated asset sync, APK inspection, emulator/UI smoke, screenshot, source change, or commit was run. This is not a duplicate of **F575**, which fixed `.ant-popover`/Popconfirm handling, and not **F498**, which only covered mobile confirmation button touch targets.
- Impact: Android App users can lose the current checkout/order context or send the app to the background while a high-impact confirmation is open, leaving transactional state unclear and making destructive or order-completion actions harder to safely cancel.
- Fix recommendation: Extend `consumeNativeBack()` with a `Modal.confirm` branch before route navigation, targeting visible `.ant-modal-confirm` dialogs and clicking the visible cancel button (`.ant-modal-confirm-btns .ant-btn:not(.ant-btn-primary)`) when present, otherwise dispatch Escape or close the top confirm through Ant Design's destroy/update API. Keep the existing Popconfirm/Select/Dropdown and ordinary Modal/Drawer priority order.
- Resolution: Fixed 2026-06-02 03:25 UTC in `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/utils/nativeBack.ts`. `consumeNativeBack()` now checks visible Ant Design `.ant-modal-confirm` dialogs after popups and before generic Modal/Drawer close-button fallback, clicks only a visible non-primary cancel button when available, and otherwise dispatches Escape while consuming Back so Android hardware Back cannot route/minimize past a visible confirmation dialog or accidentally trigger the primary confirm action.

### F582: Android release first pass can leave bootstrap mobile metadata after early failure

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Android App / release pipeline / WebView asset bootstrap rollback
- Severity: MEDIUM
- Evidence: Static source/download-directory review only. In `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-android-apk.js`, public release signing is validated and the in-memory Android version bump is prepared at lines 481-489, then the first `syncAndroid()` and first `assembleAndroidApk()` are called at lines 678-679. The rollback helper for `frontend/src/generated/mobileRelease.ts` is not created until line 687, after those early steps have already completed. During that first `syncAndroid()`, `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-web.js` lines 41-50 set `MOBILE_RELEASE_ALLOW_EMPTY_APK=true` plus Android version metadata when no staged APK exists. The frontend prebuild generator then writes no-APK bootstrap output in `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/scripts/generate-mobile-version.js` lines 180-197, with blank APK URL/file/hash/certificate fields and `releaseSigned=false`. If the first WebView sync, stale-asset guard, Capacitor sync, or first Gradle assemble/certificate check fails before line 687, there is no snapshot/restore path for the generated release source or the public/build download manifests produced by that bootstrap build.
