- Evidence: Static source review only. No release build, Gradle task, keytool/apksigner artifact inspection, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was performed.
- Severity: Medium
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:49 UTC static source review confirmed `assembleAndroidApk()` now calls `verifyReleaseApkCertificate(apkSource)` for configured release builds. The verifier reads the built APK certificate with Android SDK `apksigner --print-certs`, falls back to `keytool -printcert -jarfile`, rejects the Android Debug certificate, and requires the actual APK certificate SHA-256 to match `SHOPTEST_MOBILE_ANDROID_CERTIFICATE_SHA256` before staging or publishing. `node --check scripts/build-android-apk.js` and targeted signing/publish scans passed; no release build or artifact inspection was run per instruction.

### F559: Native update flow accepts signed manifests without explicit APK artifact metadata

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Android App release quality / native update prompt and download metadata
- Environment: `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/src/utils/mobileUpdate.ts`, native App update gate, storefront Android download resolver, `/downloads/mobile-version.json`
- Steps: Static review the release-manifest normalizer and download resolver. Consider a future manifest with the expected Android platform/app id, positive `versionCode`, `releaseSigned: true`, and a valid non-debug `certificateSha256`, but missing or blank `apkUrl`, `fileName`, `sizeBytes`, and `sha256`.
- Expected: A production native update/download surface should fail closed unless the release manifest explicitly names the APK artifact and includes immutable artifact metadata such as positive size and a 64-character APK SHA-256. It should not guess a downloadable APK URL when the signed release manifest is incomplete.
- Actual: `normalizeReleaseManifest(...)` derives `apkUrl` from `source.apkUrl || buildVersionedAndroidApkUrl(versionName) || legacyApkUrl`, while `isMobileReleaseDownloadAllowed(...)` only requires `releaseSigned === true` and a valid non-debug certificate fingerprint. `resolveMobileReleaseDownloadUrl(...)` repeats the same fallback to `/downloads/shoptest-{versionName}.apk` or the legacy APK URL. A signed-but-incomplete manifest can therefore still drive the update prompt/download URL even though no artifact URL/hash/size was explicitly published. Current deployed manifests are unsigned, so this is not prompting users today.
- Evidence: Static source review only: `mobileUpdate.ts` lines 213-226 and 244-260. No manifest mutation, runtime App check, build, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or source change was run in this QA pass.
- Impact: A release metadata mistake can send Android App users to a stale, missing, or mismatched APK path without a manifest-level SHA/size guard. This weakens the release/download quality gate even after F334/F422 certificate gating.
- Suggested fix: For release-signed manifests, require explicit `apkUrl`, `fileName`, positive `sizeBytes`, and a normalized 64-character APK `sha256` before allowing any native update or storefront download link. Remove guessed APK URL fallback for production release manifests, or limit it to clearly separated local debug/dev mode.
- Severity: Medium
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:44 UTC static source review confirmed `isMobileReleaseDownloadAllowed(...)` now requires `releaseSigned=true`, a valid non-debug release certificate fingerprint, safe explicit `apkUrl`, safe APK `fileName`, positive `sizeBytes`, and a normalized 64-character APK `sha256`. `resolveMobileReleaseDownloadUrl(...)` now uses only the explicit manifest `apkUrl` after that gate and no longer guesses a versioned or legacy APK URL for signed releases; `normalizeReleaseManifest(...)` no longer synthesizes `apkUrl` from the version. No manifest mutation, runtime App check, build, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was run per instruction.

### F558: Android Cordova allowlist permits all origins in the packaged native shell

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Android App WebView/Cordova security configuration
- Environment: `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/android/app/src/main/res/xml/config.xml`, `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/android/app/build.gradle`, bundled `assets/public/runtime-config.js`
- Steps: Static review the Capacitor/Cordova Android shell configuration and compare the WebView allowlist with the production runtime endpoints.
- Expected: A commercial WebView-like Android App should restrict Cordova/WebView access to the required production HTTPS/WSS origins, with localhost/debug allowances kept in debug-only configuration if needed.
- Actual: `config.xml` contains `<access origin="*" />` while the Android app includes `project(':capacitor-cordova-android-plugins')` and the bundled runtime endpoints are fixed to `https://pet.686888666.xyz/api` and `wss://pet.686888666.xyz/ws/support`. The native shell therefore keeps a broad Cordova origin allowlist that is wider than the production app requires.
- Evidence: Static source review only: `config.xml` line 3, `android/app/build.gradle` line 70, and bundled `runtime-config.js` lines 32-55. No runtime navigation attempt, emulator, build, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or source change was run in this QA pass.
- Impact: If any current or future Cordova/Capacitor plugin, navigation, or network bridge consults the Cordova allowlist, the packaged App can permit arbitrary origins rather than only the intended shop backend/origin. This increases blast radius if WebView content is redirected or injected.
- Suggested fix: Replace the wildcard with explicit production origins required by the App, and keep any local development origins in a debug-only config. Add a static release check that fails packaging when production `config.xml` contains `<access origin="*" />`.
- Severity: Medium
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:44 UTC static source review confirmed Android and iOS Cordova `config.xml` now use `<access origin="https://pet.686888666.xyz" />` and no checked native mobile config contains `<access origin="*" />`. ### F557: Android APK publish script copies public APKs before release manifest validation completes

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Android App release publishing / update manifest integrity
- Environment: `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile/scripts/build-android-apk.js`, `/home/guhao/shoptest/frontend/scripts/generate-mobile-version.js`, `frontend/public/downloads`, `frontend/build/downloads`, `/var/www/shoptest/downloads`
- Steps: Static review the Android APK release publish script and manifest generator order after the F556 debug-publish fix.
- Expected: The release pipeline should validate signing metadata, APK version metadata, SHA/size metadata, and manifest generation before copying any APK into public or live download directories. Publishing APK plus `mobile-version.json` should be staged and atomic, and old APK cleanup should happen only after the new manifest/artifact pair is ready.
- Actual: In the release-signed path, after `syncAndroid()` and `assembleAndroidApk()`, the script still calls `publishApkArtifacts()` before `publishMobileVersionManifest()`. `publishApkArtifacts()` copies `shoptest.apk` and the versioned APK into public/build/live download directories and removes retired APKs. Only after that does `publishMobileVersionManifest()` run `generate-mobile-version.js`, which can still fail on version/signing/update-policy metadata. A post-copy manifest failure can leave a public APK without the matching trusted manifest/checksum metadata, and can remove the previous versioned APK before the new release is fully valid.
- Evidence: Static source review only: `build-android-apk.js` lines 213-239 and 285-292, plus `generate-mobile-version.js` lines 23-34, 58-69, 87-102, and 104-124. Current download directories were inspected and contain only `mobile-version.json`, no APK files, so the issue is a release-pipeline risk rather than current public APK exposure. No build, release script, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or source change was run in this QA pass.
- Impact: A failed release publish can create a partial public Android release in `frontend/public`, `frontend/build`, or `/var/www`, causing stale/discoverable APK files, missing or mismatched update metadata, and a harder rollback path.
- Suggested fix: Stage the APK in a temporary/private directory, validate/generate the manifest against the staged artifact, then atomically publish the APK and manifest together. Defer retired-APK cleanup until after manifest publish succeeds, and clean up newly copied artifacts if any post-copy step fails.
- Severity: Medium
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:44 UTC static source review confirmed the release script now stages `shoptest.apk` and the versioned APK under `.release-staging`, runs `generate-mobile-version.js` against the staged versioned APK through `MOBILE_APK_PATH`/`MOBILE_APK_FILE_NAME` and staged output paths, publishes `mobileRelease.ts` before the second packaging pass, and only copies the final staged APKs plus `mobile-version.json` to public/build/live download directories after final manifest generation succeeds. Public copies use temp-file rename, retired APK cleanup runs after per-directory publish, and staging directories are removed on success or staging failure. `node --check` passed for both release scripts; no release build, Gradle task, artifact inspection, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was run per instruction.

### F409: Mobile update URL resolver accepts unsafe manifest-provided URLs

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Mobile App / update manifest and APK download URL handling
- Environment: `frontend/src/utils/mobileUpdate.ts`, native App update prompt, Navbar Android download entry, runtime-configured mobile manifest URL, and `frontend/public/downloads/mobile-version.json`
- Steps: Provide a mobile release manifest or runtime config with an unsafe `apkUrl`, `legacyApkUrl`, or manifest URL such as protocol-relative `//host/path`, URL credentials, encoded NUL/backslash payloads, a non-HTTP(S) scheme, or remote plaintext `http://` origin; then open the native App update flow or storefront Android download entry.
- Expected: Commercial App update/download code should only open a trusted HTTPS URL, a local-development HTTP URL, or a safe relative asset resolved from a trusted origin. Malformed or attacker-controlled URL shapes should be rejected before reaching `window.open`, the Capacitor Browser plugin, or a visible download link.
- Actual: The resolver previously normalized strings by trimming control characters and then passed more URL shapes through origin resolution. That left the update/download path too permissive for manifest-provided values that should never be accepted in a production mobile update surface.
- Evidence: Parallel App worker static review at 2026-06-01 06:20 UTC inspected the mobile update chain in `mobileUpdate.ts`, `App.tsx`, `Navbar.tsx`, runtime config, and the current public mobile manifest. The review identified the unsafe URL-shape gap as distinct from F334's debug-signing release issue.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 06:22 UTC static source review confirmed `normalizeMobileUrlValue(...)`, `parseSafeMobileHttpUrl(...)`, and `resolveMobileAssetUrl(...)` now reject control characters, backslashes, `%00`, `%5c`, protocol-relative URLs, URL credentials, non-HTTP(S) schemes, and non-localhost plaintext HTTP remote origins while preserving HTTPS, localhost HTTP, and safe relative-path resolution. No build, automated test, UI smoke, screenshot, or commit was run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Keep the URL allowlist strict and add regression tests around release-manifest URL normalization when tests are allowed. F334 separately requires a release-signed APK and publishing workflow.

### F343: Storefront home and product list load the full public catalog before local slicing

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: 2026-05-30 23:57 UTC static review found `frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx` lines 450-459 calling `productApi.getAll()` without `page`/`size`, saving the whole response snapshot, deriving only 12 featured products, storing all localized products, and setting the visible discovery batch size. Home lines 631-640 then slice discovery products locally and increment visible rows in `DISCOVERY_BATCH_SIZE` batches. `frontend/src/pages/ProductList.tsx` lines 638-667 call `productApi.getAll(kw, cid, disc, filters)` and store the whole localized response; lines 1391-1397 calculate local total pages and slice `sortedProducts` by `pageSize = 12`. `frontend/src/api/index.ts` lines 1167-1185 only appends `page` and `size` when callers provide them. Backend `ProductServiceImpl.java` lines 206-208 builds public catalog results from `productRepository.findAll()`, and lines 567-570 return the full list when both `page` and `size` are null. This is distinct from F235, which tracks direct `/search` no-pagination behavior; F343 tracks the visible home/product-list `/products` loading path.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:44 UTC static source review confirmed current Home requests `productApi.getAll(..., { page: 0, size: HOME_PRODUCT_PAGE_SIZE })`, ProductList uses `productApi.getPage(...)` with `page`/`size` for normal list loads, and fallback catalog requests are bounded. Follow-up fix changed backend `/products` to default omitted pagination to `page=0,size=24` and always return `ProductPublicPageResponse` from `findPublicProductPage(...)`; `productApi.getAll(...)` now accepts both legacy array and page response before normalizing to the array expected by existing callers. No build, automated test, screenshot, video, or trace was run in this pass.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:47 UTC no-media live regression was partial. Source and frontend request shape passed: Home rendered product text after scroll/wait and sent `GET /api/products?page=0&size=48` plus `GET /api/products/featured?limit=12`; ProductList rendered product text and sent `GET /api/products?sort=default&page=0&size=12`; no CRA overlay or page errors were observed. Live backend default-pagination verification remains blocked by stale `/opt/shoptest/shop.jar`: direct `GET /products` returned a 29-item legacy array with no `total/page/size`, and `GET /products?page=0&size=24` returned a 24-item legacy array with no metadata. This is not a new issue; live pass requires backend redeploy with current source. 未截图、未录屏、未生成 trace/图片.
- Expected fix direction: Make home and product-list screens request bounded public product pages or dedicated section endpoints with totals/next-page metadata. Enforce a backend default page size for omitted `page`/`size` on public product reads, and adjust snapshot/fallback behavior so normal page loads do not require downloading the entire live catalog.

### F341: GET payment history/latest endpoints mutate payment and order state

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: 2026-05-30 23:34 UTC static review found `PaymentController.java` lines 201-235 expose authenticated and guest payment history/latest GET routes under both class-level mappings (`/payments` and `/payment`) and delegate to `PaymentService.findByOrderId()` or `PaymentService.findLatestByOrderId()`. `PaymentService.java` lines 272-290 and 314-325 call `syncProviderPaymentState(...)` and `expirePayment(...)` inside those read helpers. `expirePayment()` at lines 377-385 calls `paymentRepository.markExpired(...)` and can call `orderService.cancelOrder(...)` when no active pending payment remains; `syncProviderPaymentState()` at lines 637-669 retrieves Stripe sessions, can call `paymentRepository.markPaidDetailed(...)`, and can move the order to `PENDING_SHIPMENT`.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:31 UTC static source review confirmed authenticated and guest GET routes in `PaymentController` now call `paymentService.findStoredByOrderId(...)` / `findStoredLatestByOrderId(...)`, which are read-only repository lookups, and admin `/admin/orders/{id}/payments` also uses `findStoredByOrderId(...)`. The state-changing `findByOrderId(...)` / `findLatestByOrderId(...)` methods remain available for explicit sync/expiry workflows but are no longer used by GET history/latest reads. No build, automated test, screenshot, video, or trace was run in this pass.
- Expected fix direction: Split side-effect-free payment history/latest reads from state-changing sync/expiry workflows. Keep GET endpoints on read-only repository access, and invoke provider sync/expiry only from explicit sync endpoints, callbacks/webhooks, scheduled expiry jobs, or deliberate customer/admin actions with audit coverage.

### F339: Nginx cache-control locations drop inherited security headers

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: 2026-05-30 23:17 UTC static review found `deploy/nginx/shoptest-static.conf` lines 61-66 and `deploy/nginx/shoptest-edge.conf.template` lines 63-68 declare HTTPS server-level security headers, including `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`, `Strict-Transport-Security`, and `Content-Security-Policy`. Child locations then define only `add_header Cache-Control` at `shoptest-static.conf` lines 68-70, 94-97, 101-103, 107-108, 112-114, 118-120, and 150-158, and at `shoptest-edge.conf.template` lines 70-72, 96-99, 103-105, 109-110, 114-116, 120-122, and 150-161. Because Nginx inherits parent `add_header` directives only when the child context has no `add_header` directives, representative `/healthz`, `/static/*`, denied `*.map`, `/runtime-config.js`, SPA fallback, generic asset, and `/uploads/` responses can lose the security headers while still returning cache headers.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:37 UTC static config review confirmed every HTTPS child location that declares `add_header Cache-Control` in `shoptest-static.conf` and `shoptest-edge.conf.template` now repeats `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`, `Strict-Transport-Security`, and `Content-Security-Policy`; HTTP `/healthz` repeats the core non-HSTS security headers. `git diff --check` passed for both Nginx files, and a text scan found no `Cache-Control` block missing the expected headers. No Nginx reload, live HTTP request, build, automated test, screenshot, video, or trace was run in this pass.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:44 UTC no-media curl/header regression against local Nginx was blocked by stale active config. The active `/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/shoptest` points to `/etc/nginx/sites-available/shoptest` modified `2026-05-30 02:32:59 +0000`, not the fixed repo deploy config. Curl via `--resolve pet.686888666.xyz` found HTTPS `/healthz`, `/static/css/main.24899161.css`, `/runtime-config.js`, `/favicon.ico`, denied `/static/js/main.6f32d2b3.js.map`, and `/uploads/f339-header-probe.jpg` returning Cache-Control but missing the full security header set; `/not-a-real-route` had security headers but missed Cache-Control. No Nginx reload/restart was performed, and no new product issue was added. 未截图、未录屏、未生成 trace/图片.
- Expected fix direction: Use a shared include/snippet or repeat the full security header set in every child location that declares `add_header`, or use a pinned supported Nginx inheritance mechanism. Verify representative static, runtime-config, denied-map, upload, health-check, and SPA fallback responses retain both cache and security headers.

### F338: Admin user status API can convert registered accounts into GUEST

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: 2026-05-30 22:37 UTC static review found `AdminController.normalizeUserStatus()` accepts `GUEST` and `updateUser()` persists it for non-self, non-privileged customer rows. `UserManagement.tsx` locks only the visible action for rows already normalized to `GUEST`, so direct `PUT /admin/users/{customerId}` requests can still bypass the UI and mark a registered customer as a guest identity. `UserMapper.findActiveCustomerIds()` includes only `USER` rows with null/`ACTIVE` status, and `NotificationService.broadcastToCustomers()` uses that query for customer broadcasts.
- Expected fix direction: Treat `GUEST` as a system-owned guest-checkout status in the admin API: reject non-guest-to-guest transitions through `PUT /admin/users/{id}` unless a dedicated audited migration flow exists, and enforce the same guest-status lock server-side that the UI now presents.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:53 UTC added the backend guard in `AdminController.updateUser()`. Direct requests attempting to set `status=GUEST`, or to change an existing `GUEST` account through the admin status API, now return `Guest status is system-managed` before persistence.

### F337: HTTP health check is shadowed by canonical HTTPS redirect

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: 2026-05-30 22:24 UTC static review found `deploy/nginx/shoptest-static.conf` lines 1-11 and `deploy/nginx/shoptest-edge.conf.template` lines 1-11 define `location = /healthz` in the same port-80 server block as a server-level `return 301 https://...$request_uri`. Nginx server-level return handling shadows that location, while `docs/static-frontend-nginx-deploy.md` still documents `curl -I http://127.0.0.1/healthz` and says the template returns `204 No Content`.
- Expected fix direction: Move the HTTP redirect into a `location /` block or otherwise exempt exact `/healthz` from the port-80 canonical redirect, then update docs/smoke checks so HTTP `/healthz` returns 204 and normal storefront paths still redirect to HTTPS.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:28 UTC changed both HTTP server blocks to keep `/healthz` as a location-level `204` response and moved the canonical HTTPS redirect into `location /`, so the documented HTTP health check is no longer shadowed by a server-level return.

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: 2026-05-30 22:08 UTC `unzip -Z1 frontend/public/downloads/shoptest.apk | rg '\.map$' | wc -l` returned 130, and `unzip -Z1 frontend/public/downloads/shoptest.apk | rg '^assets/public/static/js/.*\.map$' | wc -l` returned 82. `unzip -p frontend/public/downloads/shoptest.apk assets/public/asset-manifest.json | rg -n 'main\..*\.js\.map'` reports `main.559457b3.js.map`, and the bundled `assets/public/static/js/main.559457b3.js` ends with `//# sourceMappingURL=main.559457b3.js.map`. `frontend/public/downloads/shoptest.apk` and `frontend/build/downloads/shoptest.apk` share SHA256 `f4d3f656a717d02047ea28cf5aad69ea412347acd29ca7f5af697d29bb28e30d`.
- Expected fix direction: Build mobile web assets with production source maps disabled before Capacitor sync, and verify the public APK contains no `assets/public/static/**/*.map` files or sourceMappingURL footers unless a private diagnostic channel is explicitly required.

- Retest: 2026-05-30 23:10 UTC rebuilt `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile` and published APK version `1.0.5` with SHA256 `20363030fb3f07503bbf3bf0a4f0248451e4b219f9e8a8de92dfb4adef682baf`. `unzip -Z1 frontend/public/downloads/shoptest.apk | rg '\.map$' | wc -l` returned 0, bundled `asset-manifest.json` has 0 `.map`/`sourceMappingURL` references, bundled `main.28a0efa3.js` has no `sourceMappingURL` footer, and the APK contains 0 `assets/public/downloads/` entries. Status is FIXED.

No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static source/artifact checks were used.

### F217: Category deletion with assigned products falls through to database constraint errors

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 13:59 UTC found `CategoryServiceImpl.deleteById()` only checks `categoryRepository.existsByParentId(id)` before `categoryRepository.deleteById(id)`. `schema.sql` defines `products.category_id` as `NOT NULL` with `FOREIGN KEY (category_id) REFERENCES categories(id)`. The admin and public category delete controllers catch only `IllegalArgumentException`, so database constraint failures from deleting a product-bearing leaf category can bypass the controlled 400/audit-failure path.
- Expected fix direction: Add an explicit product-reference check before category deletion, return an actionable validation error when products still use the category, and record that failure through the existing category audit path.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:17 UTC added `ProductRepository.existsByCategoryId(...)` and made `CategoryServiceImpl.deleteById()` validate category existence, child categories, and direct product assignments before deletion. Product-bearing category deletes now throw controlled `IllegalArgumentException`, allowing existing admin/public delete controllers to return/audit a validation failure instead of falling through to a database constraint error. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F216: Brand status accepts unsupported values that admin filters and public brand lists cannot handle

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 13:53 UTC found `BrandService.save()` validates name and de-duplicates by name but never normalizes or restricts non-empty `status` before saving. Admin and public brand create/update routes both use that service. Public `/brands` returns `brandService.findAll(true)`, which queries exact `ACTIVE`, while `BrandManagement.tsx` filters only exact `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE`, counts active brands with exact `ACTIVE`, and renders raw unsupported statuses through `t(\`status.${status}\`)`.
- Expected fix direction: Normalize brand status to uppercase and reject values outside `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE` in the backend service, then keep admin filters, public active-only reads, and localized status labels aligned with the same enum.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:17 UTC added backend brand status normalization in `BrandService.save()`: blank status defaults to `ACTIVE`, non-blank status is uppercased, and values outside `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE` are rejected before persistence. Admin and public brand routes already use this service, aligning persisted statuses with frontend filters and public active-only reads. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F215: User management mislabels guest accounts as banned and cannot filter them

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 13:35 UTC found `OrderService` creates guest checkout user rows with `status="GUEST"`, and `AdminController.normalizeUserStatus(...)` accepts `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, or `GUEST`. `UserManagement.tsx` only treats exact `ACTIVE` as normal; every other status renders with `pages.adminUsers.bannedStatus`, the filter dropdown offers only `ACTIVE` and `BANNED`, and the row toggle maps any non-active account to `ACTIVE`.
- Expected fix direction: Add first-class `GUEST` status display/filter/action handling in user management, or remove `GUEST` from the admin-update status contract and represent guest accounts separately from banned users.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:21 UTC added first-class `GUEST` handling to `UserManagement.tsx`: status normalization distinguishes `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, and `GUEST`; the status tag renders guest identities with a separate guest label/color; the status filter includes `GUEST`; and guest checkout identities have their status action disabled instead of being converted to `ACTIVE`. EN/ZH/ES locale keys were added for the guest label and disabled-action message. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; locale JSON parse, static `git diff --check`, and source grep were used.

### F214: Logistics carrier status accepts unsupported values that the UI cannot consistently filter or use

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 13:10 UTC found `LogisticsCarrierService.save()` validates `name` and `trackingCode` but never normalizes or restricts non-empty `status` before saving. `LogisticsCarrierManagement.tsx` renders every non-`ACTIVE` status as inactive and filters only exact `ACTIVE` or `INACTIVE`, while `OrderManagement.tsx` loads carrier options with `logisticsCarrierApi.getAll(true)` and `OrderService.shipOrder()` requires exact `ACTIVE`.
- Expected fix direction: Normalize carrier status to uppercase and reject values outside `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE` in the backend service, then keep frontend status options aligned with the same enum.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:17 UTC added backend logistics-carrier status normalization in `LogisticsCarrierService.save()`: blank status defaults to `ACTIVE`, non-blank status is uppercased, and values outside `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE` are rejected before persistence. Admin carrier management, active-only carrier reads, and shipping flows now share the same status contract. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F213: Announcement management shows global summary counts above locally filtered results

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 13:04 UTC found `AnnouncementManagement.tsx` loading `/admin/announcements/summary` separately from `/admin/announcements`, rendering `announcementStats` from global summary fields, and then applying status/keyword filters only in the local `filteredAnnouncements` array used by the table. Backend `SiteAnnouncementService.adminSummary()` counts the full table through repository-wide active/scheduled/expired/inactive/linked queries and accepts no status or keyword scope.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found the current admin announcement list and metrics use the same server-side scope: `SiteAnnouncementController.getAll(...)` and `getSummary(...)` both accept `status` and `keyword`; `SiteAnnouncementService.adminSummary(status, keyword)` calls scoped repository counts; and `AnnouncementManagement.tsx` sends the active `statusFilter` and debounced keyword to both `adminApi.getAnnouncements(...)` and `adminApi.getAnnouncementSummary(...)`. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Make announcement summary counts use the active status/search scope, or label the cards as global totals and provide explicit filters/drilldowns so the metrics do not appear to describe the narrowed table.

### F212: Product question management shows full summary counts above a status-scoped queue

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 12:50 UTC found `ProductQuestionManagement.tsx` initializing `statusFilter` to `UNANSWERED`, loading global `/admin/questions/summary`, and then requesting `/admin/questions` with only the selected status plus a capped `limit`. The table's keyword search filters only the already-loaded `questions` array. Backend `ProductQuestionServiceImpl.adminSummary()` counts all questions, answered questions, unanswered questions, and stale unanswered questions independently of status/search, while `ProductQuestionRepository.findAdminQueue(...)` applies the answered/unanswered predicate before the page limit.
- Expected fix direction: Make product-question search and summary use the same server-side status/keyword scope, or label the metrics as global and provide explicit clickable filters so non-zero counts can navigate to the represented queue.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-31 10:20 UTC aligned the question queue and metrics scope. `/admin/questions` and `/admin/questions/summary` now both accept `status` and `search`; the service computes scoped total/answered/unanswered/stale counts; `adminApi` sends the same params to both endpoints; and `ProductQuestionManagement` uses backend search results directly instead of client-filtering only the loaded rows. No builds or tests were run per instruction.

### F211: Non-super admins can change privileged operators' login and recovery contact fields

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification found `UserManagement.tsx` exposes the profile edit action for every row and sends `email`, `phone`, and `address` through `adminApi.updateUser(...)` without a `canManageRoles` or privileged-target check. Backend `AdminController.updateUser()` only calls `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)` for role changes; it copies email/phone/address to the target account for any caller with `/admin/users` access. `UserMapper.findByUsernameOrPhoneOrEmail()` uses email and phone as login identifiers, and forgot-password verification compares the request to the stored email before `UserService.resetPassword(...)` updates the account password.
- Expected fix direction: Require super-admin authority, or a distinct privileged account-control permission, before changing login/recovery contact fields on admin or super-admin users; route contact updates through validated service logic and mirror the backend rule in the UI.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:23 UTC added backend privileged-target checks in `AdminController.updateUser()`: non-super admins cannot change email, phone, or address for other admin/custom-admin/super-admin operators. The user-management profile edit button is also disabled for privileged target rows unless the current operator is super admin or editing self. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F210: Non-super admins can ban other admin or super-admin accounts through user status updates

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification found `AdminController.updateUser()` only enforces `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)` in the role-update branch, while the status branch accepts `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, or `GUEST` and persists it without checking the target user's admin role or the caller's super-admin authority. The same controller does require super admin before deleting existing `ADMIN` or `SUPER_ADMIN` users. `UserManagement.tsx` gates role selects with `canManageRoles`, but the ban/unban button is disabled only for `isSelf`, then calls `adminApi.updateUser(user.id, { status: newStatus })`.
- Expected fix direction: Require super-admin authority, or a distinct privileged account-control permission, before status changes to admin/super-admin users; mirror the backend rule in the UI while keeping the backend as the authoritative guard.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:23 UTC made `AdminController.updateUser()` require `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)` before changing status on admin/custom-admin/super-admin target accounts. `UserManagement.tsx` mirrors the backend by disabling the status action for privileged target rows when the current operator is not super admin. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F209: Admin user status updates can ban the current operator through the backend endpoint

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification found `UserManagement.tsx` disables the status toggle when `record.id === currentUserId`, but backend `AdminController.updateUser()` has no equivalent guard for `user.getStatus() != null`. The same controller blocks current-operator role changes and deletion, yet the status branch accepts `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, or `GUEST`, assigns the normalized status, and persists it. `UserDetailsImpl.isEnabled()` returns false for `BANNED`, and `JwtAuthenticationFilter` only installs JWT authentication when the loaded user is enabled, so a direct backend request can self-ban the active operator account and lock out subsequent admin requests.
- Expected fix direction: Add a backend current-operator guard for status changes, returning an audited validation failure before persisting; consider limiting self-profile/admin updates to explicit fields so UI-disabled account controls cannot be bypassed by direct API calls.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:23 UTC added a backend self-status guard in `AdminController.updateUser()`. Direct `PUT /admin/users/{currentId}` status changes now return a controlled bad request and audit failure with `Cannot change current operator status`, matching the existing frontend disabled state and backend self-role/delete guards. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F208: Config center reloads masked secrets into the publish editor and blocks unrelated changes

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification found `ConfigCenterService.buildResponse()` returns masked sensitive values in `content`, and `ConfigCenter.tsx` puts `response.data.content` directly into the editable `content` textarea. When the operator applies or publishes that editor text, `ConfigCenterService.validateParsedProperties(...)` rejects sensitive values containing `****`; existing tests separately verify response masking and rejection of `payment.callback-secret=su****90` placeholders. Any config file with an allowed secret key can therefore block an unrelated non-sensitive edit unless the operator re-enters/removes the secret.
- Expected fix direction: Keep masked secrets out of the editable publish payload, preserve unchanged secret keys server-side, or introduce an explicit placeholder protocol that resolves to the existing value instead of being parsed as a literal rejected value.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:25 UTC static source review found `ConfigCenterService.publishInternal(...)` calls `preserveMaskedSensitiveValues(...)` before validation. Masked sensitive keys are read from current Nacos content, raw current line values replace only matching masked placeholders, validation then runs against the restored content, and `buildResponse(...)` still masks sensitive content/properties/effective values before returning to the editor. `ConfigCenterServiceTest.preservesMaskedSensitivePlaceholderFromCurrentNacosContent()` covers the successful preserve/apply path, while `rejectsMaskedSensitivePlaceholderWhenOriginalValueCannotBeRead()` keeps the safe failure path. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F207: Admin user contact edits bypass customer email/phone validation and uniqueness checks

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification found `UserManagement.tsx` sending admin profile edits through `adminApi.updateUser(...)` with only light frontend email/phone length checks. `AdminController.updateUser()` then assigns `email` and `phone` directly to the existing `User` and calls generic `userService.update(...)`, which only sets `updatedAt` and delegates to `UserMapper.update`. This bypasses `UserService.updateProfileContact(...)`, the validated contact path that lowercases/validates email, normalizes phone to the users column limit, and rejects duplicate email/phone ownership.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `UserService.update(...)` now loads the current row and calls `normalizeMutableProfileFields(...)` before persistence. That path lowercases and validates email, normalizes phone, limits address length, and rejects duplicate email/phone ownership through `findByUsernameOrPhoneOrEmail(...)` / `findByPhone(...)`. `AdminController.updateUser(...)` still routes profile edits through `userService.update(existing)`, so admin contact edits no longer bypass backend validation. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Route admin contact edits through a shared validated contact-update service method, or add equivalent backend validation/normalization/duplicate checks and audited validation failures before calling the generic user update.

### F206: Payment provider/config failures can incorrectly escalate client IPs to the payment blacklist

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification found `PaymentController` calls `ipBlacklistService.recordPaymentFailure(...)` for broad `IllegalArgumentException`/`IllegalStateException` failures across create, sync, simulation, generic callback, and Stripe webhook paths. `PaymentService` uses `IllegalStateException` for non-client faults such as missing Stripe/gateway configuration, provider request failures, invalid provider responses, disabled simulation, missing production callback secret, and missing Stripe webhook secret. Those failures flow into `IpBlacklistService.recordFailure(SOURCE_PAYMENT, ...)`, which blocks the caller IP after `security.ip-blacklist.payment-failure-threshold` failures.
- Expected fix direction: separate suspicious client-controlled payment failures from backend/provider outages before recording payment blacklist failures; keep outage/config failures in audit logs and system alerts without increasing blacklist counters.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:21 UTC static source review found `PaymentController.recordPaymentBlacklistFailureIfSuspicious(...)` now gates blacklist writes. `shouldRecordPaymentBlacklistFailure(...)` returns true for client-controlled `IllegalArgumentException` and 4xx `ResponseStatusException` failures only; provider/config/outage `IllegalStateException` paths such as missing Stripe/gateway settings, provider request/response failures, disabled simulation, and missing production callback secrets are audited but do not call `ipBlacklistService.recordPaymentFailure(...)`. Stripe webhook `IllegalStateException` is returned as `503` without blacklist escalation. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F205: Customer and guest confirm-receipt mutations bypass order audit logging

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:19 UTC found `OrderController.confirmReceipt()` and `confirmGuestReceipt()` both delegate to `confirmReceiptInternal()`, which validates registered-account or guest access, calls `orderService.updateOrderStatus(id, "COMPLETED")`, and returns `Order completed` without any `auditLogService.record(...)` call. `OrderService.assertNextStatus()` permits `SHIPPED -> COMPLETED`, and `updateOrderStatus()` writes the status plus customer notification. Nearby order lifecycle paths in the same controller record `ORDER_CANCEL`, `RETURN_REQUEST`, and `RETURN_SHIPMENT_SUBMIT`, and admin status updates record status-specific audit events, so customer/guest confirm receipt is the status-changing path missing audit coverage.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `OrderController.confirmReceiptInternal(...)` records `ORDER_CONFIRM_RECEIPT` success after `orderService.updateOrderStatus(id, "COMPLETED")`, and records failure in the catch block with order id, guest/auth context, previous status, order number, guest email, and guest order number metadata. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Add success/failure audit logging for confirm receipt, including order id, actor or guest context, previous/next status, and order number; keep guest credential failures visible in the same audit family where feasible.

### F204: IP blacklist login-failure admin endpoint mutates blocks without audit logging

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:17 UTC found `AdminIpBlacklistController` auditing manual `IP_BLACKLIST_BLOCK`, `IP_BLACKLIST_RELEASE`, and `IP_BLACKLIST_BATCH_RELEASE`, but `@PostMapping("/record-login-failure")` only calls `ipBlacklistService.recordFailure(SOURCE_LOGIN, ...)` and returns 204. `IpBlacklistService.recordFailure()` inserts or updates `ip_blacklist_entries`, increments `failure_count`, can transition a row to `BLOCKED`, updates `blocked_at`/`blocked_until`, and emits a system alert via `recordBlockedAlert(...)`. Admin-triggered calls to that endpoint can therefore change blacklist state without the actor/request audit trail present on the other IP blacklist mutations.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `AdminIpBlacklistController.recordLoginFailure(...)` now requires `ip-blacklist:record-failure`, calls `ipBlacklistService.recordFailure(...)`, and records `IP_BLACKLIST_RECORD_LOGIN_FAILURE` success/failure audit events with masked IP/reason metadata. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Either remove/lock down the endpoint if it is only a test/internal hook, or add the same success/failure audit coverage and actor/request metadata used by manual block and release operations.

### F203: User management exposes two role editors whose effects conflict for custom-role users

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:06 UTC found `UserManagement.tsx` rendering editable selects for both `roleCode` and legacy `role` on the same row. `handleRoleChange()` calls `adminApi.updateUser(userId, { role: newRole })`, while `handleRoleCodeChange()` calls `/admin/users/{id}/role-code`. Backend `AdminController.updateUser()` preserves `existing.getRoleCode()` whenever the new role is non-USER and the existing role code is nonblank; `AdminRoleService.getPermissionsForUser()` then prioritizes that nonblank role code over `role`. Changing a custom-role user through the legacy role dropdown can therefore leave effective permissions on the old custom role.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `UserManagement.tsx` now renders one editable role assignment control, the `roleCode` selector, and displays the legacy `role` column as a read-only tag. `handleRoleCodeChange(...)` routes `USER` to the standard role update and custom/admin role codes to `/admin/users/{id}/role-code`; backend standard role updates also map `ADMIN` and `SUPER_ADMIN` to their matching role codes instead of preserving stale custom codes. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Remove the legacy role dropdown for role-code-managed users, route all role assignment through a single `roleCode` model, or clear/update `roleCode` explicitly when the UI assigns standard `ADMIN`/`USER` roles.

### F202: Order summary quick cards are counted with the active status filter but clicks clear that filter

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:04 UTC found `AdminController.getOrdersPage()` returning `summary = buildAdminOrderSummary(safeStatus, safeSearch)`, and `buildAdminOrderSummary()` passing the active status into `orderService.countAdminOrders(status, search, quick)` for each quick-card count. In `OrderManagement.tsx`, `handleQuickFilterChange()` sets `filterStatus(undefined)` and `syncRouteFilters({ quick: nextQuick })`, while `buildOrderFilterSearchParams()` deletes `status` whenever `quick` is present. The displayed quick-card count can therefore be status-scoped, but the click destination is the global quick queue.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `AdminController.buildAdminOrdersPage(...)` now returns `summary = buildAdminOrderSummary(safeSearch)`, and `buildAdminOrderSummary(...)` calls `orderService.countAdminOrders(null, search, quick)`. Quick-card counts therefore match the global quick queue opened by `OrderManagement.handleQuickFilterChange(...)`. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Either compute quick summary cards in the same global scope used by the click destination, or preserve/encode the active status scope when opening a quick filter; the UI should also label scoped counts clearly if both dimensions remain active.

### F201: Dashboard payment-method breakdown counts unpaid and cancelled orders

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:01 UTC found `AdminController.getDashboard()` defining `revenueStatuses` for paid-commerce metrics and using it for `paidOrders`, `salesTrend` revenue, and `topProducts`, but building `paymentMethodBreakdown` from every order where `order.getPaymentMethod()` is nonblank. `OrderService` stores `request.getPaymentMethod()` while creating orders in `PENDING_PAYMENT`, so unpaid, cancelled, returned, or otherwise non-revenue orders can still contribute to the dashboard payment-method chart.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `AdminController.getDashboard()` now builds `paymentMethodBreakdown` from `paidOrders.stream()`, matching the same paid/revenue status scope used for paid orders, revenue, sales trend, average order value, and top products. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Filter `paymentMethodBreakdown` to the same paid/revenue status scope used by the surrounding revenue analytics, or relabel/split it as attempted checkout payment-method selections.

### F200: Coupon management filters only the truncated recent list while summary counts all coupons

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:57 UTC found `CouponService.findAll()` returning only the newest `admin.coupons.search-max-rows` coupons through `PageRequest.of(0, limit, Sort.by(DESC, "id"))`, while `CouponManagement.tsx` calls `adminApi.getCoupons()` without filter parameters and applies keyword/status/scope filters locally to the capped `coupons` array. The same page renders insight cards from `/admin/coupons/summary`, whose backend `CouponService.adminSummary()` counts the full coupon table and full active/public/expiring/low-remaining subsets. Older matching coupons outside the capped newest list can therefore make the cards non-zero while the filtered or searched table is empty.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:46 UTC static source review found `CouponManagement.tsx` now calls `adminApi.getCoupons({ keyword, status, scope, page, size })`, uses returned `items/total/page/size/totalPages`, and consumes the response `summary`. `AdminController.getCoupons(...)` returns a server-side filtered page whenever page/size or filters are present and includes `couponService.adminSummary(keyword, status, scope)`. `CouponService.adminSummary(...)` applies the same normalized keyword/status/scope filters to total, active, inactive, public-active, expiring-soon, and low-remaining counts. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Add server-side coupon pagination/search/status/scope filters with summary counts scoped to the active query, or clearly disclose the capped recent-list scope and provide navigation to older matching coupons.

### F199: Pet Gallery admin exposes a Deleted filter that the backend can never populate

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:47 UTC found `PetGalleryManagement.tsx` declaring `STATUS_OPTIONS = ['ALL', 'ACTIVE', 'DELETED']` and filtering local `photos` by selected status. Backend `PetGalleryService.adminDeletePhoto()` soft-deletes by setting `status='DELETED'`, but `findAllForAdmin()` returns only `photoRepository.findAllByStatusOrderByCreatedAtDescIdDesc(ACTIVE_STATUS)`, and `AdminController.getGalleryPhotos()` returns that active-only list directly. The `DELETED` filter is therefore permanently empty after reload even when deleted gallery records exist.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found Pet Gallery admin now uses a server-side paginated/filterable route. `PetGalleryManagement.tsx` sends `status` to `adminApi.getPetGalleryPhotos(...)`; `AdminController.getGalleryPhotos(...)` passes it to `petGalleryService.findForAdmin(...)`; and `PetGalleryPhotoRepository.searchAdminPhotos(...)` filters by the requested status, including `DELETED`. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Either expose a backend admin list that can include deleted photos by status, or remove/disable the `DELETED` filter if deleted gallery records are intentionally not auditable from the page.

### F198: Dashboard logistics coverage divides shipped orders by all tracked orders

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:44 UTC found `AdminController.getDashboard()` counting `shippedOrders` only when `order.status == SHIPPED`, but counting `ordersWithTracking` for every order with a nonblank tracking number regardless of status. Frontend `AdminDashboard.tsx` computes `trackingCoverage = ordersWithTracking / shippedOrders` and clamps it to 100, while missing-tracking count is `SHIPPED`-scoped. Historical completed/returned tracked orders can therefore inflate coverage and make `With tracking` exceed `Shipped orders` beside a non-zero missing-tracking warning.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC static source review found `AdminController.getDashboard()` now builds `currentlyShippedOrders` first, then computes `shippedOrders`, `ordersWithTracking`, and `ordersWithoutTracking` from that same `SHIPPED`-only list. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Use the same `SHIPPED` scope for both numerator and denominator, or rename/split the metrics so historical tracked orders are not presented as current shipped-order coverage.

### F197: Dashboard fulfillment readiness card counts two queues but opens only one

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:41 UTC found `AdminDashboard.tsx` setting the fulfillment card value to `pendingShipmentOrders + missingTrackingOrders`, but its target is conditional: `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT` when pending shipments exist, otherwise `/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING`. `OrderManagement.tsx` resolves either `status` or `quick`, not both, and the missing-tracking quick filter is a disjoint `SHIPPED` + blank tracking queue. If both queues have rows, the destination cannot show all work included in the displayed card count.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC development/static retest fixed the mismatch by splitting the fulfillment readiness card back to pending shipment only, with target `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT`. Missing tracking remains a separate operational action targeting `/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING`, so each count opens the queue it represents. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Split the card into separate pending-shipment and missing-tracking actions, or add a combined order filter/view that returns the exact union represented by the card value.

### F196: Permission management lets admins edit reserved roles that the backend refuses to save

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:31 UTC found `PermissionManagement.tsx` renders the edit button for every role row and opens the full role editor via `openRoleModal(role)`. The modal disables only the role-code input for existing roles; name, description, and permission checkboxes remain editable. Saving calls `adminApi.saveRole(...)`, and backend `AdminController.saveRole()` forwards to `AdminRoleService.save()`, where `USER`, `ADMIN`, and `SUPER_ADMIN` role codes are rejected with `Role code is reserved or empty`. Because `ensureSchema()` seeds `ADMIN` and `SUPER_ADMIN` into `/admin/roles`, the page can display non-functional edit actions for those built-in rows.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:46 UTC static source review found `PermissionManagement.tsx` defines `RESERVED_ROLE_CODES` for `USER`, `ADMIN`, and `SUPER_ADMIN`; `openRoleModal(...)` refuses reserved rows with a read-only message; and the table action column renders `pages.permissions.systemRole` instead of an edit button for reserved roles. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Render built-in roles as read-only with no save action, or expose only backend-supported fields/actions for reserved roles and keep custom-role editing separate.

### F195: Review management insight cards mix full-result and current-page scopes

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:28 UTC found `ReviewManagement.tsx` stores only the current paginated `res.data.items` in `reviews` and computes `lowRating`, `needsReply`, and `averageRating` from that page-local array. The same `reviewStats` object uses backend `reviewSummary` for `pending` and `approved` when present. Backend `AdminController.getAllReviews()` returns `summary = reviewService.summarizeAdminReviewStatuses(safeSearch)`, and `ReviewServiceImpl.summarizeAdminReviewStatuses()` counts PENDING/APPROVED/HIDDEN across all rows matching the search term, not the active status filter or current page. The API exposes no full-result low-rating, needs-reply, or average-rating fields, so the cards can disagree with the visible paginated/status-filtered table.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:14 UTC development/static retest aligned review insights with the active server-side scope. `AdminController.getAllReviews(...)` now calls `reviewService.summarizeAdminReviews(safeStatus, safeSearch)`, and `ReviewServiceImpl` computes status counts, low-rating, needs-reply, and average rating with both status and search filters before pagination. `ReviewManagement.tsx` consumes `res.data.summary` for all insight cards. `git diff --check` passed for the touched files; no build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Return a single backend review insights summary for the active filter scope, including low-rating, needs-reply, and average-rating across the full filtered result set, or label page-derived metrics as current-page metrics and keep all cards page-scoped.

### F194: Product batch approve/reject can update hidden rows after filters change

- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:57 UTC development pass added a current-visible-selection layer in `ProductManagement.tsx`. Batch approve/reject now submits only `selectedVisibleProductIds`, the table receives `selectedVisibleProductKeys`, stale hidden selections are pruned when `filteredProducts` changes, and the update-template download uses the same visible selection scope.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:22 UTC found `ProductManagement.tsx` keeps `selectedProductIds` independent from `filteredProducts`; changing `filterStatus`, `filterCategory`, `searchKeyword`, or `listingQualityFilter` does not clear or intersect the selection. The table renders `dataSource={filteredProducts}` while `handleBatchProductStatus()` sends every id in `selectedProductIds` to `adminApi.batchUpdateProductStatus(...)`. Backend `/admin/products/batch-status` updates every supplied id.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:57 UTC static retest confirmed `handleBatchProductStatus(...)` guards on and submits `selectedVisibleProductIds`, while the table selection state is intersected with `visibleProductIdSet` from `filteredProducts`. No build, automated test, or screenshot run was performed in this pass.
- Expected fix direction: Clear selected product ids when filters change, or derive the submitted batch ids from the current visible rows and show an explicit count of hidden selected rows before applying a batch status update.

### F193: Dashboard refunding-payment card routes to completed refund audit logs

### F192: Alert search treats empty severity/category filters as WARNING and APPLICATION

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:17 UTC found `AlertManagement.tsx` sends `severity: undefined` for the `ALL` selector and `category: undefined` for a blank category field, but backend `SystemAlertService.search()` binds `blankToNull(normalizeSeverity(severity))` and `blankToNull(normalizeCategory(category))`. Because `normalizeSeverity(null)` returns `WARNING` and `normalizeCategory(null)` returns `APPLICATION`, omitted filters become real SQL filters instead of wildcards.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 17:57 UTC static source review confirmed `SystemAlertService.search(...)` now computes `normalizeSeverityFilter(...)` and `normalizeCategoryFilter(...)`, preserving null, blank, and `ALL` as null SQL filters while still normalizing supplied values. No build, automated test, screenshot, video, or trace was run in this pass.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:10 UTC no-media regression confirmed the current source keeps null/blank/`ALL` severity and category filters as null SQL wildcards through `(? IS NULL OR severity = ?)` and `(? IS NULL OR category = ?)`. Live admin API direct verification remained blocked by 401 without a runtime admin token, so this pass used source evidence for backend semantics and authenticated frontend fixtures for request-shape checks. 未截图、未录屏、未生成 trace/图片.
- Expected fix direction: Preserve null/blank severity and category through search as null filters, and only apply `normalizeSeverity()` / `normalizeCategory()` when a caller actually supplies a filter value; add a service test for null severity/category search semantics.

### F191: Single alert acknowledge API can regress resolved alerts back to acknowledged

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:08 UTC found `AlertManagement.tsx` only renders Ack for `OPEN` alerts and batch acknowledge uses a backend `status <> RESOLVED` guard, but `SystemAlertService.acknowledge()` calls `updateStatus(id, STATUS_ACKNOWLEDGED, actor)` with SQL `WHERE id = ?` only. Direct or stale calls to `/admin/alerts/{id}/acknowledge` can therefore update an already resolved alert back to `ACKNOWLEDGED` while leaving resolved metadata populated.
- Evidence: 2026-05-31 04:47 UTC static retest confirmed `SystemAlertService.updateStatus(...)` now guards single alert acknowledge and resolve SQL with `WHERE id = ? AND status <> RESOLVED`, matching the batch transition guard.
- Resolution: Single alert acknowledge/resolve updates now preserve resolved alerts as terminal and stale acknowledge requests cannot regress them to `ACKNOWLEDGED`.
- Expected fix direction: Add a current-status guard to single acknowledge, such as only acknowledging `OPEN` alerts, and return a clear no-op/not-found/conflict result for already resolved alerts; add a service/controller test that resolved alerts cannot regress.

### F190: Admin support WebSocket replies bypass security audit logging

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:04 UTC found REST admin support actions in `SupportController` record `SUPPORT_MESSAGE_SEND`, `SUPPORT_SESSION_CLOSE`, `SUPPORT_SESSION_ASSIGN`, and `SUPPORT_SESSION_REOPEN` audit events, but `SupportWebSocketHandler` has no `SecurityAuditLogService` and directly calls `supportService.sendAdminMessage(...)` for admin `SEND` frames and `supportService.closeSession(...)` for `CLOSE` frames. `SupportManagement.send()` prefers the WebSocket send path whenever the socket is open, so normal connected admin replies bypass the REST audit trail.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:41 UTC static source review found `SupportWebSocketHandler` now injects `SecurityAuditLogService` and records `SUPPORT_MESSAGE_SEND` success/failure for admin WebSocket `SEND` frames plus `SUPPORT_SESSION_CLOSE` success/failure for admin WebSocket `CLOSE` frames. The audit helper reads actor id/username/role from the authenticated WebSocket session, uses `SUPPORT_SESSION` resource metadata, and returns without recording for customer-side sockets. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Add equivalent audit logging to the WebSocket admin action path, or route admin WebSocket sends/closes through an audited application service shared with the REST controller, including failure logging where possible.

### F189: Support admin WebSocket updates bypass the active queue filter

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 09:59 UTC found `SupportManagement.tsx` loads initial sessions with the selected backend `status`, but WebSocket `MESSAGE` prepends `payload.session` without checking the active filter, and `SESSION_UPDATED` / `SESSION_CLOSED` replace matching rows without removing rows that no longer match. The rendered `filteredQueueSessions` only filters for `NEEDS_REPLY`; `OPEN`, `CLOSED`, and `ALL` render the local `sessions` array directly.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 20:41 UTC static source review found `SupportManagement.tsx` now derives `queueSessions` with `supportSessionMatchesQueue(session, filter, queueSearch)`. The local queue enforces `OPEN`, `CLOSED`, `NEEDS_REPLY`, `ALL`, and active search text after WebSocket upserts, so realtime session/message updates can remain cached without being rendered outside the active queue. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Apply the active queue filter to realtime upserts/removals, or derive visible sessions from `sessions` with explicit `OPEN`/`CLOSED`/`NEEDS_REPLY` filtering so WebSocket updates cannot violate the selected queue scope.

### F185: Admin refund modal blocks recovery of payments already marked REFUNDING

- Status: FIXED

Review-needed labels include `pages.petGalleryAdmin.likes`, `pages.alertAdmin.fingerprint`, `pages.trafficControl.circuitBreakers`, `pages.systemMonitor.driver/error`, `pages.logAdmin.on/off`, `pages.adminQuestions.totalQuestions`, `pages.productAdmin.richVideo/myStore`, `pages.productDetail.color`, and `pages.brandAdmin.logo`.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 06:08 UTC static locale parsing confirmed the review-needed Spanish UI labels are now localized: alert fingerprint, traffic circuit breakers, system monitor driver/error, log on/off, pet-gallery likes, product-question total count, product rich-video, product detail color, brand logo, and product-list material/color filters. Remaining Spanish values identical to English are expected brand/technical/payment/city/unit or Spanish-identical values (`ShopMX`, `$`, `ID`, `SKU`, payment names, Toronto/Shanghai, `d/h/m`, `Total`, `Manual`, `Normal`, `No`, and URL placeholders). No build, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Translate review-needed UI labels while leaving confirmed brand/payment/technical constants unchanged.
