### F175: Admin product listing lacks server-side pagination metadata and ProductManagement loads all rows

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static analysis at 2026-05-30 08:45 UTC found `AdminController.getProducts()` returns `List<Product>` while other admin endpoints return paginated objects. Rechecked at 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC: `ProductManagement.tsx` calls `adminApi.getProducts()` without `page`/`size` and applies client-side filtering/export/table pagination over the full list.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:54 UTC static source review confirmed `/admin/products` now returns `ProductAdminPageResponse` metadata from `productService.findAdminProductPage(...)`; `findAdminProductPage(...)` uses repository `findAll(Specification, PageRequest)` for admin keyword/category/status/featured/discount/price filters; `adminApi.getProducts(...)` normalizes page metadata; and `ProductManagement.tsx` requests server page/filter params and controls Ant Table pagination from backend `total`. `rg` found no remaining touched-path full-list admin product loading references, and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Return paginated response with total count metadata for admin product listing and migrate ProductManagement to server-side pagination/filtering for large catalogs.

### F174: Admin review entity serialization may expose full Product objects

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `Review` entity has `@ManyToOne Product product` without DTO mapping for admin endpoints.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 22:16 UTC static source review confirmed `/admin/reviews` now returns `List<AdminReviewResponse>` items from `reviewService.searchAdminReviewResponses(...)`, and admin reply/status endpoints return `AdminReviewResponse` instead of raw `Review`. The DTO contains `productId`, `productName`, `productImageUrl`, `userId`, `username`, and review moderation fields, with no full `Product` object. Review Management consumes the safe product display fields. `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No build or automated tests were run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Use a DTO for admin review responses that includes only necessary product display fields.

### F173: Manual controller errors bypass the shared API error envelope

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `GlobalApiExceptionHandler`/`ApiErrorResponseFactory` produce a shared envelope with `error`, `message`, status metadata, path, request id, and timestamp, and frontend `getApiErrorMessage()` reads both `error` and `message`. Rechecked at 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC: multiple controllers still manually return `Map.of("error", ...)`, omitting the shared metadata and `message` field.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 06:03 UTC static source review confirmed `ManualApiErrorResponseAdvice` now intercepts JSON-like controller `Map` bodies that contain `error` but miss shared envelope fields, rebuilds the payload through `ApiErrorResponseFactory`, preserves extra fields, and forces a bad-request status if a manual error body somehow leaves with a non-error status. Existing manual `Map.of("error", ...)` controller paths therefore serialize with `message`, `status`, `statusText`, `path`, `requestId`, and `timestamp` without controller-by-controller rewrites. No build, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Route manual bad-request/error responses through the shared `ApiErrorResponseFactory` or throw handled exceptions consistently.

### F172: Admin order list shows only userId and has no username/customer display field

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Rechecked at 2026-05-30 09:02 UTC: `OrderManagement.tsx` displays a `userId` column only, while `AdminController.getOrdersPage()` returns raw `Order` rows from `OrderMapper.searchAdminOrders`. The mapper selects only from `orders` and does not join `users`, so frontend `Order.username?: string` is never populated.
- Expected fix direction: Add a dedicated admin order response DTO with customer display fields, or join `users` in the admin order query and map a safe username/email/guest marker.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 23:51 UTC static source review confirms `/admin/orders` and `/admin/orders/page` now map paged rows to `AdminOrderResponse`, `OrderMapper.searchAdminOrders` left-joins `users` and aliases `customerDisplayName`, `customerEmail`, `customerPhone`, and `customerType`, admin order search also checks username/email/phone, CSV export includes the same customer fields, and `OrderManagement.tsx` renders a localized customer column instead of a bare `userId` column. No builds or automated tests were run per instruction.

### F171: Signed-in checkout does not preserve recipient/contact fields structurally

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Rechecked at 2026-05-30 09:02 UTC: `CheckoutRequest` still only has `userId`, `cartItemIds`, `shippingAddress`, `paymentMethod`, and `userCouponId`. `Checkout.tsx` flattens recipient name, phone, region, postal code, and detail into `shippingAddress`, so the information is not fully lost, but it is stored as an opaque string. The `orderApi.checkout()` helper still accepts and posts `recipientName`, `recipientPhone`, and `contactEmail` when supplied, but the backend ignores those fields.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 05:51 UTC static source review confirmed signed-in checkout now sends `recipientName` and `recipientPhone`, `CheckoutRequest` accepts structured contact fields, `OrderService.checkout(...)` persists them to `orders.recipient_name`, `orders.recipient_phone`, and `orders.contact_email`, and admin/customer order DTOs plus admin CSV/export/shipping-label paths expose the structured fields. Guest checkout also stores the same fields and guest email lookups prefer `contact_email` before legacy address parsing. No build, automated test, screenshot, video, trace, or commit was run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: Add structured recipient/contact fields to the backend order DTO/storage or remove the dead frontend payload fields and make the single-string contract explicit.

### F170: backend/ contains orphan com.shop controllers with incompatible API contracts

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Rechecked at 2026-05-30 09:06 UTC: `backend/src/main/java/com/shop/*` contains orphan `ReviewController` (`/api/reviews`) and `PaymentController` (`/api/payment`) contracts that differ from the live `src/main/java/com/example/shop/*` controllers. `backend/pom.xml` sets `<sourceDirectory>${project.basedir}/../src/main/java</sourceDirectory>`, so the `com.shop` tree is not compiled by that module and is dead/misleading source.
- Expected fix direction: Remove the orphan `backend/src/main/java/com/shop/*` tree or make the module structure unambiguous so developers cannot edit dead controllers expecting runtime changes.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:04 UTC source cleanup removed all files under `backend/src/main/java/com/shop`, while `backend/pom.xml` still points compilation at the authoritative root `../src/main/java`. Static `find backend/src/main/java -type f` returned no orphan backend source files. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F169: WebSocket support path is missing from IP blacklist protected prefixes

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: `/ws/support` is `permitAll`, `IpBlacklistFilter` only checks paths allowed by `IpBlacklistService.shouldCheckPath()`, and the default `security.ip-blacklist.path-prefixes` include `/support/guest` but not `/ws/support` while `block-all-paths=false`. `SupportWebSocketHandler` does not perform a direct blacklist check.
- Expected fix direction: Add `/ws/support` to IP blacklist protected path defaults or enforce blacklist checks during WebSocket handshake/connection establishment.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:00 UTC static source review found `/ws/support` in `IpBlacklistService.DEFAULT_PROTECTED_PATH_PREFIXES`, `src/main/resources/application.properties` default `security.ip-blacklist.path-prefixes`, and `ConfigCenterService` default content, so WebSocket support handshakes are now covered by the default IP-blacklist protected path set. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F168: Guest checkout lacks endpoint-specific anti-spam controls beyond generic rate limiting

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Corrected at 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC: global `RateLimitFilter` is installed in `SecurityConfig` and `/orders/checkout/guest` is not skipped, so generic public rate limiting applies. The remaining evidenced gap is no guest-checkout-specific lower quota, CAPTCHA, velocity check, email/recipient throttling, or other order-spam control beyond the default public bucket.
- Expected fix direction: Add endpoint-specific guest checkout abuse controls in addition to the generic public rate limit.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:00 UTC static source review found `RateLimitService.endpointLimitFor(...)` now assigns `POST /orders/checkout/guest` to a dedicated `checkout:guest` one-hour bucket, backed by the new `traffic.rate-limit.guest-checkout-per-hour` runtime property with default 10/hour in `application.properties`, config-center defaults, and `deploy/backend.env.example`. The dedicated bucket is consumed alongside the existing public bucket and uses the same Redis/local counter path as other endpoint-specific limits. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F167: Product URL import has DNS rebinding SSRF risk despite private-IP validation

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Rechecked at 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC: `ProductUrlImportService.normalizeAndValidateUri()` already blocks non-http(s), credentials, non-standard ports, and hosts resolving to local/private/link-local/multicast addresses. The remaining risk is DNS rebinding/time-of-check-to-time-of-use because validation resolves the host before `HttpClient` performs its own DNS resolution for the fetch.
- Expected fix direction: Pin resolved addresses or otherwise verify the actual outbound connection target after DNS resolution, including redirects and dynamic fallback fetches.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:20 UTC static source review found `ProductUrlImportService` now creates a public DNS pin snapshot for each outbound import request. `fetchHtml(...)` and `sendTopmRequest(...)` resolve and validate the host address set immediately before sending, then call `DnsPin.verifyUnchanged()` after the response; any changed address set or newly blocked private/local/multicast address rejects the import. Redirect targets, nested `goods_url` fallback fetches, and TOPM dynamic POST/GET endpoints all pass through those guarded paths. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F166: Hardcoded infrastructure IPs in runtime defaults, deploy templates, and docs

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Rechecked at 2026-05-30 09:06 UTC: `158.101.11.223` remains in `src/main/resources/application.properties`, `src/main/resources/application.yml`, `shop-gateway/src/main/resources/application.yml`, deploy env examples, Docker compose defaults, and deployment docs.
- Expected fix direction: Use environment variables or neutral placeholder values instead of hardcoded infrastructure IPs in defaults/templates/docs.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:17 UTC static source review found the real infrastructure IP removed from `src/main/resources`, `shop-gateway`, `deploy`, and `docs`. Runtime defaults now use local-only Nacos/Redis fallbacks, deploy templates/examples require explicit origins or neutral internal host placeholders, and docs use placeholder hosts such as `db.internal.example`, `nacos.internal.example`, `redis.internal.example`, and `gateway.internal.example`. Follow-up at 2026-05-31 21:25 UTC also changed `ConfigCenterService.nacosServerAddr()` fallback to `localhost:8848`; `rg -n "158\\.101\\.11\\.223" src/main/java src/main/resources shop-gateway deploy docs` returned no matches. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F165: Empty JWT secret defaults accepted in non-production mode

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: JwtService.java only validates secret strength in production mode. Empty secrets accepted otherwise.
- Expected fix direction: Validate JWT secret in all modes or at least warn loudly.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:07 UTC static source review found `JwtService.ensureJwtSecretConfigured()` no longer checks runtime mode before validation. Token generation and parsing now reject empty, known placeholder, or shorter-than-32-character secrets in every runtime mode, `application.properties` documents `JWT_SECRET` as required in every runtime, and `JwtServiceTest` no longer expects dev mode to accept the placeholder secret. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F164: Admin notification HTML relies on client-side sanitization before storage

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Corrected at 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC: product `detailContent` is structured JSON and storefront rendering uses React text/media, so the original product-detail wording was too broad. The remaining gap is `POST /admin/notifications/broadcast`: `NotificationManagement.tsx` sanitizes HTML before submit and `Notifications.tsx` sanitizes again before `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, but `AdminController.broadcastNotification()` passes raw `body.get("message")` to `NotificationService.broadcastToCustomers()`, which stores `message.trim()` unchanged when `contentFormat=HTML`.
- Expected fix direction: Add server-side HTML sanitization/allowlisting before persisting HTML notifications, independent of frontend behavior.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 21:11 UTC static source review found `NotificationService.broadcastToCustomers(...)` now normalizes messages before insert. For `contentFormat=HTML`, server-side sanitization removes blocked executable/embedded tags and comments, strips event-handler/style/srcdoc attributes, drops unsafe URL attributes, normalizes safe `_blank` anchors with `rel="noopener noreferrer"`, and rejects messages that sanitize to empty. No build or automated test was run per instruction.

### F163: Password hash exposure in admin API responses is already blocked

- Status: WONTFIX
- Evidence: Evidence review at 2026-05-30 08:59 UTC found `User.password` already has `@JsonProperty(access = JsonProperty.Access.WRITE_ONLY)` and `UserJsonSerializationTest.passwordIsAcceptedOnInputButNeverSerialized()` asserts serialized `User` JSON omits `password`. The MyBatis password mapping does not by itself expose the field through Jackson responses.
- Expected fix direction: No change needed for this specific reported exposure; DTOs can still be considered as a separate hardening/refactor item.

### F162: Storefront pages call translation keys that do not exist in the parsed locale map

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static extraction of literal `t('...')` calls found unresolved runtime keys in navigation, order tracking, product list, stock alerts, and pet-gallery management. `translate()` turns missing paths into humanized English placeholders, so examples resolve to `Next Action Ready Title`, `Direct Ready`, `Choose Options Action`, `Result Context Label`, `Login`, `Products`, and `Support` instead of localized copy.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 06:20 UTC static source review confirmed the missing storefront locale paths now exist in English, Spanish, and Chinese. JSON parsing passed for `frontend/src/locales/{en,es,zh}.json`, and the literal `t('...')` scan over `frontend/src` now reports `literal t() missing keys: 0`. No build, Jest, Maven, UI smoke, screenshot, or commit was run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: either correct the component calls to existing translation paths or add the missing keys to the proper namespaces in English, Spanish, and Chinese; extend i18n tests so literal `t()` calls must resolve in the English baseline and duplicate-key overwrites cannot hide runtime misses.

### F161: Duplicate pet-gallery admin locale keys silently override translations

- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static scan found duplicate `pages.petGalleryAdmin` objects in `frontend/src/locales/en.json`, `frontend/src/locales/es.json`, and `frontend/src/locales/zh.json`. JSON parsing keeps only the second object, so translated keys from the first object are silently discarded. This explains why F157 still reports 12 Spanish keys missing and also means English/Chinese can render raw pet-gallery management keys for copy that only exists in the discarded first object.
- Retest: 2026-05-30 08:19 UTC targeted `npm test -- --runInBand src/i18n.test.ts` failed on the same parsed Spanish missing-key set.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 06:20 UTC static duplicate-key recheck found exactly one `petGalleryAdmin` source block in each locale file. Parsed `pages.petGalleryAdmin` now has 40 keys in English, Spanish, and Chinese, so the earlier duplicate-overwrite condition is no longer present. No build, Jest, Maven, UI smoke, screenshot, or commit was run per instruction.
- Expected fix direction: merge each duplicate `pages.petGalleryAdmin` namespace into a single object per locale, preserving both the current management-page keys and the newer stats/preview keys; add or extend a duplicate-key guard so future locale JSON cannot silently overwrite namespaces.

## Resolved Issues

### F407: Native App bottom rail leaves a large blank area after hiding the support tab

- Area: Frontend / Native App bottom navigation
- Environment: Android WebView-like native App shell with five visible bottom-rail actions
- Steps: Open the native App storefront and inspect the fixed bottom navigation rail after the App-specific support tab is hidden.
- Expected: The five visible actions should occupy five equal columns across the full rail, with the account action aligned to the far-right slot and no unused blank column.
- Actual: The footer workbench visually showed five buttons, but the right side of the rail had a large empty area because the App CSS relied on positional child hiding while later bottom-rail overrides could leave the visible items out of the intended five-column placement.
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added semantic bottom-rail item classes in `Navbar.tsx`, hid the App support tab by class in `mobile-app.css`, pinned the remaining Home/Products/Coupons/Cart/Account actions to five equal grid columns, and forced visible items to stretch to their grid cells. Follow-up hardening makes native App mode omit the footer support tab from the DOM, adds a `shop-nav__bottomBar--native` five-area layout, and replaces the older positional `nth-child(5)` App hide rule with semantic support-tab hiding so Account cannot be mistaken for the removed support slot. The floating App support launcher remains available.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 17:57 UTC static source review confirmed the semantic class wiring and final App-only CSS override. No build, automated test, screenshot, video, or trace was run in this pass.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 18:20 UTC no-screenshot Playwright DOM/layout probe at Android WebView-like `390x844` and `360x800` confirmed five bottom-item DOM nodes, five visible items, no support DOM node, equal five-column grid (`maxWidthDelta` <= 0.01px), Account in the far-right slot with only 4-6px rail padding to the right edge, and `scrollWidth == clientWidth`. `git diff --check` for the touched nav files and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` both passed.

### F133: Public payment channel response exposes provider implementation details

- Area: Backend / Payment channels; Frontend / checkout payment typing
- Environment: Public `/payments/channels` used by checkout, profile payment recovery, and payment component
- Steps: Inspect enabled channel JSON from `/payments/channels`
- Expected: Public channel data contains only checkout display and recommendation fields
- Actual: Public channel data included `provider` and `refundMode`, exposing payment adapter and refund-mode internals that the storefront did not use
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Removed `provider` and `refundMode` from `PaymentChannelResponse` and frontend `PaymentChannel`; server-side payment/refund configuration remains internal.

### F132: Frontend exposes unimplemented guest cart quote route

- Area: Frontend / cart API contract; Backend / route parity
- Environment: Future guest cart quote or checkout wiring using `/cart/guest/quote`
- Steps: Compare frontend `cartApi.quoteGuestItems()` with backend `CartController` mappings
- Expected: Exported cart helpers correspond to implemented backend routes
- Actual: The frontend exported a helper for `/cart/guest/quote`, but no backend route existed and current pages did not use it
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Removed the unused helper so no frontend API contract advertises the missing route.

### F131: Frontend public coupon contract still models admin coupon fields

- Area: Frontend / Coupon Center and coupon API typing
- Environment: Public coupon center reads through `/coupons/public`
- Steps: Compare frontend public coupon types/helpers with backend `CouponPublicResponse`
- Expected: Public coupon frontend state models only public response fields and does not depend on admin-only coupon metadata
- Actual: `couponApi.getPublic()` and Coupon Center helpers used full `Coupon` typing, including `scope`, `status`, `totalQuantity`, and `claimedQuantity`
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `CouponPublic`, typed public coupon API/cache/page/helpers with it, and changed public fallback/test data to use `remainingQuantity` while preserving full `Coupon` for admin management.

### F130: Frontend exposes unimplemented membership API routes

- Area: Frontend / API contract; Backend / route parity
- Environment: Future membership UI or QA route coverage using `/membership/plans`, `/membership/me`, or `/membership/orders`
- Steps: Compare frontend `membershipApi` exports with backend route mappings and schema
- Expected: Exported commercial feature APIs have matching implemented backend routes, or the unfinished feature remains hidden
- Actual: The frontend exported `membershipApi` and membership types, but no backend membership controller/service/schema existed
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Removed the unused frontend membership API export and orphaned membership types so the app no longer exposes guaranteed-404 membership routes.

### F129: Pet gallery quota response exposes account and IP upload counters

- Area: Backend / Pet Gallery; Frontend / quota API contract
- Environment: Signed-in Home and Pet Gallery upload readiness checks through `GET /pet-gallery/quota`
- Steps: Load the pet-gallery upload UI as a signed-in customer and inspect the quota JSON
- Expected: Quota response includes only upload limit, remaining uploads, and whether upload is allowed
- Actual: Quota response included `userUploads` and `ipUploads`, exposing internal account/IP anti-abuse counters that the storefront did not need
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Removed account/IP upload counters from `PetGalleryQuota` and frontend quota typing while keeping the backend account/IP remaining-quota calculation server-side.

### F128: Customer review submit response exposes raw review entity

- Area: Backend / Reviews; Frontend / review API contract
- Environment: Signed-in product detail review submission
- Steps: Submit a product review through `POST /reviews/product/{productId}` and inspect the response JSON
- Expected: Submit response uses the same narrowed storefront review DTO as public reads
- Actual: Submit response returned raw `Review`, including internal user/order/moderation/entity-derived fields not needed by the storefront
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Mapped saved reviews through `PublicReviewResponse.from(...)` in the customer submit endpoint and typed `reviewApi.create` as `PublicReview`. Admin review queue, reply, and status endpoints keep full `Review` for moderation.

### F127: Logistics tracking response exposes raw provider payload

- Area: Backend / Logistics; Frontend / tracking widget contract
- Environment: Customer, guest, and admin logistics tracking through `/logistics/track`
- Steps: Query logistics tracking with a configured provider and inspect the response payload/UI states
- Expected: Tracking responses include only normalized display fields and events, without third-party raw provider bodies
- Actual: `LogisticsTrackResponse` included `rawResponse`, and frontend types/copy still modeled raw provider data
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Removed `rawResponse` from backend DTO/service population, narrowed frontend logistics response typing, and removed stale raw-provider locale/test mock copy. Empty tracking states now depend on normalized events/status/summary only.

### F126: Customer product-question submit response exposes raw question entity

- Area: Backend / Product questions; Frontend / question API contract
- Environment: Signed-in product detail Q&A submission
- Steps: Submit a question through `POST /product-questions/product/{productId}` and inspect the response JSON
- Expected: Submit response uses the same narrowed storefront question DTO as public reads
- Actual: Submit response returned raw `ProductQuestion`, including customer/moderation/product entity-derived fields not needed by the storefront
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Mapped ask responses through `ProductQuestionPublicResponse`; admin queue and answer endpoints keep full `ProductQuestion` for moderation.

### F125: Customer and guest order APIs expose raw order entities

- Area: Backend / Orders and order tracking; Frontend / order API contract
- Environment: Customer profile order center, guest checkout/order tracking, support order context, and order item reads
- Steps: Load `/orders/me`, `/orders/{id}`, `/orders/guest/{id}`, order item routes, checkout responses, or `/orders/track` and inspect returned JSON
- Expected: Storefront order responses include only customer-facing order summary, payment, shipping, tracking, return, and item display fields
- Actual: Raw `Order` and `OrderItem` entities exposed internal owner `userId`, coupon linkage ids, item `orderId`, guest email embedded in shippingAddress, and update metadata to storefront callers
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `OrderCustomerResponse` and `OrderItemCustomerResponse`, mapped customer/guest order reads and checkout results through them, and made public tracking return the same narrowed DTOs via `OrderTrackResponse`. Admin order APIs and admin-visible compatibility paths keep full entity data for operations.

### F124: Public category APIs expose raw category entity and missing by-id route

- Area: Backend / Categories; Frontend / category API contract
- Environment: Storefront category navigation, homepage discovery, product filters, and future category detail reads
- Steps: Inspect `/categories` or call frontend `categoryApi.getById(id)`
- Expected: Public category reads return a stable storefront DTO and `/categories/{id}` is available with the same safe contract
- Actual: Public category list returned raw `Category` entities, and the frontend-declared by-id route was missing in the backend
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `CategoryPublicResponse`, mapped public list/top-level/children/by-id category reads through it, enriched by-id with public productCount, and typed frontend category reads as `CategoryPublic`. Admin category create/update/delete still use the existing `Category` payload contract.

### F123: Customer support responses expose account and assignment identifiers

- Area: Backend / Support REST and WebSocket; Frontend / support types
- Environment: Customer support widget, guest order support, and admin support queue
- Steps: Load customer/guest support session/messages or inspect customer WebSocket support payloads
- Expected: Customer support payloads include only conversation display fields and no account/admin assignment identifiers
- Actual: Raw `SupportSession` and `SupportMessage` payloads exposed `userId`, `assignedAdminId`, `contextKey`, `senderId`, and admin read-state fields to customer/guest support clients
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added customer support session/message DTOs, mapped customer and guest REST responses through them, and split WebSocket broadcast payloads so customer sockets receive narrowed DTOs while admin sockets keep the full queue-management contract. Customer `CONNECTED` WebSocket messages no longer include `userId`, and frontend support id fields are optional for compatibility.

### F122: Public product APIs expose raw product entity fields

- Area: Backend / ProductController, SearchController, PageController; Frontend / product API contract
- Environment: Storefront product list/detail/search/home aliases, recommendations, finder candidates, add-on candidates, by-ids, featured, and personalized recommendations
- Steps: Inspect public product JSON from `/products`, `/products/{id}`, `/products/featured`, `/products/by-ids`, `/products/{id}/recommendations`, `/products/add-on-candidates`, `/products/finder-candidates`, `/products/personalized-recommendations`, `/search`, or `/home/products`
- Expected: Public product responses expose only storefront display and purchase fields, while backend status and audit timestamps remain admin-only
- Actual: Public product endpoints returned raw `Product` entities, exposing fields such as `status`, `createdAt`, and `updatedAt`
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `ProductPublicResponse` and mapped all public product reads, search, and home-product aliases through it. The DTO preserves storefront display, option-selection, bundle, promotion, review, and purchase fields while omitting raw entity status/timestamps; admin product management keeps full `Product`.

### F121: Customer notification APIs expose owner userId

- Area: Backend / Notifications; Frontend / notification types
- Environment: Signed-in storefront notification center
- Steps: Load `/notifications/me` as a customer and inspect the response payload
- Expected: Customer responses include only notification display/read fields and do not include owner `userId`
- Actual: Raw `Notification` entities were returned, so `userId` appeared in notification JSON
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `NotificationResponse`, mapped notification list responses through it, and made frontend `AppNotification.userId` optional for compatibility. Read/unread-count/delete paths continue to enforce ownership through server-side entity lookups.

### F120: Cart and wishlist customer APIs expose owner userId

- Area: Backend / Cart, wishlist and favorites aliases; Frontend / cart and wishlist types
- Environment: Signed-in storefront cart and wishlist pages
- Steps: Load `/cart/me`, `/wishlist/me`, or `/favorites` as a customer and inspect the response payload
- Expected: Customer responses include only cart/wishlist item display fields and do not include owner `userId`
- Actual: Raw `CartItem` and `Wishlist` entities were returned, so `userId` appeared in customer cart/wishlist JSON
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `CartItemResponse` and `WishlistItemResponse`, mapped cart list and wishlist/favorites list responses through those DTOs, and made frontend `CartItem.userId` / `WishlistItem.userId` optional for compatibility. Mutations continue to enforce ownership using server-side entity lookups.

### F119: Public brand API exposes admin metadata and admin brand list route is missing

- Area: Backend / BrandController, AdminController; Frontend / brandApi, BrandManagement, ProductManagement
- Environment: Public `/brands` and admin brand management `/admin/brands`
- Steps: Inspect public brand JSON and open Brand Management after it calls `adminApi.getBrands()`
- Expected: Public brand reads contain only active storefront display fields, while admin brand management has an authenticated full-list route
- Actual: Public `/brands` returned raw `Brand` rows with admin metadata, and `/admin/brands` was not defined even though the frontend admin page requested it
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `BrandPublicResponse` for public `/brands`, restricted public reads to active display fields, added `/admin/brands` to `AdminController` for full brand inventory under existing admin brand permission, and updated frontend public brand typing to `BrandPublic`.

### F118: Customer address APIs expose userId in customer responses

- Area: Backend / User addresses; Frontend / address types
- Environment: Signed-in customer address book, default-address lookup, address create/update responses, and legacy `/user/addresses` alias
- Steps: Load `/addresses/me`, `/addresses/{id}`, `/addresses/me/default`, or save an address as a customer and inspect the JSON response
- Expected: Customer address responses expose only address display/editing fields and do not include the owner user id
- Actual: Raw `UserAddress` entities were returned, so `userId` appeared in customer address responses
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `UserAddressResponse` and mapped customer address endpoints plus `/user/addresses` through it. Create/update responses now re-read the persisted address before mapping, preserving generated ids/timestamps/default state while hiding `userId`. Frontend `UserAddress.userId` is optional for compatibility with any remaining admin or legacy data shapes.

### F117: Public announcement API exposes admin scheduling and status fields

- Area: Backend / Site announcements; Frontend / Navbar announcement banner
- Environment: Public `GET /announcements/active` storefront banner data
- Steps: Inspect the public active-announcement response for fields beyond banner display data
- Expected: Public responses contain only id, title, content, and a sanitized link URL
- Actual: Raw `SiteAnnouncement` entities exposed admin fields such as status, sortOrder, startsAt, endsAt, createdAt, and updatedAt
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `SiteAnnouncementPublicResponse`, mapped active announcement reads through it after safe-link filtering, and updated frontend public announcement typing. Admin announcement APIs still use the full announcement entity for scheduling and management.

### F116: Frontend user-scoped caches can survive account switch

- Area: Frontend / API cache layer and auth session helpers
- Environment: Same browser switching between accounts without a full reload
- Steps: Load account-scoped data such as addresses, available coupons, notifications, gallery state, or order items as one user, then sign in as another user before cache TTL expiry
- Expected: Account-scoped cache entries are scoped to the current user and cleared when the account or role changes
- Actual: Several frontend caches used fixed keys such as `list`, `default`, `mine`, `available`, or `auth`, and some login/logout paths bypassed shared auth cleanup
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added current-user cache scoping for affected frontend caches, centralized session persistence/cleanup through `persistAuthSession` and `clearStoredAuthSession`, and wired Login, Navbar, AdminLayout, useAuth, and Checkout auth-expiry cleanup through those helpers.

### F115: Public brand API wrapper ignores active-only filtering

- Area: Frontend / brandApi
- Environment: Frontend calls to `brandApi.getAll({ activeOnly: true })`
- Steps: Inactivate a brand, then inspect the `/brands` request from active-only callers
- Expected: Active-only calls pass `activeOnly=true` and do not show inactive brands
- Actual: The API wrapper ignored the option and requested `/brands` without params, returning all brands
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Public `brandApi.getAll()` now defaults to active-only reads, explicit `activeOnly:false` uses a separate all-brand cache, and BrandManagement now loads full inventory through `adminApi.getBrands()`.

### F114: Checkout discount coupon preview uses the wrong percent semantics

- Area: Frontend / Checkout
- Environment: Signed-in checkout with percentage coupons
- Steps: Compare frontend estimated savings for a `DISCOUNT` coupon with backend coupon calculation
- Expected: Frontend and backend both treat `discountPercent` as payable percent, so 80 means 20% off
- Actual: Checkout estimated percentage coupons as `subtotal * percent / 100`, making 80 look like 80% off instead of 20% off
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Checkout coupon estimation and next-unlock hints now calculate percentage discounts as `subtotal * (100 - payablePercent) / 100`, matching backend `CouponService`.

### F113: Customer coupon APIs expose raw coupon and user-coupon entities

- Area: Backend / CouponController, CouponService, CouponQuoteResponse; Frontend / coupon types
- Environment: Coupon center, wallet, claim, available-coupon, and checkout quote APIs
- Steps: Inspect public coupon and signed-in coupon JSON responses
- Expected: Storefront/customer APIs expose only display and usage fields required by the UI
- Actual: Public APIs returned raw `Coupon`; customer APIs returned raw `UserCoupon`, including operational and ownership fields such as `userId`, `orderId`, `couponScope`, and backend coupon status
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `CouponPublicResponse` and `UserCouponResponse`. Public coupons expose safe campaign data plus `remainingQuantity`; customer coupon and quote responses expose only the user's coupon id, coupon id, status/timestamps, display rule, validity window, and description.

### F112: Customer profile API returns the full user entity

- Area: Backend / UserController; Frontend / userApi
- Environment: Signed-in `/users/profile` calls used across storefront profile/auth and admin permission checks
- Steps: Call `/users/profile` while authenticated and inspect the response fields
- Expected: Self-profile response exposes only identity/contact and authorization fields needed by the frontend
- Actual: The endpoint returned raw `User`, exposing unrelated entity fields such as address/status/timestamps and making future entity fields part of the customer contract by default
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `UserProfileResponse` and changed `/users/profile` to return the safe DTO. Frontend `UserProfile` and `userApi.getProfile` now match that narrowed contract.

### F111: Public pet gallery responses expose upload owner and file metadata

- Area: Backend / Pet Gallery; Frontend / PetGallery, API types
- Environment: Public `/pet-gallery` list, upload, and like responses
- Steps: Inspect the public gallery JSON response for uploaded or seeded photos
- Expected: Public gallery data contains only storefront display fields and viewer flags, not uploader account IDs, client IPs, file metadata, or internal moderation/source state
- Actual: Raw `PetGalleryPhoto` entities were returned publicly, exposing fields such as `userId`, `ipAddress`, `originalFilename`, `contentType`, `fileSize`, `status`, and `source`. Logged-in users could also reuse the anonymous gallery cache, causing stale `canDelete` and viewer like state.
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Public gallery endpoints now return `PetGalleryPhotoPublicResponse` with id, masked/curated username, imageUrl, likeCount, likedByMe, canDelete, and createdAt only. Frontend public gallery types were narrowed, and gallery cache keys are separated for anonymous and signed-in reads.

### F110: Customer and guest payment APIs expose gateway/internal payment references

- Area: Backend / PaymentController; Frontend / paymentApi, OrderManagement
- Environment: Customer payment creation, recovery, sync, guest payment lookup, callback response, and admin payment history
- Steps: Create or recover a payment as a customer/guest and inspect payment endpoint responses
- Expected: Customer/guest responses include only checkout-safe payment data; gateway provider references, refund references, and callback metadata remain admin-only, and failed guest payment probes feed blacklist counters
- Actual: Shared `Payment` entities were returned to storefront and callback callers, exposing fields such as `providerReference`, `refundReference`, and `callbackAt`
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `PaymentResponse` for non-admin payment responses. Customer and guest payment create/sync/history/latest endpoints now return safe DTOs, callbacks return `{received:true}`, failed guest payment lookups record payment blacklist failures, and admin order management uses explicit admin payment helpers with `AdminPayment` for full operational fields.

### F109: Reviewable-order lookup exposes more order data than the product review form needs

- Area: Backend / Product reviews; Frontend / ProductDetail, ProductReview
- Environment: Signed-in product detail review composer and `GET /reviews/product/{productId}/reviewable-orders`
- Steps: Open a product page as a signed-in customer with completed orders and inspect the reviewable order selector response
- Expected: The helper endpoint returns only the minimum fields required to choose the order being reviewed, remains explicitly authenticated, and keeps review helper lists bounded
- Actual: The endpoint returned full `Order` objects even though the UI only needed order id/order number/date labels, and the public review list had no configured row cap. Signed-in review fetches could also reuse the anonymous review cache and miss owner-only pending reviews.
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Added `ReviewableOrderResponse` with only id, orderNo, createdAt, and completedAt; added an explicit authenticated security matcher for `/reviews/product/*/reviewable-orders`; capped public review rows with `review.public-max-rows` and reviewable-order rows with `review.reviewable-order-max-rows`; and made signed-in review loads bypass the anonymous review cache.

### F108: Public product reviews expose internal user/order/moderation fields

- Area: Backend / Product reviews; Frontend / ProductDetail, ProductReview
- Environment: Public product review section and review alias endpoints
- Steps: Inspect `GET /reviews/product/{productId}` or `GET /products/{productId}/reviews` for a product with reviews
- Expected: Public review responses expose only display-safe review content and masked customer identity; internal user/order/moderation fields remain admin-only
- Actual: Public review endpoints returned raw `Review` entities, including fields such as `userId`, `orderId`, `status`, and product payloads that are not needed on the storefront
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Public review reads now return `PublicReviewResponse` with only id, productId, rating, comment, masked username, merchant reply, timestamps, and `editableByCurrentUser`. Frontend storefront review types now consume `PublicReview`; admin review pages still use the full `Review` contract.

### F107: Public product questions expose unanswered content and customer identifiers

- Area: Backend / Product questions; Frontend / ProductDetail
- Environment: Public product detail Q&A section
- Steps: Ask a product question and view the product page before an admin answers it
- Expected: Public product page shows only answered/merchant-reviewed Q&A and no customer identifiers; answering is handled in admin
- Actual: Public endpoint returned raw question entities, including unanswered questions and `userId`/`username`, and the product page rendered an answer form
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Public product question reads now use `ProductQuestionPublicResponse` and only return answered rows, capped by `product-question.public-max-rows`. The product page only displays public question text, date and merchant answer. The customer-side answer API was removed from `questionApi`, tests now use `adminApi.answerQuestion`, and the obsolete `/product-questions/{id}/answer` backend route plus permission/interceptor mappings were removed.

### F106: Public order tracking exposes registered-account order details through order number and email

- Area: Backend / OrderService, OrderTrackResponse; Frontend / OrderTracking.tsx
- Environment: Public `GET /orders/track` for registered-account orders
- Steps: Use a registered customer's order number and checkout email in public order tracking
- Expected: Public lookup reveals only low-sensitivity status; full registered-account order details require sign-in
- Actual: Public tracking returned the complete order object and order items, including address, payment, shipment, return and item details
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Registered-account public tracking responses now set `detailsRestricted=true` and include only a minimal order status summary with no item rows or sensitive order fields. The frontend renders a login prompt and status-only summary, while true guest orders keep the existing full public tracking flow.

### F105: Sensitive public credential endpoints do not consistently feed IP blacklist operations

- Area: Backend / IP blacklist, auth, guest order, guest support
- Environment: Public forgot-password, admin bootstrap, guest order and guest support endpoints
- Steps: Submit repeated wrong credentials or mismatched guest session/order values
- Expected: Repeated failures are visible in IP blacklist management and can trigger/release blocks
- Actual: Several public credential-style endpoints were outside the default protected path list or did not record failures into the blacklist service
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Default `security.ip-blacklist.path-prefixes` and the config-center template now include `/auth/forgot-password`, `/auth/register`, `/auth/refresh`, `/users/create-admin`, `/orders/track`, `/orders/guest`, and `/support/guest` in addition to login/payment/guest-checkout paths. Controllers now record specific LOGIN-source failure reasons for forgot-password, admin bootstrap, guest order credential/action, and guest support order/session failures.

### F104: Guest checkout email can be claimed as a registered account without verifying mailbox ownership

- Area: Backend / AuthController, UserService; Frontend / Register.tsx
- Environment: `POST /auth/register` after a guest checkout created a `GUEST` user for the same email
- Steps: Know a guest checkout email and register a new account with that email
- Expected: Converting a `GUEST` account into a registered account requires proving mailbox ownership first
- Actual: Registration could upgrade the reused guest email account without an email-code challenge
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Guest-email claims now require a valid 6-digit code verified by `EmailLoginService` before `UserService.register(..., guestEmailVerified=true)` can upgrade the account. The frontend reacts to `emailCodeRequired`, shows the code input and send-code action, and submits the code with registration. Register email-code failures are recorded with a register-specific reason for blacklist/audit clarity.

### F103: Anonymous pet-gallery likes are not database-idempotent under concurrency

- Area: Backend / PetGalleryService, PetGalleryPhotoLike; Schema / pet_gallery_photo_likes
- Environment: Public `POST /pet-gallery/{id}/like`
- Steps: Send concurrent anonymous like requests for the same photo from the same client IP
- Expected: One viewer can increase a photo's like count only once
- Actual: Anonymous de-duplication used a service-level pre-check by IP, but the database unique key only covered `(photo_id, user_id)`, allowing concurrent null-user inserts
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Pet-gallery likes now store a required `viewerKey` and enforce unique `(photo_id, viewer_key)`. Startup schema hardening backfills old rows, removes duplicate historical rows, recalibrates historical `like_count` from de-duplicated like rows, and adds the new index/constraint.

### F102: Guest support sessions can mix messages across orders for the same guest email

- Area: Backend / SupportController, SupportService, SupportSessionMapper; Schema / support_sessions
- Environment: Multiple guest orders created with the same guest email
- Steps: Open support on one guest order, then use another guest order for the same email to load/send guest support messages
- Expected: Guest support access is scoped to the verified order context
- Actual: Support sessions were keyed only by reused guest `user_id`, so support history could be shared across orders for the same guest email
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Guest support sessions now use `contextKey=guest-order:{orderNo}` and access checks require both the guest user and order context to match. Existing signed-in support remains unchanged with null/blank context, and normal support lists, unread counts, REST access, and WebSocket access are restricted to that null/blank context so guest-order sessions cannot be reached through ordinary user support APIs.

### F93: WebSocket URL missing token parameter (test mismatch)

- Area: Frontend / api/index.ts
- Environment: Frontend test (api/index.test.ts)
- Steps: Run `supportWebSocketUrl('token')` and check URL
- Expected: URL contains `?token=token` query parameter
- Actual: URL does not contain token (token moved to subprotocol per B16 fix)
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Updated frontend tests to assert token-free WebSocket URLs plus `support.v1` / `auth.<base64url-token>` protocols. Customer and admin support WebSocket clients now pass those protocols, and the backend support handler reads JWTs from `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` while preserving legacy query-token compatibility.

### F94: Auth storage not cleared on 401 error

- Area: Frontend / api/index.ts
- Environment: Frontend test (api/index.test.ts)
- Steps: Trigger 401 error without refresh token, check localStorage
- Expected: `localStorage.getItem('token')` returns null
- Actual: Returns "7" (storage not cleared)
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Expanded shared auth-session cleanup to remove `userId`, `username`, `role`, and `adminDefaultPath` along with token data.

### F95: IP blacklist empty list prevents management

- Area: Frontend / IpBlacklistManagement.tsx
- Environment: Admin IP blacklist center
- Steps: Open IP blacklist management when there are no blocked rows
- Expected: Admin can manually block an IP, clear filters, and understand the empty state
- Actual: The default blocked-only empty list made the page feel unmanageable
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: Defaulted the page to all records, added an operational notice, localized status/source filters, added reset filters, and added empty-state actions for manual block and showing all records. Follow-up root-cause fixes: backend source filtering no longer treats empty/ALL as MANUAL, invalid/empty status filters no longer produce empty queries, automatic LOGIN/PAYMENT entries show in the list, status includes total/released counts, refresh/search/Enter handlers no longer pass React events as query overrides, dropdown changes reload immediately, manual block/reset reload the full list, old `ip_blacklist_entries` tables are hardened on startup, row mapping tolerates missing non-core columns during rollout, and frontend list rendering no longer depends on the status-summary request succeeding.

### F96: Admin review management can render empty because review API contract is inconsistent

- Area: Backend / AdminController reviews, Frontend / ReviewManagement.tsx
- Environment: Admin review management
- Steps: Open review management after frontend expects `AdminReviewPage.items` while backend returns raw `List<Review>`
- Expected: Review table loads real rows and supports reliable status/search pagination
- Actual: The page could show an empty table despite review data, and local-only filters could miss records outside the loaded page
- Status: Fixed
- Resolution: `/admin/reviews` now returns a paginated page object with totals and status summary, backend supports status/search filters, frontend uses service-side pagination, and API normalization still accepts legacy raw-array responses.

F93, F94, F95, and F96 fixed in code. See QA_ISSUES.md for full history.

- Frontend: 92+ pages, 15+ components, 4 hooks, 43 test files reviewed
- Security: CORS, CSRF, JWT, rate limiting, IP blacklist, SSRF protection verified
- Performance: Database queries, caching, pagination, batch operations reviewed
- i18n: EN/ZH/ES locales complete with 2776 keys each

- 2026-06-02 13:35 UTC: **Manual agent regression and release pass (no screenshots).** Followed the user constraint that no screenshots are taken during testing. Full frontend Jest passed: 42/42 suites, 217/217 tests. Full backend Maven tests passed after final changes: 378 tests, 0 failures. - 2026-06-02 13:35 UTC: **UI/accessibility issues fixed.** Navbar and product-list search submit controls now use real Ant Design `Button` elements with accessible names. Product-list pagination previous/next controls now receive localized accessible names. Product-detail breadcrumb home control and mobile thumbnail buttons now have accessible labels/titles. Mobile product-detail carousel arrows now have at least 44px touch targets.
- 2026-06-02 13:35 UTC: **Upload media path regression fixed.** `resolveApiAssetUrl` no longer rewrites `/uploads/...` and `uploads/...` into `/api/uploads/...`; upload assets remain same-origin `/uploads/...`. Added/update tests for that behavior.
- 2026-06-02 13:35 UTC: **Pet gallery upload-image 404 fixed.** Root cause was Nginx location precedence: the generic static image extension rule intercepted `/uploads/...webp` before the uploads proxy. Updated live Nginx and deploy templates to use `location ^~ /uploads/`, then reloaded Nginx. Source-origin and public cache-busted upload URLs returned 200 image/webp. Public pet-gallery API now appends a stable `?v=...` parameter to local upload image URLs to bypass stale Cloudflare 404 cache entries. Added DTO and service coverage for local upload visibility/cache behavior.
- 2026-06-02 13:35 UTC: **No-screenshot E2E/smoke regression passed.** Playwright DOM/network smoke covered desktop 1366x900 and mobile 390x844 across `/`, `/products`, product detail, `/pet-gallery`, `/cart`, `/checkout`, `/coupons`, `/login`, `/register`, `/profile`, `/wishlist`, and `/track-order`. Final result: passed with zero issues. Navigation-time `ERR_ABORTED` cancellations were treated as noise; real 4xx/5xx and rendered broken image checks passed.
- 2026-06-02 13:35 UTC: **APK generated.** Ran `SHOPTEST_MOBILE_ALLOW_DEBUG_APK_PUBLISH=true npm run build:apk` in `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile`. ### F749: Native App update UI could surface Android APK flow on iOS and missed resume/network retries

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Native App update UX
- Severity: High
- Evidence: App UI test engineer review at 2026-06-03 found `NativeMobileUpdateGate` checked only `isNativeMobileApp()` while the release manifest is Android APK-specific, and Navbar initialized Android download state without platform filtering. The same review found no retry hook for online, foreground visibility, Capacitor `resume`, or `appStateChange` after an initial failed update fetch.
- Expected: iOS WebView shells must not show Android APK update/download UI, and Android mandatory update checks should retry after network recovery or App foregrounding.
- Actual: iOS could consume Android release metadata, and Android Apps that started offline or resumed after a mandatory release could miss the update prompt until a full reload.
- Resolution: Added platform helpers in `frontend/src/utils/mobileUpdate.ts`, gated `NativeMobileUpdateGate` and `Navbar` so Android APK update/download UX runs only in browser or Android native contexts, and added online/visibility/Capacitor resume/appStateChange retry checks for Android native update fetches. Added `mobileUpdate.test.ts` coverage proving iOS native is recognized as native but not as the Android APK update target.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:54 UTC `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/utils/mobileUpdate.test.ts` passed 1 suite / 5 tests. Playwright iOS WebView simulation with a mandatory signed Android manifest observed `manifestRequests=0`, `modalCount=0`, and `androidDownloadLinks=0`.

### F750: Mobile Web/App purchase-path text was clipped in catalog, product detail, cart, and registration

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Mobile Web and Native App UI
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Playwright mobile layout sweep at 390px found product-list badges clipped to a 24px row (`Sale New Best seller`), product-detail buybar labels (`Favorite`, `Add to cart`) ellipsized, cart amount helper copy (`Add $49.45 more for free shipping`, saved-item value copy, free-shipping progress label) truncated by `nowrap + hidden`, and the register privacy hint forced to one line.
- Expected: Mobile commerce labels should remain readable without losing 44px touch targets or introducing horizontal overflow.
- Actual: Several customer-facing purchase-path labels were truncated on mobile Web and App-sized WebView screens.
- Resolution: Relaxed final mobile badge height/overflow rules in `ProductList.css` and `mobile-app.css`, allowed product-detail buybar labels to wrap without ellipsis in `ProductDetail.css` and App overrides, restored mobile cart amount phrases to wrap while keeping money atomic in `Cart.css`, and removed the one-line clamp from the mobile register hint in `Register.css`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:54 UTC Playwright mobile Web and Android WebView simulations at 390x844 passed on `/products`, `/products/1`, `/cart`, `/register`, and `/pet-finder` with no real clipped customer-facing text; the only remaining clipped signal was the hidden 1px accessible text for the `Filters` icon button, treated as a non-issue. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F751: App WebView admin shell and Config Center sticky controls did not account for safe areas

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Native App Admin UI
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: App UI test engineer review at 2026-06-03 found `AdminLayout.css` used sticky `top: 0`/`height: 100vh` for the admin sider/header and `ConfigCenter.css` used mobile sticky `top: 0` for high-risk Apply/Publish controls, with no `env(safe-area-inset-*)` compensation.
- Expected: Native App admin header, side navigation, content bottom, and Config Center sticky operation area should avoid iOS status bars/notches and bottom gesture areas.
- Actual: On iOS/gesture WebView screens, admin chrome and Config Center actions could sit under the system status area or behind the admin header.
- Resolution: Added shared admin safe-area variables to `AdminLayout.css`, offset App admin sider/header/loading/content by top/bottom safe-area insets, and changed Config Center mobile sticky top to `safe-area + admin header height + gap`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 18:54 UTC source review confirmed `body.shop-mobile-app .admin-layout` now defines safe-area variables and `ConfigCenter.css` uses `calc(var(--shop-admin-safe-top) + var(--shop-admin-header-height) + 8px)`. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.

### F752: Mobile Web support and admin alert controls regressed on small screens

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Mobile Web UI
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Mobile UI retest after the dedicated tester pass found the customer support close control still measured below the intended touch size on narrow Web screens, `/admin/support` mobile pagination controls remained below 44px, and the `/admin/alerts` F744 table scroll guard could stretch the page chrome horizontally on 360-430px widths.
- Expected: Support close, admin support pagination, and admin alerts table overflow should be usable on mobile without page-level horizontal scroll.
- Actual: Touch targets and table overflow containment still regressed after earlier UI fixes.
- Resolution: Added the final 46px mobile support close guard in `CustomerSupportWidget.css`, raised `/admin/support` pagination hit targets in `SupportManagement.css`, and isolated `/admin/alerts` table `scroll.x=980` to the table wrapper in `AlertManagement.css`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 19:19 UTC Playwright mobile checks passed: support close button measured about `45.43 x 45.43`, `/admin/support` pagination items measured `44 x 44`, and `/admin/alerts` at 360/390/430px kept `documentElement.scrollWidth` equal to the viewport while table wrapper scroll width remained `980`.

### F753: Desktop admin reviews and dashboard tracking controls clipped content

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Desktop Admin UI
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Desktop Web test engineer review found `/admin/reviews` content and reply columns were too narrow for review moderation text, and the admin dashboard 17TRACK button could clip/ellipsis its action label.
- Expected: Review moderation columns and dashboard tracking actions should remain readable on desktop admin screens.
- Actual: Review text and the tracking action button were truncated in normal desktop layouts.
- Resolution: Increased review content/reply column widths to `360/300` and table `scroll.x` to `1730` in `ReviewManagement.tsx`, and added a `96px` minimum tracking button width in `SeventeenTrackWidget.css`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 19:19 UTC Playwright desktop checks passed: `/admin/reviews` rendered the content column at `360px` and reply column at `300px`; `/admin/dashboard` rendered the tracking button at `96px` with text not clipped.

### F754: App WebView shell assets were stale and Pet Finder product names were truncated

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Native App WebView UI
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: App tester regression initially found mobile WebView assets were stale after frontend fixes, and `/pet-finder` product names still used Ant Design single-line ellipsis so long names could truncate or overflow in App/mobile WebView layouts.
- Expected: App WebView assets should match the current frontend build, and Pet Finder recommendation product names should wrap naturally.
- Actual: WebView shell resources lagged behind the fixed frontend bundle, and product names were still single-line ellipsized.
- Resolution: Replaced the Ant Design `ellipsis` product-name rendering with `.pet-finder-page__productName` natural wrapping in `PetFinder.tsx`/`PetFinder.css`, then ran `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile npm run sync` to copy current assets into Web, Android, and iOS shells.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 19:19 UTC mobile WebView checks passed: `main.d2d08784.js` was present across synced shell assets at that point; `/pet-finder` sampled product names including long unbroken names with `clientWidth=scrollWidth=150`, no horizontal overflow, no line clamp, and no `ant-typography-ellipsis-single-line` class. App retest also reconfirmed iOS no Android update modal, `/register` hint unclamped, and admin safe-area computed offsets applied.

### F755: Web App download entry disappeared when no release-signed APK is published

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Web and Native App release UX
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: The user reported the Web APP download entry and App auto-upgrade function were gone. Web/App tester agents confirmed the download entry was hidden because `Navbar` required `androidApkUrl`, while the current unsigned manifest intentionally has `releaseSigned:false` and empty `apkUrl`. Android App update checks still ran, but no modal appeared because the current manifest is not newer and lacks installable release metadata.
- Expected: Browser Web and mobile Web should keep a visible Android App entry even when no release-signed package is currently published, while actual APK downloads and App auto-update modals must remain gated to release-signed metadata.
- Actual: Browser Web/mobile Web hid the App entry entirely under an unsigned/empty manifest, making the feature look removed.
- Resolution: Changed `Navbar.tsx` so browser Web/mobile Web always render the Android App entry; when `resolveMobileReleaseDownloadUrl(...)` is empty, it renders a button/menu item that shows localized "package not published yet" feedback and does not create an APK `href`. Added `nav.mobileAppUnavailable` locale strings and a `mobileUpdate.test.ts` case proving Android native update checks resolve the configured manifest through the API origin.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 19:41 UTC full frontend Jest passed 47 suites / 235 tests; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed; `npm run build` passed with `main.2b66ba95.js`; `/home/guhao/shoptest-mobile npm run sync` passed. Playwright confirmed desktop Web entry is visible as a button with no APK href and shows the unavailable message, mobile Web more-menu entry is visible with no horizontal overflow and shows the same message, Android WebView requests the current unsigned manifest without showing a download modal, and a mocked signed `10024` manifest displays the App update modal and download button. No APK/AAB files were found in public/build/webroot/mobile WebView asset paths, so F334 remains OPEN but not regressed.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 20:15 UTC rebuilt a complete `frontend/build` after detecting an incomplete live sync, then synced it to `/var/www/shoptest`. Live HTTPS now serves `main.2b66ba95.js`. Playwright live smoke confirmed desktop Web renders `Android app` as a `BUTTON` with no href, mobile Web renders `Download Android app` in the More menu with no href, both clicks show the unavailable message, and neither viewport has horizontal overflow. Desktop Web and mobile Web tester agents also reported PASS with no APK request/download events.

### F756: App WebView update manifest lacked CORS headers

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Deployment / Nginx, Native App update UX
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: App update logic still existed and App shell runtime config resolved native update checks to `https://pet.686888666.xyz/downloads/mobile-version.json`, but the live manifest response did not include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`. A Capacitor WebView origin such as `capacitor://localhost` could therefore be blocked before the update gate could read current release metadata.
- Expected: Android App WebView should be able to fetch the public version manifest with GET/OPTIONS while APK downloads remain gated by release-signed metadata.
- Actual: Live manifest GET returned the JSON body but no CORS allow header, making the automatic update check look absent in real WebView conditions.
- Resolution: Added an exact Nginx rule for `/downloads/mobile-version.json` in `/etc/nginx/sites-available/shoptest` and `deploy/nginx/shoptest-static.conf` with no-store caching, `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`, `Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, OPTIONS`, and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Origin, Accept, Content-Type`, while preserving the existing security headers. Reloaded Nginx after `nginx -t` passed.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 20:15 UTC live `GET` with `Origin: capacitor://localhost` returned `200` plus `access-control-allow-origin: *`; live `OPTIONS` returned `204` plus the same CORS allow headers. Android WebView simulation requested `https://pet.686888666.xyz/downloads/mobile-version.json`; the current unsigned manifest produced no invalid update modal, and a mocked signed `10024` manifest produced the App update modal and download button. The App tester agent independently confirmed GET/OPTIONS CORS pass and no new App update UI regression. No APK/AAB files were found in public/build/live/mobile WebView asset paths, so F334 remains OPEN but not regressed.

### F757: Mobile Web Android App entry and follow-on mobile UI audit gaps were still easy to miss

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Mobile Web UI and App download entry
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: The dedicated mobile Web UI tester found that after restoring the browser Android App entry, the mobile bottom navigation still did not expose a direct App entry in the primary touch area. The same pass found additional small-screen UI defects: the home trust strip could clip at 414px, product discovery categories behaved like a horizontal rail and the select input measured below the desired touch height, coupon quick-nav/priority/filter rails and visible CTAs could shrink below 44px, and the Pet Finder match summary could be clipped or overlapped by the mobile bottom navigation.
- Expected: Mobile Web should have a direct Android App entry in the bottom navigation while unsigned APK downloads remain blocked, and core mobile commerce surfaces should avoid clipped text, hidden horizontal rails, and under-sized touch targets.
- Actual: Users could miss the App entry on mobile Web, and several high-traffic mobile sections still had clipping, overflow, or touch-target regressions.
- Resolution: Added a direct mobile browser bottom-nav Android App item in `Navbar.tsx` that renders as a no-href unavailable button when `releaseSigned:false` leaves no safe APK URL; Support remains available from the More menu. Added final mobile guards in `Navbar.css`, `Home.css`, `ProductList.css`, `CouponCenter.css`, and `PetFinder.css` so trust-strip items, product discovery/select controls, coupon quick actions/filters/CTAs, and the Pet Finder match summary fit without horizontal overflow or bottom-nav overlap.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 20:52 UTC Playwright regression passed for the 414px mobile bottom-nav `Android app` entry, no APK links, no page-level horizontal overflow, home trust strip `clientWidth=scrollWidth`, product discovery grid and `44px` select input, coupon quick-nav/priority/filter grids with visible CTAs at `44px`, and Pet Finder match summary with no bottom-nav intersection. The mobile Web tester reran live checks at 375x812, 390x844, and 414x896 and reported PASS: the bottom-nav App entry measured about `54-60px` wide and `46-48px` high, click showed the unavailable message, no `.apk`/debug/unsigned request was made, direct debug/unsigned APK samples returned 404, and the specified mobile UI sections had no clipping or page horizontal overflow.

### F758: Native App login links could be overlapped while the update chain needed signed-release regression coverage

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Native App UI and update UX
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: The App UI tester found the App login bottom links could overlap the fixed bottom navigation at 390x740. The same App-focused pass confirmed the update logic still existed but needed end-to-end regression coverage after the manifest CORS and unsigned-download hardening: current unsigned metadata must not surface a debug/unsigned package, while a future release-signed newer manifest must still show the update modal.
- Expected: App auth-flow links should remain tappable and unobstructed, current unsigned metadata should produce no invalid update prompt, and mocked release-signed newer metadata should still exercise the automatic update modal and download handoff.
- Actual: The login recovery/register links could sit under bottom UI in App-sized viewports, and the restored update path needed explicit UI regression after the Web/App download-entry fixes.
- Resolution: Added App auth-flow spacing guards in `mobile-app.css` so `.shopee-login-root` and `.shopee-login-links` clear the native bottom area. Kept the update chain gated by `releaseSigned:true` plus safe APK metadata, preserving the existing unsigned manifest behavior while verifying the signed-release modal path.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 20:52 UTC Playwright native App regression passed: login links measured `44px` high with no bottom-nav overlap, the current live unsigned manifest generated no invalid update modal or Browser open calls, and a mocked `releaseSigned:true` `10024` manifest displayed the update modal with `Later` and `Download update`. The App tester independently verified live manifest CORS GET `200` and OPTIONS `204` with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`, confirmed current `releaseSigned:false`/empty `apkUrl` shows no update/download UI, confirmed the mocked signed update modal at 390px has no horizontal overflow, and confirmed App login links are not obstructed. No APK/AAB files were found in public/build/live/mobile WebView asset paths, so F334 remains OPEN but not regressed.

### F759: Checkout coupon opportunity action could disappear after payment-channel loading

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Checkout savings UX
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Regression #208 reported `Checkout.test.tsx:223` timing out while looking for the contextual `Review coupons: Coupon within reach` button. Local retest reproduced the failure: the coupon opportunity existed after the first quote, then the checkout cart/address loading effect re-ran when `paymentChannels` changed and a later coupon quote overwrote the opportunity state with the default empty quote.
- Expected: Loading or refreshing payment channels should not reload cart/address data or clear coupon-opportunity guidance; checkout savings actions should remain visible and keep their contextual accessible names.
- Actual: Payment-channel state changes could cause a second checkout data load, which retriggered coupon quote and made the coupon opportunity action disappear, creating both UI instability and the Jest timeout.
- Resolution: Removed payment-channel-driven preferred-method initialization from the cart/address loading effect and narrowed that effect to checkout item selection plus translation dependencies. The existing payment-channel effect remains the single owner for restoring and validating the preferred payment method once channels are loaded.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 20:53 UTC targeted `npm test -- --watchAll=false --runTestsByPath src/pages/Checkout.test.tsx` passed 1 suite / 2 tests; full frontend Jest passed 47 suites / 235 tests; production frontend build compiled successfully with `main.1c0d5ced.js` and no ESLint warnings; live `/var/www/shoptest` and mobile WebView Web/Android/iOS shells all reference `main.1c0d5ced.js`; live manifest CORS GET/OPTIONS passed; no APK/AAB files were found in public/build/live/mobile WebView asset paths. The separate backend permission failure noted in Regression #208 was retested with `./mvnw -Dtest=SupportWebSocketHandlerAdminPayloadTest test` and passed 2 tests, so it was not carried as a current open issue.

### F760: QA Maven runs could not overwrite root-owned generated test artifacts

- Status: FIXED
- Area: QA / Build artifact ownership
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Regression #211 reported the backend Maven gate still failed with `SupportWebSocketHandlerAdminPayloadTest.class` write permission denied. Running the same target test as root passed, but running it as `guhao` initially failed again when Surefire tried to overwrite `target/surefire-reports/com.example.shop.websocket.SupportWebSocketHandlerAdminPayloadTest.txt`. `stat` and `find` showed root-owned generated files under `target/test-classes/com/example/shop/websocket` and `target/surefire-reports`, plus root-owned generated WebView assets after mobile sync.
- Expected: The QA/cron user should be able to recompile tests and overwrite Surefire reports after agent-driven builds, syncs, and targeted Maven runs.
- Actual: Generated artifacts left by root-run tooling blocked subsequent QA Maven runs, causing false backend regressions even when the code-level target test passed.
- Resolution: Restored generated artifact ownership to `guhao:guhao` for the full Maven `target` directory and generated frontend/live/mobile WebView asset directories.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 21:09 UTC `stat` confirmed the affected websocket test output path and mobile WebView public indexes are `guhao:guhao`. Running `su -s /bin/bash guhao -c 'cd /home/guhao/shoptest && ./mvnw -Dtest=SupportWebSocketHandlerAdminPayloadTest test'` passed 2 tests with BUILD SUCCESS. Live static still serves `main.1c0d5ced.js`; manifest CORS remains enabled; no APK/AAB files were found in public/build/live/mobile WebView asset paths. F334 remains OPEN but was not regressed.

### F761: Mobile home quick entries overlapped the bottom navigation

- Evidence: Mobile UI audit at 390x844 and 360x800 found the second row of `.shopee-mobile-quick-panel` buttons intersecting the fixed `.shop-nav__bottomBar`, making Track order, Live support, Pet finder, and History visually and tactically unreliable.

- Actual: The quick-entry panel sat partly under the bottom nav on narrow mobile viewports.

### F767: Native App More dropdown menu items were only 42px tall

- Evidence: App UI audit at 390x844 found top More dropdown items such as Log in, Register, Live support, Compare products, Browsing history, and Stock alerts stabilized at `42px`, because an App-scoped dropdown rule overrode the general 44px target.
- Expected: Native App dropdown menu items should meet the 44px touch-target floor.
- Actual: More menu items were slightly below target and inconsistent with the rest of the App shell controls.

### F768: Payment redirect and gateway URL policy still allowed public HTTP hops

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Backend payment URL safety
- Severity: High
- Evidence: Security and UI-flow review found the shared frontend `safeUrl` helper still treated `http://...` as a safe external payment/navigation URL, and backend `GatewayUrlValidator` could accept public HTTP payment gateway URLs when local gateways were enabled for development.
- Expected: Customer payment redirects and production gateway URLs should require HTTPS; plaintext HTTP should be reserved only for explicitly local/private development gateways.
- Actual: A public `http://payments.example.com/...` URL could pass the safe-navigation/gateway checks in some paths, weakening checkout redirect integrity.
- Resolution: Changed `frontend/src/utils/safeUrl.ts` to accept HTTPS URLs only, reject credentials/control characters/backslash obfuscation, and keep normalized navigation behavior. Changed `src/main/java/com/example/shop/util/GatewayUrlValidator.java` so public HTTP gateway URLs are rejected even when local gateways are allowed, while explicitly local/private HTTP dev gateways remain possible only with `allowLocal=true`.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 23:18 UTC focused frontend Jest for `src/utils/safeUrl.test.ts` passed, including rejection of public HTTP payment URLs. Focused backend Maven `GatewayUrlValidatorTest` passed, including rejection of public HTTP with both local disabled and enabled, plus allowance for explicit local HTTP dev gateways. Production frontend build passed with `main.c71a17b3.js`.

### F769: Announcement links accepted insecure external HTTP URLs

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Backend announcement management
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Announcement link review found the frontend normalizer and backend service both accepted external `http://...` announcement links. The frontend already sanitized script/credential/control-character forms, but plaintext external campaign links were still permitted.
- Expected: Storefront announcement links should be either same-origin relative paths or HTTPS external URLs without credentials or obfuscation.
- Actual: Admin-created announcements could persist or preview insecure external HTTP links, creating mixed-content and downgrade risk from storefront announcement banners.
- Resolution: Changed `frontend/src/utils/announcementLinks.ts` and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/SiteAnnouncementService.java` to allow relative paths or HTTPS external links only, and updated validation copy accordingly. Added frontend/backend regression coverage for HTTP, protocol-relative, credentialed, script, backslash, and control-character rejection.
- Retest: 2026-06-03 23:18 UTC focused frontend Jest for `src/utils/announcementLinks.test.ts` passed. Focused backend Maven `SiteAnnouncementServiceTest` passed, including the new HTTP-link rejection before persistence. Production frontend build passed.
