### F774: Admin support build-server WebSocket and mobile admin controls regressed

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Admin Support UI and Local Build Test Server
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Desktop/App tester review found the static production build server returned the SPA shell instead of proxying `/ws/support`, causing WebSocket probes to see an unexpected `200`; `/admin/support` mobile/App search button could measure about `31x44`; collapsed/mobile admin brand text could clip; and the storefront More dropdown rows required stable mobile touch targets.

- Actual: The local build server did not proxy WebSocket upgrades, stream resets could crash the server, and several admin/navigation mobile controls were under-sized or clipped.
- Resolution: Added `frontend/scripts/serve-build.js` and `npm run serve:build` to serve `frontend/build` with `/api`, `/uploads`, and `/ws` proxying, including stream error handling. Added final admin support search layout guards in `SupportManagement.css`, collapsed/mobile brand tile guards in `AdminLayout.css`, a shorter bottom account label in Navbar locales/markup, and final dropdown/menu row guards in `Navbar.css`/`mobile-app.css`.

### F777: Mobile/App operation modals and cart recommendation controls were still fragile

- Status: FIXED
- Area: Frontend / Mobile Web and Native App Modal Interaction UI
- Severity: Medium
- Evidence: Mobile Web/App interaction audit found shared operation modals still had small close/form/footer targets in some profile/product/admin flows; profile pet and admin announcement editor modals could exceed the 360x800 App viewport under later CSS overrides; cart drawer personalized recommendation product-name buttons rendered below the 44px touch target; and Native App body-class helpers could read `document.body.classList` before body was available in early WebView/test initialization.
- Expected: Mobile Web and App/WebView operation overlays should stay inside the viewport, keep visible controls at least 44px, avoid hidden micro-targets, and initialize native body classes without page errors.
- Actual: Ant Design modal/input defaults and late App CSS could leave under-sized controls, expose 1x22 `InputNumber` handler targets, and allow App modals to bottom out beyond the viewport.
- Resolution: Added final shared modal control guards in `frontend/src/App.css`, hid safe-modal `InputNumber` handler wraps on mobile, tightened profile-safe modal height budgets in `frontend/src/pages/Profile.css`, added App-specific modal budget overrides in `frontend/src/mobile-app.css`, lowered the admin announcement editor content budget in `frontend/src/pages/AnnouncementManagement.css`, made personalized recommendation product names 44px targets in `frontend/src/components/PetPersonalizedAssistant.css`, and guarded Native App body class reads in `App.tsx`, `Navbar.tsx`, `nativeScroll.ts`, and `mobileContrastGuard.ts`.
- Retest: 2026-06-04 02:48 UTC production-build Playwright checks covered mobile Web 390x844 and App/WebView 360x800 cart drawer, customer support panel, product size guide, profile edit/address/pet modals, admin announcement editor, and logistics carrier editor. Final Native Capacitor App checks measured profile pet and announcement modals at bottom=758/800 with content max-height 736px, zero small controls, zero document overflow, and zero page errors. `npm run build`, `git diff --check`, and release-safety/ownership checks passed; public/build mobile manifests remain unsigned with empty APK URLs.

Legacy prefixed rows remain sanitized in customer responses. | Guest checkout persistence/API response regression with old and new orders. |
| F1547 | CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / ARCHIVED | Checkout totals use `.max(BigDecimal.ZERO)` before shipping and `requirePositiveCheckoutTotal(...)` before persistence. | Coupon/paid/free-shipping checkout regression only. |
| F1548 | CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / ARCHIVED | Current source has no `updateProductSalesAndStock` duplicate path; checkout reserve/restore updates aggregate product stock and selected variant stock symmetrically. | SKU checkout/cancel/refund inventory regression. |
| F1549 | STALE / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED | Current cart quantity update accepts only positive quantities; deletion is explicit through cart delete endpoints. | Standard cart update/delete regression. |
| F1550 | CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / ARCHIVED | Current source has no `processOrderPaid`, `pointService`, or `addPoints` payment-award path. | None until points are reintroduced. |
| F1551 | CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / ARCHIVED | `OrderService.cancelExpiredUnpaidOrders()` is a scheduled fixed-delay cleanup and calls `cancelSingleExpiredOrder(...)` in new transactions. | Pending-payment expiry regression. |
| F1552 | STALE / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED | Current product entity/schema/service has no `storeId`/`store_id` and no hardcoded `1L` product ownership assignment. | None unless multi-store product ownership is introduced. |
| F1554 | CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / ARCHIVED | Cart add/update/delete does not reserve stock; stock is reserved at checkout and restored from order items on cancellation/refund/return paths. | Cart delete plus checkout-stock regression. |
